<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001355 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00028: (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `304001355`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001355/2026x+Refresh1+Cameo+Collaborator+for+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:18.266+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud is a web-based product designed to present models in a simplified form for stakeholders, sponsors, customers, and engineering teams. It allows editing and reviewing models. The product is built with scalability in mind, making it possible to deploy it both in a single node and a cluster setting. It works in all form factors (desktop, tablet, and smart phones), making model sharing and reviewing easy.

Watch the following video to see Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud in action:

800450

Use Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud to:

- Present models to stakeholders, sponsors, or customers.
- Present models to engineering teams (modelers and non-modelers) within your organization.
- Keep stakeholders and engineering teams up-to-date with the precise, consistent information from the single source of truth - model repository.
- Review models and write comments to provide feedback to model authors.
- Address comments by making changes to models without using a modeling tool.
- Edit models on the web without having to use a modeling tool, e.g., for specification of interface libraries or editing requirements

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The conceptual schema of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud.

To learn how to install an use Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, refer to the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud is a web-based product designed to present models in a simplified form for stakeholders, sponsors, customers, and engineering teams. It allows editing and reviewing models. The product is built with scalability in mind, making it possible to deploy it both in a single node and a cluster setting. It works in all form factors (desktop, tablet, and smart phones), making model sharing and reviewing easy.</p><p style="text-align: left;">Watch the following video to see Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud in action:<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41338db9-c745-4d83-a11f-a0eb646f7bcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">800</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YE6GCoJVHeg" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">450</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p class="bodytext" style="text-align: left;">Use Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud to:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><p>Present models to stakeholders, sponsors, or customers.</p></li><li><p>Present models to engineering teams (modelers and non-modelers) within your organization.</p></li><li><p>Keep stakeholders and engineering teams up-to-date with the precise, consistent information from the single source of truth - model repository.</p></li><li><p>Review models and write comments to provide feedback to model authors.</p></li><li><p>Address comments by making changes to models without using a modeling tool.</p></li><li><p>Edit models on the web without having to use a modeling tool, e.g., for specification of interface libraries or editing requirements</p></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="high_level_architecture.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The conceptual schema of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud.</h6><p>To learn how to install an use Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, refer to the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002868 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00030: (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud licensing

- page_id: `304002868`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002868/2026x+Refresh1+Cameo+Collaborator+for+Teamwork+Cloud+licensing
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:51:52.778+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Deployment Guide > (2026x Refresh1) License management
- labels: ['twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### License types

You can purchase the following types of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud licenses, depending on the number of connections you want to use:

| License types | Authenticated commenting | Anonymous commenting | Number of concurrent authenticated connections | Access to content shared for anonymous use* | Predefined portals | Customized portals |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 5-connection license | + | - | 5 | Unlimited | + | + |
| 10-connection license | + | - | 10 | Unlimited | + | + |
| 25-connection license | + | - | 25 | Unlimited | + | + |
| 50-connection license | + | - | 50 | Unlimited | + | + |
| 75-connection license | + | - | 75 | Unlimited | + | + |
| 100-connection license | + | - | 100 | Unlimited | + | + |
| Unlimited license | + | + | Unlimited | Unlimited | + | + |

***** Unauthenticated users can open Cameo Collaborator documents via [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sharing document links' space=''].

##### Applying licenses to new installations (no existing license server)

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud uses a floating license managed by the FlexNet license server or DSLS (Dassault Systèmes License Server). The license server information is specified when installing Web Application Platform with the Cameo Collaborator service, so make sure that you install a license server before installing Web Application Platform. The steps below explain how to apply the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud license and start using the product.

To apply the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud license

1. Install and start one of the following license servers:
  - [DSLS](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=dsls-installation-and-licensing&version=2026x Refresh1)
  - [FlexNet](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-license-server-installation-and-licensing&version=2026x Refresh1)
2. Check if the license server is running.
3. Add the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud license to the license server.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Web Application Platform Installation on a separate machine' space=''] with the Cameo Collaborator service and specify the license server details during the installation.

Once you apply the license as described above, the product is ready to use. Note that you can change the license server details later if needed.

#### WARNING: Working with multiple licenses

Working with multiple licenses

The FlexNet license server can contain only one license. If you want to add multiple licenses to the same FlexNet license server (e.g., Cameo Collaborator, Teamwork Cloud, and MagicDraw), contact your sales executive and ask them to merge the licenses.

##### Managing licenses via Teamwork Cloud Admin

There are multiple ways to manage Cameo Collaborator licenses

#### NOTE: Multiple pods' licensing

Multiple pods' licensing

If you have multiple pods (i.e. Kubernetes), you can configure all licenses either via the*webappplatform**.properties*file OR via the user interface in the **Settings**application.

If you want to manage licenses via the user interface in the **Settings**application, but at least one pod has the license configured via the *webapp.properties*file, you need to remove the license from the *webapp.properties*file and restart the services.

###### Applying a license via Teamwork Cloud Admin

To apply a Cameo Collaborator license via Teamwork Cloud Admin

1. Open Teamwork Cloud in a web browser using this address format: http(s)://<server_address>:<port>/webapp . 8443 is the default port.
2. Sign in to Teamwork Cloud with a user who has the Server Administrator role .
3. Click the Settings application to open it.
4. From the left-side menu, select the Cameo Collaborator license page. [ATTACHMENT filename='cc_license_in_webapp.png']
5. Select either the FlexNet or DSLS tab:
  - In the FlexNet tab, enter the license server address to view the list of available licenses. [ATTACHMENT filename='cc_flexnet_license.png'] Select a license and click Confirm to apply.
  - In the DSLS tab, select either Organization defined or Custom.
    - If you select Organization defined, choose a license from the list and click Confirm to apply.
    - If you select Custom, enter the license server address, choose a license from the list and click Confirm to apply.

###### Viewing active users

The Cameo Collaborator license page allows you to view active connections (users). They are displayed in the **Recently active connections**part of the page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Managing licenses via the properties file

###### Checking which license is in use

You can check Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud license information either or as described below.

To check the license information

1. In your internet browser, go to *http(s)://<server_address>:<port>/webapp*. Default portBy default, Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud uses port 8443.
2. If you are not signed in, enter your user credentials on the authentication page.
3. In the same internet browser, go to *http(s)://<domain_name>:<port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info*

The web page displays Cameo Collaborator license details, including the number of connections and expiration date.

###### Specifying which license should be used

If there are several licenses in the license server, you can specify which Cameo Collaborator license (edition) should be used.

To specify which Cameo Collaborator license should be used

1. Go to the *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf* directory.
2. Open for editing the *webappplatform.properties* file.
3. In the file, add the **cc.license.edition** property and specify its value (e.g., **cc.license.edition=100+ Connections** for an unlimited license). Valid property valuesThe valid values for the **cc.license.edition** property are:5 Connections10 Connections25 Connections50 Connections75 Connections100 Connections100+ Connections
4. Restart the Web Application Platform.

###### Resetting a license

The instructions below explain how to reset the Cameo Collaborator license if, for example, the connection with the license server was lost.

To reset the Cameo Collaborator license

1. In an internet browser, go to *http(s)://<server_address>:<port>/webapp*. Default portBy default, Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud uses port 8443.
2. If you are not signed in, enter your user credentials on the authentication page.
3. In the same internet browser, go to *http(s)://<domain_name>:<port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/reset*

**If the license reset was successful, the open web page will display this message:

{"initialized":true}

###### Specifying license server information for Web Application Platform

The licensing information should be specified when installing Teamwork Cloud and/or Web Application Platform. If you have not specified licensing information during installation or if you want to change it later, you can do this by following the steps below.

To specify/change the license server information

1. Go to the *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf* directory.
2. Open for editing the *webappplatform.properties* file. In the *webappplatform.properties* file:The values of some properties are placeholders, e.g., **mail.from.default=info@company.com**. Make sure to replace the placeholders with actual values.Read the comments with property descriptions to ensure accuracy when specifying property values.
3. Specify the following properties under the FlexNet/DSLS server properties for Web Application Platform services property group.
  - For the DSLS server:
    - **license.framework**
    - **license.server**
    - **license.dsls.server.mode**
  - For the FlexNet server:
    - **license.framework**
    - **license.server** Cameo Collaborator supports the [FlexNet Three-Server Redundancy mode (Triad License Server)](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-three-server-redundancy---triad-license-server&version=2026x Refresh1). If you want to use the Triad License Server, specify the **license.server** property in this format:license.server=<port>@<server1_address>,<port>@<server2_address>,<port>@<server3_address>
4. Restart Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 84.0922%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 19.6478%;" /><col style="width: 11.5537%;" /><col style="width: 9.53017%;" /><col style="width: 15.1438%;" /><col style="width: 17.8854%;" /><col style="width: 13.512%;" /><col style="width: 12.7271%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;"><p>License types</p></th><th style="text-align: connections</p></th><th style="text-align: center;"><p>Access to content shared for anonymous use*</p></th><th style="text-align: center;"><p>Predefined portals</p></th><th style="text-align: center;"><p>Customized portals</p></th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>5-connection license</strong></p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>-</p></td><td center;"><p>+</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>10-connection license</strong></p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>-</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>25-connection license</strong></p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>-</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>25</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Unlimited</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>50-connection license</strong></p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>-</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>50</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Unlimited</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>75-connection license</strong></p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>-</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>75</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Unlimited</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>100-connection license</strong></p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>-</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>100</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Unlimited</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Unlimited license</strong></p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Unlimited</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Unlimited</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td><td style="text-align: center;"><p>+</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>*</strong> Unauthenticated users can open Cameo Collaborator documents via <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sharing document links" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[public links]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h3>Applying licenses to new installations (no existing license server)</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target">Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud uses a floating license managed by the FlexNet license server or <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">DSLS (Dassault Systèmes License Server)</span>. The license server information is specified when installing Web Application Platform with the Cameo Collaborator service, so make sure that you install a license server before installing Web Application Platform. The steps below explain how to apply the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud license and start using the product.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud license</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="fe825fb1-2c54-4494-a709-3622b65a2ce6"><p>Install and start one of the following license servers:</p><ul><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=dsls-installation-and-licensing&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">DSLS</a></span></p></li><li><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-license-server-installation-and-licensing&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">FlexNet</a></p></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="a2915bbf-2e91-4fd4-9bfe-4c00f6bbe08e"><p>Check if the license server is running.</p></li><li data-uuid="aefaa230-5bd0-4f65-a73b-6074740150ba"><p>Add the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud license to the license server.</p></li><li data-uuid="2e4011b2-f68c-4c9a-8436-ef9f9b6ef77f"><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Web Application Platform Installation on a separate machine" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Install Web Aplication Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the Cameo Collaborator service and specify the license server details during the installation.</p></li></ol><p>Once you apply the license as described above, the product is ready to use. Note that you can change the license server details later if needed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e978a07-965f-415c-a1af-b7eb46839f02"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Working with multiple licenses</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The FlexNet license server can contain only one license. If you want to add multiple licenses <span>to the same FlexNet license server</span> (e.g., Cameo Collaborator, Teamwork Cloud, and MagicDraw), contact your sales executive and ask them to merge the licenses.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing  licenses via Teamwork Cloud Admin</h3><p>There are multiple ways to manage Cameo Collaborator licenses</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfa1d0d2-333e-4cd0-b197-f14e5469b0df"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Multiple pods' licensing</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: left;">If you have multiple pods (i.e. Kubernetes), you can configure all licenses either via the<em style="text-align: left;">webappplatform</em><em>.properties </em>file OR via the user interface in the <strong>Settings </strong>application.</p><p style="text-align: left;">If you want to manage licenses via the user interface in the <strong>Settings </strong>application, but at least one pod has the license configured via the <em>webapp.properties </em>file, you need to remove the license from the <em>webapp.properties </em>file and restart the services.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Applying a license via Teamwork Cloud Admin</h4><p>To apply a Cameo Collaborator license via Teamwork Cloud Admin</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="482fdcad-e238-4144-a9a4-3a4d74f00445">Open Teamwork Cloud in a web browser using this address format: <em style="text-align: left;">http(s)://&lt;server_address&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em>. 8443 is the default port.</li><li data-uuid="ef90b35e-4a03-4fc0-b456-f815b72fe97e"><span data-ref="97f3e5de-0d4f-4ad8-8602-ee5325800dad">Sign in to Teamwork Cloud with a user who has the Server Administrator role</span><span data-ref="97f3e5de-0d4f-4ad8-8602-ee5325800dad">.</span> </li><li data-uuid="0d5e49ab-5dfa-41ae-b9c2-8381a7eba5cb">Click the Settings application to open it.</li><li data-uuid="eac94b3b-f076-4573-baaa-8d5ea78d9a7e">From the left-side menu, select the Cameo Collaborator license page.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_license_in_webapp.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud licensing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="9e2ebadb-8fe5-49b5-aec5-e28d25fe7233">Select either the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-license-server-installation-and-licensing&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">FlexNet </a>or <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=dsls-installation-and-licensing&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">DSLS</a> tab:<ul><li>In the FlexNet tab, enter the license server address to view the list of available licenses.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_flexnet_license.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud licensing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Select a license and click <strong>Confirm </strong>to apply.</li><li>In the <strong>DSLS</strong> tab, select either <strong>Organization defined</strong> or <strong>Custom.</strong><ul><li>If you select <strong>Organization defined,</strong> choose a license from the list and click <strong>Confirm </strong>to apply.</li><li>If you select<strong> Custom, </strong>enter the license server address, choose a license from the list and click <strong>Confirm </strong><span>to apply.</span></li></ul></li></ul></li></ol><h4>Viewing active users</h4><p><span>The Cameo Collaborator license page allows you to view active connections (users). They are displayed in the <strong>Recently active connections </strong>part of the page. </span></p><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_license_active_connections.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud licensing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p><br /></p><h3>Managing licenses via the properties file</h3><h4><span>Checking which license is in use</span></h4><p>You can check Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud license information either <ac:link ac:anchor="Applying a license through Teamwork Cloud Admin"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[in the Settings app]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To check the license information</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="18d462e5-35ec-496d-987e-b63b332ae978"><p class="auto-cursor-target"> In your internet browser, go to <em style="text-align: left;">http(s)://&lt;server_address&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe1482e9-c4ab-4a93-ae27-618dcb8a6d1d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Default port</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud uses port 8443.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="ea2981ee-b4b3-4ce5-9bce-60d391c944f3"><p>If you are not signed in, enter your user credentials on the authentication page.</p></li><li data-uuid="db555e05-838e-4673-af5b-de3bcd59fd63"><p>In the same internet browser, go to <em>http(s)://&lt;domain_name&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info</em></p></li></ol><p>The web page displays Cameo Collaborator license details, including the number of connections and expiration date.</p><h4>Specifying which license should be used</h4><p>If there are several licenses in the license server, you can specify which Cameo Collaborator license (edition) should be used.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify which Cameo Collaborator license should be used</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1ed8af92-ab53-4dd6-8d9b-d1a713b301ff"><p>Go to the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em> directory.</p></li><li data-uuid="8912b841-36ec-45e3-91ac-46ed6c1ebaad"><p>Open for editing the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</p></li><li data-uuid="799f03b0-221d-4c17-9af9-ecd815eb1bad"><p>In the file, add the <strong>cc.license.edition</strong> property and specify its value (e.g., <strong>cc.license.edition=100+ Connections</strong> for an unlimited license).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08653ab3-0b85-4ce8-9b5f-14a6cc65c9f6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Valid property values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The valid values for the <strong>cc.license.edition</strong> property are:</p><ul><li><p>5 Connections</p></li><li>10 Connections</li><li><p>25 Connections</p></li><li><p>50 Connections</p></li><li><p>75 Connections</p></li><li><p>100 Connections</p></li><li><p>100+ Connections</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="c0234653-73a1-4af5-9b0c-63a632899550"><p>Restart the Web Application Platform.</p></li></ol><h4>Resetting a license</h4><p>The instructions below explain how to reset the Cameo Collaborator license if, for example, the connection with the license server was lost.</p><p><br /></p><p>To reset the Cameo Collaborator license</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e3a1d477-68d2-459e-9f7e-960a7b712c3d"><p class="auto-cursor-target"> In an internet browser, go to <em style="text-align: left;">http(s)://&lt;server_address&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb51825a-badf-4eed-b217-db90141c5a6d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Default port</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, Cameo Collabora</p><p>tor for Teamwork Cloud uses port 8443.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="c8c971ec-3aef-4208-977b-87aeb2125ef0"><p>If you are not signed in, enter your user credentials on the authentication page.</p></li><li data-uuid="212e017f-dc6c-46b3-a729-467f7b383fdd"><p>In the same internet browser, go to <em>http(s)://&lt;domain_name&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/reset</em></p></li></ol><p><em> </em>If the license reset was successful, the open web page will display this message:</p><p>{&quot;initialized&quot;:true}</p><h4>Specifying license server information for Web Application Platform</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The licensing information should be specified when installing Teamwork Cloud and/or Web Application Platform. If you have not specified licensing information during installation or if you want to change it later, you can do this by following the steps below.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To specify/change the l<span>icense server information</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c93596cd-4dc1-4841-a245-d84500b3a244"><p>Go to the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em> directory.</p></li><li data-uuid="fa66d05e-c3cc-45b1-883b-9d3f7b21066c"><p>Open for editing the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e24de815-3242-4ef4-8043-eedd63e07e43"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file:</span></p><ul><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The values of some properties are placeholders, e.g., <strong>mail.from.default=info@company.com</strong>. Make sure to replace the placeholders with actual values.</span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Read the comments with property descriptions to ensure accuracy when specifying property values.</span></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="41c7f86b-12b5-4c6b-bf79-3677d951afbc">Specify the following properties under the <em>FlexNet/DSLS server properties for Web Application Platform services</em> property group.<ul><li><p>For the DSLS server:</p><ul><li><p><strong>license.framework</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>license.server</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>license.dsls.server.mode</strong></p></li></ul></li><li><p>For the FlexNet server:</p><ul><li><p><strong>license.framework</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>license.server<br /></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7db8618e-5799-4302-9e90-687cd73d1271"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Cameo Collaborator supports the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-three-server-redundancy---triad-license-server&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">FlexNet Three-Server Redundancy mode (Triad License Server)</a>. If you want to use the Triad License Server, specify the <strong>license.server</strong> property in this format:</p><p>license.server=&lt;port&gt;@&lt;server1_address&gt;,&lt;port&gt;@&lt;server2_address&gt;,&lt;port&gt;@&lt;server3_address&gt;</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b457b3e9-3803-42c9-a6ce-601a43838eca"><p>Restart Web Application Platform.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001885 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00031: (2026x Refresh1) Case study

- page_id: `304001885`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001885/2026x+Refresh1+Case+study
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:03.700+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Roles application > (2026x Refresh1) Role-Based Access Control concept
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This case study presents a simple situation in an organization where users are classified into two groups: Team A and Team B according to their type. Team A is a group of users imported from an LDAP server and Team B is a group of internal users created using Teamwork Cloud Admin. This case study is used to illustrate how roles are assigned to each user in each team to work on different projects.

The following figure illustrates the two groups of users in the organization, Team A and Team B.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The users are grouped into Team A (external users) and Team B (internal users).

There are three projects for the teams to work on: Project A, Project B, and Project C.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project A is assigned to Team A, Project B is assigned to Team B, and Project C is assigned to both Team A and Team B.

Before starting to work on the projects, the organization needs to create a proper role and scope for everyone in the teams so that it can divide the tasks among them. The following figures show the team members' roles in Project A and Project B.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A specific role is assigned to each member in Team A in Project A and Team B in Project B.

Since Project C is the largest, the organization decided to assign it to both Team A and Team B.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Besides working on their respective project, both teams are also responsible for Project C.

More roles are created to manage the team members and the system. The following figure, for example, shows the user u7's roles as both User Manager and Security Manager, and the u8's role as a Project Creator. The user u7's role is to manage all users and access so the right scope to assign is **Global**. The user u8's role is to create projects so the right scope to assign is also **Global**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The role scope of the user u7 and user u8 is Global.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This case study presents a simple situation in an organization where users are classified into two groups: Team A and Team B according to their type. Team A is a group of users imported from an LDAP server and Team B is a group of internal users created using Teamwork Cloud Admin. This case study is used to illustrate how roles are assigned to each user in each team to work on different projects.</p><p>The following figure illustrates the two groups of users in the organization, Team A and Team B.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="casestudy1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case study" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The users are grouped into Team A (external users) and Team B (internal users).</h6><p>There are three projects for the teams to work on: Project A, Project B, and Project C. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="casestudy2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case study" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Project A is assigned to Team A, Project B is assigned to Team B, and Project C is assigned to both Team A and Team B.</h6><p>Before starting to work on the projects, the organization needs to create a proper role and scope for everyone in the teams so that it can divide the tasks among them. The following figures show the team members' roles in Project A and Project B.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="casestudy3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case study" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A specific role is assigned to each member in Team A in Project A and Team B in Project B.</h6><p>Since Project C is the largest, the organization decided to assign it to both Team A and Team B.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="casestudy4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case study" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Besides working on their respective project, both teams are also responsible for Project C.</h6><p>More roles are created to manage the team members and the system. The following figure, for example, shows the user u7's roles as both User Manager and Security Manager, and the u8's role as a Project Creator. The user u7's role is to manage all users and access so the right scope to assign is <strong>Global</strong>. The user u8's role is to create projects so the right scope to assign is also <strong>Global</strong>.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="casestudy5.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case study" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The role scope of the user u7 and user u8 is Global.</h6><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001947 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00032: (2026x Refresh1) Cassandra and the product cluster setup

- page_id: `304001947`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001947/2026x+Refresh1+Cassandra+and+the+product+cluster+setup
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:11.011+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Deployment Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Installation > (2026x Refresh1) Advanced installation
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page provides instructions to set up a Teamwork Cloud cluster on your system. A Teamwork Cloud cluster is composed of two clustering layers – Teamwork Cloud and Cassandra.

To set up a Teamwork Cloud cluster, you need to perform two separate tasks:

1. 
2. 

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An illustration of a Teamwork Cloud cluster and Cassandra cluster nodes within the Teamwork Cloud cluster.

##### Setting up a Cassandra cluster

Prior to establishing a Cassandra cluster, you need to determine the following.

- The initial number of nodes in the cluster.
- The IP address of each node.
- Determine which node will be the contact point.

The minimum recommended cluster size for Cassandra is 3 nodes, when taking availability and redundancy into account. Cassandra's default read and write consistency is quorum. Replication factor should match the minimum node count of 3. It is recommended to scale up a cluster in multiples of 3.

When setting up a Cassandra node, you need to configure a seed that indicates an initial contact point for the new node. So, you can configure the contact point using the IP of any existing active node in the cluster. Use the node's own IP when configuring the first node. For example, if you are configuring a three-node cluster and the IPs of the nodes are*10.1.1.101*,*10.1.1.102,*and*10.1.1.103,*select one of them (e.g.*10.1.1.101*) as the**seed**. When configuring**cassandra.yaml**, specify*10.1.1.101*as the contact point value for all of the three nodes.

| Machine IP | 10.1.1.101 | 10.1.1.102 | 10.1.1.103 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| seeds | 10.1.1.101, 10.1.1.102 | 10.1.1.101, 10.1.1.102 | 10.1.1.101, 10.1.1.102 |
| listen_address | 10.1.1.101 | 10.1.1.102 | 10.1.1.103 |
| broadcast_rpc_address | 10.1.1.101 | 10.1.1.102 | 10.1.1.103 |

Note that

listen_address

and

broadcast_rpc_address

are still set to their respective machine’s address

Now that you understand what a seed is and how to configure it, follow the instructions to install and configure Cassandra on Windows or Linux operating system.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Installing Cassandra on Windows' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Installing Apache Cassandra' space='']

After installing and configuring all nodes, start the seed node. Verify that it is operational by issuing the command "nodetool status".

Once the seed node is operational, you can proceed to start the other nodes in the cluster. You must allow the node to join the cluster before proceeding to the next node. After all nodes have started, the "nodetool status" command will display results as shown below.

```text

```

#### NOTE: Node Consistency

Node Consistency

In order to ensure that all nodes are consistent, please run the "nodetool repair" command nightly. This can be scheduled as a crontab job or via Windows Task Scheduler. This only needs to be executed on a single node.

###### Cassandra configuration parameters****

*-***esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic.contact-points**

The value of this parameter is a list of Cassandra node IP addresses. You can find this parameter in **application.conf**by looking for the following.

```text

```

*-***esi.persistence.cassandra.keyspace.replication-factor**

This parameter defines the Cassandra replication factor which describes how many copies of your data will be written by Cassandra. For example, replication factor 2 means your data will be written to 2 nodes.

For a three-node cluster, if you would like the cluster to be able to survive 1 node loss, you will need to set the replication factor to 3.

```text

```

Please note that this configuration will be used only for the first time Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudconnects to Cassandra and creates a new twc keyspace. Changing the replication factor after the keyspace has already been created is rather a complex task. Read [this document](https://docs.datastax.com/en/cql/3.3/cql/cql_using/useUpdateKeyspaceRF.html) if you need to change it.

Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Clouduses QUORUM for both write and read consistency levels.

[Click here](https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra/3.0/cassandra/dml/dmlAboutDataConsistency.html) for a detailed explanation of data consistency.

To start up the Teamwork Cloud cluster, start the server on the seed machine and wait until you see a message similar to the following in the **server.log**.

```text

```

Then you can start the server on the remaining machines. You should see the following messages in the **server.log** which shows all 3 nodes are forming the cluster.

```text

```

##### Setting up a product cluster

Before setting up a Teamwork Cloud cluster, you need to determine the following.

- The initial number of nodes in the cluster.
- The IP address of each Teamwork Cloud node.
- Prepare a list of IPs of Cassandra nodes.

The instructions below use the following sample environment for ease of understanding.

- 3 nodes Teamwork Cloud cluster.
- Teamwork Cloud node IP addresses are 10.1.1.111, 10.1.1.112 , and 10.1.1.113.
- All nodes will be used as seed nodes.
- IP addresses of Cassandra nodes are 10.1.1.101, 10.1.1.102, 10.1.1.103.

Setting up the Teamwork Cloud cluster involves 2 groups of parameters in **application.conf**.

###### Teamwork Cloud clustering parameter

*-***akka.cluster.seed-nodes**

This parameter indicates the initial seed for the cluster.

If you install Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudusing the installer file, you will be asked to provide the seed node IP during installation process and the value will be configured in **application.conf**.

If you install using the zip file, you will need to manually configure the parameter in****application.conf**. Search for the following:

```text

```

Replace *${seed-node.ip}*with the IP addresses of the seed nodes, so it should look similar to the following:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page provides instructions to set up a Teamwork Cloud cluster on your system. A Teamwork Cloud cluster is composed of two clustering layers – Teamwork Cloud and Cassandra.</p><p>To set up a Teamwork Cloud cluster, you need to perform two separate tasks:</p><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Setting up a Cassandra cluster"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Set up a Cassandra cluster]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Setting up a Teamwork Cloud cluster"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Set up a Teamwork Cloud cluster]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="teamwork_cloud_cluster.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cassandra and the product cluster setup" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An illustration of a Teamwork Cloud cluster and Cassandra cluster nodes within the Teamwork Cloud cluster.</h6><h3>Setting up a Cassandra cluster</h3><p>Prior to establishing a Cassandra cluster, you need to determine the following.</p><ul><li>The initial number of nodes in the cluster.</li><li>The IP address of each node.</li><li>Determine which node will be the contact point.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b27a4cad-02d2-472d-ace0-bc5ce80dd311"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The minimum recommended cluster size for Cassandra is 3 nodes, when taking availability and redundancy into account. Cassandra's default read and write consistency is quorum. Replication factor should match the minimum node count of 3. It is recommended to scale up a cluster in multiples of 3.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">When setting up a Cassandra node, you need to configure a seed that indicates an initial contact point for the new node. So, you can configure the contact point using the IP of any existing active node in the cluster. Use the node's own IP when configuring the first node. For example, if you are configuring a three-node cluster and the IPs of the nodes are </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">10.1.1.101</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">10.1.1.102,</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">10.1.1.103,</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> select one of them (e.g. </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">10.1.1.101</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">) as the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">seed</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. When configuring </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">cassandra.yaml</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, specify </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">10.1.1.101</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> as the contact point value for all of the three nodes.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Machine IP</th><th>10.1.1.101</th><th>10.1.1.102</th><th>10.1.1.103</th></tr><tr><td>seeds</td><td><strong>10.1.1.101, 10.1.1.102</strong></td><td><strong>10.1.1.101, 10.1.1.102</strong></td></tr><tr><td>listen_address</td><td><span>10.1.1.101</span></td><td><span>10.1.1.102</span></td><td><span>10.1.1.103</span></td></tr><tr><td>broadcast_rpc_address</td><td><span>10.1.1.101</span></td><td><span>10.1.1.102</span></td><td><span>10.1.1.103</span></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002072 space=MCS version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00033: (2026x Refresh1) Cassandra cluster operations

- page_id: `304002072`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002072/2026x+Refresh1+Cassandra+cluster+operations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:17.278+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Deployment Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Installation > (2026x Refresh1) Advanced installation
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Replacing the down node

To replace a down/unresponsive node with a new node (different address) in the cluster, prepare the replacement node configuration to match other cluster nodes. Then, start the replacement node by specifying the dead node to replace with.

Prepare the replacement node by configuring it with the same cluster name, seed nodes, and data center name as other nodes in the cluster.

Add the following parameter to JVM_OPTS in *cassandra-env.sh* (default location: */etc/cassandra/conf*) for the replacement node:

```text
]]>
```

This parameter will drop the dead node and add the new node to the cluster upon startup. Remove the parameter after successful startup and replacement node joins the cluster.

To monitor the state and progress of replacement operations (including move and remove), use this command to display the progress of streaming operations:

```bash

```

##### Dropping node

Before dropping a node from a live cluster, make sure there are enough live nodes in the cluster to maintain replication factor. If dropping a node that is down, refer to [Replace Down Node] procedure.

To drop a live node, run the following command while on the node:

```bash

```

To drop a dead/offline node, run the following command:

```bash
]]>
```

If the dropped node was a seed node, update cassandra.yaml and reload seeds on all live nodes by executing the following command:

```bash

```

Otherwise, Teamwork Cloud service may not start.

##### Modifying the seed node

If a seed node was added, removed, or replaced from a cluster, the seed node list will need to be updated.

Use the following command to check what seeds are currently configured for the cluster:

```bash

```

Update the *seeds* list configuration, under *seed_provider* of *cassandra.yaml*, for every node in the cluster. Use the following command to apply the seed list change on a live node.

```bash

```

Otherwise, the changes will not be applied until each node is restarted.

##### Syncing nodes

The Cassandra repair tool is used to fix data inconsistencies between nodes. If a down node was replaced, repair will stream differences in the data from out-of-sync data between nodes.

For incremental repair of the current node, use the command:

```bash

```

Incremental repairs should be run regularly on each node. Apache recommends doing this every 7 days.

For a full repair of all data, use the command:

```bash

```

A new node to the cluster should bootstrap and sync data from other nodes. If a new node has not streamed data from other nodes on the cluster, use the following command to initiate the process:

```bash

```

For additional information, refer to the [official Cassandra documentation](https://cassandra.apache.org/doc/4.1/index.html).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Replacing the down node</h3><p><span>To replace a down/unresponsive node with a new node (different address) in the cluster, prepare the replacement node configuration to match other cluster nodes. Then, start the replacement node by specifying the dead node to replace with.</span></p><p><span>Prepare the replacement node by configuring it with the same cluster name, seed nodes, and data center name as other nodes in the cluster.</span></p><p><span>Add the following parameter to JVM_OPTS in <em>cassandra-env.sh</em> (default location: <em>/etc/cassandra/conf</em>) for the replacement node:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9db496b5-a4a1-48f7-87a1-3891eb11abcf"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-Dcassandra.replace_address_first_boot=<dead_node_ip>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This parameter will drop the dead node and add the new node to the cluster upon startup. Remove the parameter after successful startup and replacement node joins the cluster.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To monitor the state and progress of replacement operations (including move and remove), use this command to display the progress of streaming operations:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="deaf9a63-18e4-473c-b01f-d23c438c0b33"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool netstats]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Dropping node</h3><p><span>Before dropping a node from a live cluster, make sure there are enough live nodes in the cluster to maintain replication factor. ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a0f40e3-7083-4696-a166-d4538ec1ab4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter If a down node was replaced, repair will stream differences in the data from out-of-sync data between nodes. </span></p><p><span>For incremental repair of the current node, use the command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f00da33-99f1-45f2-93c5-7e17da232a29"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool repair]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Incremental repairs should be run regularly on each node. Apache recommends doing this every 7 days.</span></p><p><span>For a full repair of all data, use the command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d2520ec-1407-4bb4-97cf-c122c67befc2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool repair --full]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A new node to the cluster should bootstrap and sync data from other nodes. If a new node has not streamed data from other nodes on the cluster, use the following command to initiate the process:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f54edcd-5f1e-4d59-b4c7-e6502f3af87c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool rebuild]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>For additional information, refer to the <a href="https://cassandra.apache.org/doc/4.1/index.html">official Cassandra documentation</a>.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002836 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00034: (2026x Refresh1) Changing appearance of custom comment fields

- page_id: `304002836`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002836/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+appearance+of+custom+comment+fields
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:18.583+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Sharing and exporting > (2026x Refresh1) Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Custom comment fields are rendered in the *comment/comment-fragment.html* file. You can only customize the appearance of comment fields when comments are saved in the project. Comment fields are rendered in the following code block:

```text
]]>
```

##### Hiding custom comment fields

You can hide custom comment fields in the exported document.

To hide custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/comment-fragment.html file.
2. Find the custom comment field code block and remove it from the template.

##### Example of changing the appearance of custom comment fields

Let’s assume that you want to change the current appearance of custom comment fields in the exported document from chips to a simple HTML list.

To change the appearance of custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/comment-fragment.html file.
2. Find the custom comment field code block and change it as displayed below: ****
 ****
]]>

##### Changing the color of the custom comment field chip

You can change the default color of custom comment fields

To change the color of custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/styles.html file.
2. Find the *style* tag with *th:fragment="CommentStyles"*, locate the *main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip*tag within, and change the property *background-color: #336699;* to any other desired color code: table.main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip {
 background-color: #336699;
 …
 }
…
]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Custom comment fields are rendered in the <em>comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file. You can only customize the appearance of comment fields when comments are saved in the project. Comment fields are rendered in the following code block:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d35c102-e4b7-4bf3-8dd8-348ccdb1ad03"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<div th:if="${comment.customization}" class="comment-customization-fields">
    <div th:each="field : ${comment.customization.fields}"
        class="comment-field-chip">
       <b th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.name, '
', '<br>')}"></b>
       <b th:if="${field.showValue}" th:text="':'"></b>
       <span th:if="${field.showValue}" th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.value, '
', '<br>')}"></span>
    </div>
</div>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Hiding custom comment fields</h3><p>You can hide custom comment fields in the exported document.</p><p><br /></p><p>To hide custom comment fields</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the custom comment field code block and remove it from the template.</li></ol><h3><br />Example of changing the appearance of custom comment fields</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to change the current appearance of custom comment fields in the exported document from chips to a simple HTML list.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the appearance of custom comment fields</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the custom comment field code block and change it as displayed below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="50d1faf7-eac6-42da-b85e-54d5c5b04554"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<ul th:if="${comment.customization}" class="comment-customization-fields">

', '<br>')}"></b>

       <span th:if="${field.showValue}" th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.value, '
', '<br>')}"></span>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002985 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00035: (2026x Refresh1) Changing font color

- page_id: `304002985`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002985/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+font+color
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:22.619+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Sharing and exporting > (2026x Refresh1) Customizing PDF/HTML templates > (2026x Refresh1) Changing font properties
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The color for the whole document is not set in templates. It is rgb(0, 0, 0) by default. If you want to set a non-default color, you have to add color information to the HTML template as a CSS *color* property.

However, some elements of exported documents use non-default colors. If you want to change the font color of such an element, you need to locate the element in the templates and change its font color value.

##### Example of changing the font color for the whole document

Let’s assume you want to change the font color for the whole document to rgb(90, 90, 90) (dark grey).

To change the font color for the whole document

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles".
3. Locate the *body* tag within and add the *color: rgb(90, 90, 90);* property to the list of other body style attributes. body {
 color: rgb(90, 90, 90);
 …
 }
…
]]>

If you export a document with the added color property, you will see that the font color of the document text is dark grey, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample document with the font color changed to dark grey.

##### Example of changing the font color of page numbers in the footer

Let’s assume that you want to change the font color of the pagination information at the bottom of the page to blue.

To change the font color of page numbers in the footer to blue

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/footer.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles".
3. Locate the *.footer* class definition and change the color property value to *blue*. .footer {
 color: blue;
 …
 }
…
]]>

Now, the page numbers in the footer of the exported document will be in blue font.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample document with the pagination font color changed to blue.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

If you want to set a non-default color, you have to add color information to the HTML template as a CSS <em>color</em> property.</p><p>However, some elements of exported documents use non-default colors. If you want to change the font color of such an element, you need to locate the element in the templates and change its font color value.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Example of changing the font color for the whole document</h3><p>Let’s assume you want to change the font color for the whole document to rgb(90, 90, 90) (dark grey).</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font color for the whole document</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the <em>style</em> tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>body</em> tag within and add the <em>color: rgb(90, 90, 90);</em> property to the list of other body style attributes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81175f14-144a-4477-83e0-a76157a56f7e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   body {
            color: rgb(90, 90, 90);
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>If you export a document with the added color property, you will see that the font color of the document text is dark grey, as shown below.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_font_color.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing font color" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="letter-spacing: normal;line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A sample document with the font color changed to dark grey.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Example of changing the font color of page numbers in the footer</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to change the font color of the pagination information at the bottom of the page to blue.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font color of page numbers in the footer to blue</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/footer.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the style tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>.footer</em> class definition and change the color property value to <em>blue</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d0c6117-27fb-43b9-8a82-cf5ed71f297c"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   .footer {
            color: blue;
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>Now, the page numbers in the footer of the exported document will be in blue font.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_pagination_font_color.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing font color" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="letter-spacing: normal;line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A sample document with the pagination font color changed to blue.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002917 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00037: (2026x Refresh1) Changing font size

- page_id: `304002917`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002917/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+font+size
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:20.653+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Sharing and exporting > (2026x Refresh1) Customizing PDF/HTML templates > (2026x Refresh1) Changing font properties
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Font sizes are changed by setting or changing the standard *font-size* CSS property. The base size for the whole document is not set by default, so you will need to add this property to the *<template_folder>/common/styles.html* template.

Some document elements, like headers and cover page headings, have their own font sizes. To change the size of such a font, you need to find out which file defines the property and change its value.

##### Example of changing the font size for the whole document

Let’s assume you want to change the font size for the whole document to 15px.

To change the font size for the whole document to 15px

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles" .
3. Locate the *body* tag within and add the *font-size: 15px;* property to the list of other body style attributes. body {
 font-size: 15px;
 …
 }
…
]]>

Now, the main font size of the exported document will be 15px.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Font sizes are changed by setting or changing the standard <em>font-size</em> CSS property. The base size for the whole document is not set by default, so you will need to add this property to the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> template.</p><p>Some document elements, like headers and cover page headings, have their own font sizes. To change the size of such a font, you need to find out which file defines the property and change its value.</p><h3><br />Example of changing the font size for the whole document</h3><p>Let’s assume you want to change the font size for the whole document to 15px.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font size for the whole document to 15px</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the <em>style</em> tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>body</em> tag within and add the <em>font-size: 15px;</em> property to the list of other body style attributes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97bb7ad3-f7c4-477d-89bc-1aa3755e7bf5"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   body {
            font-size: 15px;
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>Now, the main font size of the exported document will be 15px.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002892 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00038: (2026x Refresh1) Changing server or service address

- page_id: `304002892`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002892/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+server+or+service+address
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:19.815+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Deployment Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Configuration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you configure a signed SSL certificate, you may need to switch the server address from an IP address to a Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN). In such cases, you only need to update the public-facing address for Web Application Platform. All other service contact points on the backend remain as internal addresses.

###### Switching from IP address to FQDN for an all-in-one deployment

Edit the fields listed in the table below and replace the IP address with the FQDN.

| Configuration file | Parameter |
| --- | --- |
| authserver.properties | authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist |
| webappplatform.properties | authentication.server.ip |
| service.url |  |

###### Updating server address for all deployments

If you need to update a server address due to deployment or networking changes, edit the following configuration files. Replace all instances of the old address with the new address.

| Configuration file to edit | Default path |
| --- | --- |
| cassandra.yaml | /etc/cassandra/conf |
| application.conf | <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration |
| authserver.properties | <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf |
| webappplatform.properties | <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf |

###### Additional configuration for multi-server deployment

You may need additional configuration changes if services are deployed on different servers.

| Configuration file | Remote service | Parameter to check |
| --- | --- | --- |
| application.conf | cassandra | contact-points in esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver |
| webapp (authentication) | url in esi.auth |  |
| webappplatform.properties | twcloud | twc.ip |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you configure a signed SSL certificate, you may need to switch the server address from an IP address to a Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN). In such cases, you only need to update the public-facing address for Web Application Platform. All other service contact points on the backend remain as internal addresses.</span></p><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Switching from IP address to FQDN for an all-in-one deployment</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Edit the fields listed in the table below and replace the IP address with the FQDN.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Configuration file</span></th><th scope="col"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Parameter</span></th></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">authserver.properties</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist</span></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">webappplatform.properties</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">authentication.server.ip</span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">service.url</span></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Updating server address for all deployments</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you need to update a server address due to deployment or networking changes, edit the following configuration files. Replace all instances of the old address with the new address.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003140 space=MCS version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00039: (2026x Refresh1) Changing SSL certificates

- page_id: `304003140`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003140/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+SSL+certificates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:35.232+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Administration Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

By default, Web Application Platform is installed with SSL certificates. If needed, you can change the existing SSL certificate file with a new one as described below.

##### Changing the SSL certificate file

You can change the default SSL certificate file that comes with Web Application Platform installation as described below.

To change the SSL certificate file

1. Go to the <web_app_platform_installation_directory>\conf directory and open to edit the server.xml file.
2. In the server.xml file, change the default values of the following properties to the actual path to the certificate file and password: ImportantIf you havecreated the SSL certificatefile as described in the above section, the **certificateKeystorePassword** property value must be the same as the **-storepass** and **-keypass** property values that you specified when creating the SSL certificate.
  - certificateKeystoreFile="<path_to_certificate_file>"
  - certificateKeystorePassword="<password>"
3. Save and close the server.xml file.
4. Restart Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>By default, Web Application Platform is installed with SSL certificates. If needed, you can change the existing SSL certificate file with a new one as described below.</p><h3><br />Changing the SSL certificate file</h3><p>You can change the default SSL certificate file that comes with Web Application Platform installation as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the SSL certificate file</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;web_app_platform_installation_directory&gt;\conf</em> directory and open to edit the <em>server.xml</em> file.</li><li>In the <em>server.xml</em> file, change the default values of the following properties to the actual path to the certificate file and password:<br /><ul><li><pre>certificateKeystoreFile=&quot;&lt;path_to_certificate_file&gt;&quot;</pre></li><li><pre>certificateKeystorePassword=&quot;&lt;password&gt;&quot;<br /><br /></pre></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="027c18b8-3066-4e11-9adb-e8dab5278eae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you have </span>created the SSL certificate<span> file as described in the above section, t</span>he <strong>certificateKeystorePassword</strong> property value must be the same as the <strong>-storepass</strong> and <strong>-keypass</strong> property values that you specified when creating the SSL certificate.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the <em>server.xml</em> file.</li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002932 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00040: (2026x Refresh1) Changing the font for the whole document

- page_id: `304002932`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002932/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+the+font+for+the+whole+document
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:21.295+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Sharing and exporting > (2026x Refresh1) Customizing PDF/HTML templates > (2026x Refresh1) Changing font properties
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change the font for the whole document, you need to do the following:

1. Specify the font in the HTML templates.
2. .
3. If you want the font of the table of contents to be other than the default Arial font, specify it in the properties file.

##### Example of changing the document font to Noto Sans

Let’s assume you want to use the Google *Noto Sans* font as the main font of the document.

To change the default font to Noto Sans

1. Change the font-family in the templates of the document-exporter:
  1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html .
  2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles" .
  3. Locate the body tag within and change the font-family value to "Noto Sans" .
  4. Add a generic font family, e.g., sans-serif, to the end of the list so that an alternative font is selected if the main font is unavailable. body {
 font-family: "Noto Sans", sans-serif;
 …
 }
…
]]>
2. Install the font on the operating system where the document-exporter web application is deployed.
3. Open the *<web_app_platform_install_root>/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties*file and set*Noto Sans*as the value of the*fonts.toc.default*property.

##### Installing fonts

Some publicly available fonts have their own installation scripts and instructions. If this is the case, follow the official installation instructions.

Fonts must be embeddable. You can configure the export to avoid embedding for most of the fonts, but it is not recommended because all users who open the exported document need to have these fonts installed on their computers.

###### Example of installing the Noto Sans font

Let's say you need to install the Noto Sans font.

To install the Noto Sans font on Linux

1. Download the Noto Sans font from the official Google Noto Fonts website.
2. Extract the archive to a directory of your choice.
3. Copy the extracted font files to the /usr/share/fonts/noto directory.
4. Execute the following command to clean the font cache:
5. Execute the following command to check if the new Noto sans font is installed:

To install the Noto Sans font on Windows

1. Download the Noto Sans font from the official Google Noto Fonts website.
2. Extract the archive to a directory of your choice.
3. Select and right-click the extracted font files, then select Install .
4. Open the Windows fonts list to see if the Noto Sans font is installed.

###### Example of installing a custom/proprietary font

Let's assume you need to install a custom font, e.g., *MyCompanyFont*, available as a .tiff file, e.g., *MyCompanyFont.tiff.*

**

To install a custom font on Linux

1. Copy the font file to the /usr/share/fonts/mycompany directory.
2. Execute the following command to clean the font cache:
3. Execute the following command to check if the *MyCompanyFont* font is installed:

To install a custom font on Windows

1. Right-click the MyCompanyFont.tiff file and select Install .
2. Open the Windows fonts list to see if the MyCompanyFont font is installed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
