<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002789 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00041: (2026x Refresh1) Changing the license

- page_id: `304002789`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002789/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+the+license
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:14.685+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Deployment Guide > (2026x Refresh1) License management
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Using the FlexNet license server

###### Magic Collaboration Studio

You need to have the Server Administrator role to change the license.

To change the Magic Collaboration Studio license using the FlexNet license server

1. In a web browser, go to http(s)://<domain_name>:<port>/webapp to open the authentication page.
2. Sign in to the Administrator account or a user account with the Server Administrator ****role
3. Open the Settings application.
4. In the left-side menu select the Server license .
5. In the License information card, click the Change License button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete the license will open.
6. Click the **Change** button. You cannot undo your action after clicking the **Change** button.
7. If you want to apply a license from another license server, change the license server address in the Server license field.
8. Click the Show Licenses button.
9. Select a new license key and click the Confirm button.

After changing the license, a new license is displayed on the **License information** card.

###### Teamwork Cloud

Changing a license or updating it through the **Server license** section on the **Settings application**requires a user with a Server Administrator role. Changing a license means returning the current one you are using to the FLEXnet License Server, setting the server capacity back to zero, and then leasing a new one.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### License information in Server settings.

The **License Information** section contains the details about the license server and the license key that is currently in use. Click **Change License**button only if you want to change your license because it will cause the current license to be deleted and allow you to select one of the available licenses and use it.

To get a new Teamwork Cloud license from the current license server

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Accessing web applications' space=''] with an Administrator account or user with a Server Administrator role.
2. Using the App bar, navigate to the Settings application.
3. Click the Change License button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete the license you are currently using will open.
4. Click **Update**. You cannot undo your action after clicking **Update**.
5. Your current license server address appears in the License Server box. Click the Show Licenses button: [ATTACHMENT filename='show_license.png'] Teamwork Cloud Admin will update the license information and show all available license keys on the current license server in tabular form: [ATTACHMENT filename='applying_license.png']
6. Select a new license key from the table and click Confirm button. Your new license will be applied and appear in the Server License section.

To get a new Teamwork Cloud license from another license server

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Accessing web applications' space=''] with an Administrator account.
2. Using App bar, navigate to Settings application.
3. Click the Change License button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete your current license will open.
4. Click the Yes button.
5. Type the address and port of another license server in the Server license box.
6. Click the Show License button. All available license keys on the new server license will appear in tabular form.
7. Select a new license key from the table and click Confirm . Your new license will be applied and appear in the License Information section.

##### Using DSLS

###### Magic Collaboration Studio

If you use DSLS, follow the steps for[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying the license' space=''] and change the appropriate property values to update the license.

###### Teamwork Cloud

Use the**application.conf**file in your Teamwork Cloud installation directory to change Teamwork Cloud license. Follow the procedure for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying the license' space=''] and change the appropriate property values.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Using the FlexNet license server</h3><h4>Magic Collaboration Studio</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b05ed720-80cf-4802-846e-276fdca0dfda"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to have the Server Administrator role to change the license.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To change the Magic Collaboration Studio license using the FlexNet license server</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a7bd54ff-bac4-4768-982d-0425e7b4a52b">In a web browser, go to <em>http(s)://&lt;domain_name&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em> to open the authentication page.</li><li data-uuid="e466838c-a5ec-40d1-854b-a41be9270012">Sign in to the Administrator account or a user account <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">with the </span>Server Administrator<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <strong> </strong>role</span></li><li data-uuid="6bc01335-13d3-409a-8bc4-3ddd76270bc3">Open the <strong> Settings </strong>  application.</li><li data-uuid="7e161248-6936-4561-b00f-9dbaa5749325">In the left-side menu select the <strong>Server license</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="cfe6e4d0-8d43-471b-bb82-4b7adccca353">In the <strong>License information</strong> card, click the <strong>Change License </strong>button. Changing a license means returning the current one you are using to the FLEXnet License Server, setting the server capacity back to zero, and then leasing a new one.  </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_license.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing the license" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">License information in Server settings.</h6><p>The <strong>License Information</strong> section contains the details about the license server and the license key that is currently in use. Click <strong>Change License </strong>button only if you want to change your license because it will cause the current license to be deleted and allow you to select one of the available licenses and use it. </p><p><br /></p><p>To get a new Teamwork Cloud license from the current license server</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a977bee8-6f6c-479e-a317-b260345cfb51"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Accessing web applications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Log into the Teamwork Cloud Admin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with an Administrator account or<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> user with a </span><strong>Server Administrator</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> role. </span></li><li data-uuid="6df5be06-e4fb-42aa-a6f6-e9be42c71c74">Using the App bar, navigate to the <strong>Settings</strong> application.</li><li data-uuid="a77edc3e-1cf0-4b02-b67a-037e4135fe35">Click the <strong>Change License </strong>button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete the license you are currently using will open.</li><li data-uuid="324bdb96-7ccd-4651-a92a-3a9d1ecef600"><p>Click <strong>Update</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b036a481-38b5-4332-b822-89925f29ffb5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot undo your action after clicking <strong>Update</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="1211901c-98e1-4b6f-8176-94337520eb40">Your current license server address appears in the <strong>License Server</strong> box. Click the <strong>Show Licenses </strong>button:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_license.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing the license" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Teamwork Cloud Admin will update the license information and show all available license keys on the current license server in tabular form:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="applying_license.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing the license" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="d359e3e4-e14e-40fa-9c90-641243ba6e10">Select a new license key from the table and click <strong>Confirm </strong>button. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001519 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00042: (2026x Refresh1) Changing the title of the table of contents

- page_id: `304001519`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001519/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+the+title+of+the+table+of+contents
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:29.860+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Sharing and exporting > (2026x Refresh1) Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can define the table of contents in the c*ommon/table-of-contents.html* template file and customize the appearance of the table of contents, including:

- The title
- The indentation of items
- The color

##### Example of changing the title of the table of contents for a document

Let’s assume you want to change the default title *Table of Contents* for a document to *Index.*

To change the default title*Table of Contents*

1. Open the *<template folder>/common/table-of-contents.html* file.
2. Find the *h1* element with a value of *Table of Contents*within the html *body*element, and change its value to *Index*: textIndex]]>

If a document is exported using the updated template, the new table of contents title *Index* would appear as the title of the table of contents:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a modified table of contents title.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>You can define the table of contents in the c<em>ommon/table-of-contents.html</em> template file and customize the appearance of the table of contents, including:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="849acf33-e70b-47d9-970a-8a0212b0ddda"><span>The title</span></li><li data-uuid="378925af-387a-4367-b1a0-8eddbf7c7492"><span>The indentation of items</span></li><li data-uuid="1cd0283b-2fba-4acb-af23-ce5d1d18203a"><span>The color</span></li></ul><h3><span>Example of changing the title of the table of contents for a document</span></h3><p><span>Let’s assume you want to change the default title <em>Table of Contents</em> for a document to <em>Index.</em></span></p><p><span>To change the default title<em> Table of Contents</em></span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3acd9f8a-c9d9-4f45-9165-07853fe84e58"><span>Open the <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/table-of-contents.html</em> file.</span></li><li data-uuid="36272b8c-0f75-46f1-abdb-db2af36aed43"><span>Find the <em>h1</em> element with a value of <em>Table of Contents </em>within the html <em>body </em>element, and change its value to <em>Index</em>:<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4fbc681-0c3f-400d-83c0-a7ee6fe6b6e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[…
<body>
   <h1>Index</h1>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span><br /></span></li></ol><p><span>If a document is exported using the updated template, the new table of contents title <em>Index</em> would appear as the title of the table of contents:</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_exported_table_of_contents.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing the title of the table of contents" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of a modified table of contents title.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003138 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00043: (2026x Refresh1) Changing Web Application Platform property values

- page_id: `304003138`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003138/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+Web+Application+Platform+property+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:34.850+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Administration Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After installing the Web Application Platform, you can change its property values in the *webappplatform.properties* file. Click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using no-installer' space=''], to learn more about each property.

To change Web Application Platform property values

1. Go to the *<* install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open for editing the webappplatform.properties file.
2. Change the desired property values.
3. Restart the Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After installing the Web Application Platform, you can change its property values in the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file. Click <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using no-installer" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, to learn more about each property.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change Web Application Platform property values</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><em>&lt;</em></span><em style="text-align: left;">install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em> directory and open for editing the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li>Change the desired property values.</li><li>Restart the Web Application Platform.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003171 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00044: (2026x Refresh1) Checking current version and user count

- page_id: `304003171`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003171/2026x+Refresh1+Checking+current+version+and+user+count
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:39.378+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Administration Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can check the version, maximum user count and other licence information of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud installed in your environment.

To check Cameo Collaborator for TWC licence information

1. Sign in to Cameo Collaborator for TWC.
2. Go to http(s)://<cc_for_twc_host>:<cc_for_twc_port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info .

The website displays all licence information, such as licence type, expiration date, maximum user count and product version.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can check the version, maximum user count and other licence information of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud installed in your environment.</p><p><br /></p><p>To check Cameo Collaborator for TWC licence information</p><hr /><ol><li>Sign in to Cameo Collaborator for TWC.</li><li>Go to <em>http(s)://&lt;cc_for_twc_host&gt;:&lt;cc_for_twc_port&gt;/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info</em>.</li></ol><p><br />The website displays all licence information, such as licence <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d4430fa4-f2e4-49a6-a0d3-6698f17baa5b">type</ac:inline-comment-marker>, expiration date, maximum user count and product version.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001951 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00045: (2026x Refresh1) Checking LDAP connection

- page_id: `304001951`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001951/2026x+Refresh1+Checking+LDAP+connection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:11.758+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Settings application > (2026x Refresh1) Managing LDAP servers
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Upon adding and configuring the LDAP server, you may want to test whether your connection to the LDAP server works. If the test is not successful, you need to make necessary changes to the settings, save the changes, and retest the connection.

To check the connection to a LDAP server

1. On the **LDAP Management** page, select an LDAP server which connection you want to check and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
2. Select **Check connection**. If the connection works, you will get a success message, otherwise connection-related error messages will appear.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Upon adding and configuring the LDAP server, you may want to test whether your connection to the LDAP server works. If the test is not successful, you need to make necessary changes to the settings, save the changes, and retest the connection.</p><p><br /></p><p>To check the connection to a LDAP server</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the <strong>LDAP Management</strong> page, select an LDAP server which connection you want to check and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Select <strong>Check connection</strong>. If the connection works, you will get a success message, otherwise connection-related error messag<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8d9608cd-f9a4-45af-af63-ab4980ff04c2">es will appear.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002861 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00046: (2026x Refresh1) Collaborator View Diagram

- page_id: `304002861`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002861/2026x+Refresh1+Collaborator+View+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:53:52.074+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Publishing documents > (2026x Refresh1) Document templates > (2026x Refresh1) Working with custom document templates > (2026x Refresh1) Creating a custom template
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator View Diagram defines the structure of a Cameo Collaborator document. The essential components of this diagram are Document, View, and ViewPoint. You can create them by using the diagram palette of the Collaborator View Diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Basic View Diagram containing Document, View, and Viewpoint elements with Directed Composition and Conform relations.

##### The Document element

A Document is the root element of a template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template. You can select the template by this name this name in the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog when [publishing a project](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/(2026x Refresh1) Publishing+from+graphical+user+interface).

##### The View element

A View is a standalone description of the model within your template. Each view is used to display a certain type of information. You can create as many views as required. Different views not only display different types of information, e.g., requirements or use cases, but also create separate navigation trees (views) in a published project. Note that you can choose to have all of your views displayed in a single tree by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Enabling the Single Tree View' space=''].

The main properties of a View element are:

- Skip if Empty - if set as true , a published project does not display views without data.
- Document View - if set as true , this particular view of a document is presented as in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Enabling the Document View' space=''].
- **Allow To Switch Property View Mode** - if set as *true*, the mode of this particular view can be switched from Model View to Document View and vice versa. To change the mode when browsing a document, open the view, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select **Model View** or **Document View**.

##### The Viewpoint element

A Viewpoint is used to define the rules that dictate how information is collected from the model and displayed on the web. Viewpoints are related to views through a Conform relationship. The rules contained in a viewpoint are modeled in an activity-like form utilizing a special [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram' space=''].

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The essential components of this diagram are Document, View, and ViewPoint. You can create them by using the diagram palette of the Collaborator View Diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_template_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Collaborator View Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Basic View Diagram containing Document, View, and Viewpoint elements with Directed Composition and Conform relations.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Document element</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A Document is the root element of a template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template. You can select the template by this name this name in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog when</span> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/(2026x Refresh1) Publishing+from+graphical+user+interface">publishing a project</a>.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The View element</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A View is a standalone description of the model within your template. Each view is used to display a certain type of information. You can create as many views as required. Different views not only display different types of information, e.g., requirements or use cases, but also create separate navigation trees (views) in a published project. Note that you can choose to have all of your views displayed in a single tree by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling the Single Tree View" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[enabling the Single Tree View mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p>The main properties of a View element are:</p><ul><li><strong>Skip if Empty</strong> -<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> if set as </span><em>true</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, a published project does not display views without data.</span></li><li><strong>Document View</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> - </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">if set as </span><em>true</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, this particular view of a document is presented as in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling the Document View" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Document view mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Allow To Switch Property View Mode</strong> - if set as <em>true</em>, the mode of this particular view can be switched from Model View to Document View and vice versa. Viewpoints are related to views through a Conform relationship. The rules contained in a viewpoint are modeled in an activity-like form utilizing a special <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Additional resources</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://youtu.be/NAoYVUJhT2A">Webinar <em>Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator</em></a></li><li><a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Collaborator View Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002873 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00047: (2026x Refresh1) Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram

- page_id: `304002873`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002873/2026x+Refresh1+Collaborator+Viewpoint+Method+Diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:19.361+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Publishing documents > (2026x Refresh1) Document templates > (2026x Refresh1) Working with custom document templates > (2026x Refresh1) Creating a custom template
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram is a specialized activity diagram that defines a Viewpoint. This diagram allows you to obtain model data using several types of operators and present the obtained data as tables, images, paragraphs, lists, and sections in a web portal.

Once a Viewpoint is applied to a View, exposed elements can be operated by three types of operators: Collect, Sort, and Filter. You can use these operators to expand or narrow the collection of elements that are used in a Viewpoint method. After the data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, use the Table Structure and Template operators to adjust how the data is displayed. The figure below shows how available operators are grouped in the diagram palette.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The diagram palette containing operators available in a Viewpoint Method Diagram.

##### Collect operators

In a Viewpoint Method Diagram, Collect operators are used to divide the elements in an exposed Package into the elements that will be presented in a web portal and the elements that will be ignored. This way you can expand or narrow the collection of elements used in a Viewpoint method. There are a number of collection methods, each of which have their own Call Behavior Action.

##### Filter operators

Filter operators allow you to narrow the scope of data to the data of interest according to one or multiple filter criteria of various types (such as stereotype, name, metaclasse, etc). In most cases, the "FilterBy..." operator needs to have the "CollectBy..." operator preceding it so that the filter operator would have the data set to look through and filter.

##### Sort operators

Sort operators allow you to sort a data set according to the desired property. Data can be sorted by attribute, property, or expression. Note that first you have to collect elements in order to have a data set to sort.

##### Table Structure operators

Table Structure operators allow you to create custom tables by using various table components. The Viewpoint Method Diagram below shows the example of a simple table with only two columns,

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of a simple table with two columns: Animal Name and Description.

##### Template operators

After data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, several operators can be used to adjust how the data will be displayed. Template operators are used in a Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram to determine in what format data will be displayed in the document views - as paragraphs, properties tables, images, bulleted lists, etc.

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram is a specialized activity diagram that defines a Viewpoint. This diagram allows you to obtain model data using several types of operators and present the obtained data as tables, images, paragraphs, lists, and sections in a web portal. </p><p>Once a Viewpoint is applied to a View, exposed elements can be operated by three types of operators: Collect, Sort, and Filter. You can use these operators to expand or narrow the collection of elements that are used in a Viewpoint method. After the data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, use the Table Structure and Template operators to adjust how the data is displayed. The figure below shows how available operators are grouped in the diagram palette.</p><p><br /></p><h3><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="viewpoint_method_diagram_pallet.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h3><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The diagram palette containing operators available in a Viewpoint Method Diagram.</span></h6><h3>Collect operators</h3><p>In a Viewpoint Method Diagram, Collect operators are used to divide the elements in an exposed Package into the elements that will be presented in a web portal and the elements that will be ignored. This way you can expand or narrow the collection of elements used in a Viewpoint method. There are a number of collection methods, each of which have their own Call Behavior Action.</p><h3><br />Filter operators</h3><p>Filter operators allow you to narrow the scope of data to the data of interest according to one or multiple filter criteria of various types (such as stereotype, name, metaclasse, etc). In most cases, the &quot;FilterBy...&quot; operator needs to have the &quot;CollectBy...&quot; operator preceding it so that the filter operator would have the data set to look through and filter.</p><h3><br />Sort operators</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Sort operators allow you to sort a data set according to the desired property. Data can be sorted by attribute, property, or expression. Note that first you have to collect elements in order to have a data set to sort.</span></p><h3><br />Table Structure operators</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Table Structure operators allow you to create custom tables by using various table components. The Viewpoint Method Diagram below shows the example of a simple table with only two columns,</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_structure_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The example of a simple table with two columns: Animal Name and Description.</h6><h3>Template operators</h3><p>After data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, several operators can be used to adjust how the data will be displayed.  Template operators are used in a Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram to determine in what format data will be displayed in the document views - as paragraphs, properties tables, images, bulleted lists, etc.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Additional resources</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://youtu.be/NAoYVUJhT2A">Webinar <em>Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator</em></a></li><li><a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003124 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00048: (2026x Refresh1) Comments pane

- page_id: `304003124`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003124/2026x+Refresh1+Comments+pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:34.032+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['comments-pane', 'filtering-comments', 'sorting-comments', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The comments pane is located on the right side of a published model portal. It displays all comments of all the views of a published model and their replies. Comments on the comments pane can be textual or graphical, resolved or unresolved, low or high priority. See the figure below to learn how different kinds of comments are displayed on the comments pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The comments pane of a published model portal.

The comments pane can be shown or hidden.

To show/hide the comments pane

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='show-hide_comments_pane.png'] in the title bar of a published model portal.

##### Navigating between comments and commented items

Clicking a comment or its reply in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the commented area. If the comment is graphical, clicking it in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the comment. Navigation between comments and commented items work both ways. If you select a commented item or a graphical comment in the content pane, the related comment is selected in the comments pane. If you select an item in the content pane that has several comments, the list of comments opens and you can select the comment you want find. The figures below display how graphical comments and items with textual comments are displayed in the content pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commented items with textual comments displayed on the content pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Graphical comments displayed on the content pane.

##### Searching for comments

The comments pane allows you to search for comments by the comment name, text, author and even creation date. You can also select to see only the unresolved comments.

To search for comments

1. Type a search phrase in the comment search box on the top of the comments pane.
2. Optionally, do one or both of the following to filter comments:
  - Under the comment search box, select the Unresolved check box, to search only for unresolved comments.
  - Under the comment search box, select the **Unread** check box, to search only for the comments that have not been read.
  - Under the comment search box, select the **High priority** check box, to search for comments with high priority.

When you start typing the search phrase in the comments search box, the comment pane shows only the comments that match the search phrase text. Also, selecting one or several of the available filters helps to narrow down search results.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The comments pane is located on the right side of a published model portal. It displays all comments of all the views of a published model and their replies. Comments on the comments pane can be textual or graphical, resolved or unresolved, low or high priority. See the figure below to learn how different kinds of comments are displayed on the comments pane.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comments_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Comments pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">The comments pane of a published model portal.</h6><p>The comments pane can be shown or hidden.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show/hide the comments pane</p><hr /><ul><li style="text-align: left;">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show-hide_comments_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Comments pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the title bar of a published model portal.</li></ul><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Navigating between comments and commented items</h3><p>Clicking a comment or its reply in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the commented area. If the comment is graphical, clicking it in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the comment. Navigation between comments and commented items work both ways. If you select a commented item or a graphical comment in the content pane, the related comment is selected in the comments pane. If you select an item in the content pane that has several comments, the list of comments opens and you can select the comment you want find. The figures below display how graphical comments and items with textual comments are displayed in the content pane.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="commented_items_in_content_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Comments pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">Commented items with textual comments displayed on the content pane.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="graphical_comments_in_content_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Comments pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">Graphical comments displayed on the content pane.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Searching for comments</h3><p>The comments pane allows you to search for comments by the comment name, text, author and even creation date. You can also select to see only the unresolved comments.</p><p><br /></p><p>To search for comments</p><hr /><ol><li>Type a search phrase in the comment search box on the top of the comments pane.</li><li>Optionally, do one or both of the following to filter comments:<br /><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Unresolved</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a"> check box, to search only for unresolved comments.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the <strong>Unread</strong> check box, to search only for the comments that have not been read.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the <strong>High priority</strong> check box, to search for comments with high priority.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></li></ol><p><br />When you start typing the search phrase in the comments search box, the comment pane shows only the comments that match the search phrase text. Also, selecting one or several of the available filters helps to narrow down search results.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003200 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00049: (2026x Refresh1) Committing changes

- page_id: `304003200`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003200/2026x+Refresh1+Committing+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:42.916+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Editing and contextualizing
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every time you modify a model in Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, the changes are saved locally, so you have to commit them to Teamwork Cloud when you are done editing. If there are changes in a Cameo Collaborator document that are not yet committed, the yellow dot over the cloud icon instantly notifies you about it, as shown in the image below.

To commit changes to Teamwork Cloud

1. In the app bar, click the cloud icon with the yellow dot notifying that there are uncommitted changes (see the image below).
2. In the **Uncommitted changes** dialog, enter the commit message in the **Commit message** field. This step is optional.
3. In the same dialog, Click the **Commit** button to commit model changes to Teamwork Cloud. Discarding model changesIf you want to discard uncommitted model changes, click the **Discard** button in the **Uncommitted changes** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Committing model changes to Teamwork Cloud.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

If there are changes in a Cameo Collaborator document that are not yet committed, the yellow dot over the cloud icon instantly notifies you about it, as shown in the image below.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To commit changes to Teamwork Cloud</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the app bar, click the cloud icon with the yellow dot notifying that there are uncommitted changes (see the image below).</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Uncommitted changes</strong> dialog, enter the commit message in the <strong>Commit message</strong> field. This step is optional.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the same dialog, Click the <strong>Commit</strong> button to commit model changes to Teamwork Cloud.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d191a22c-cd4f-426f-ba5e-fded8b0c3c7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Discarding model changes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to discard uncommitted model changes, click the <strong>Discard</strong> button in the <strong>Uncommitted changes</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="committing_changes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Committing model changes to Teamwork Cloud.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001607 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00050: (2026x Refresh1) Common Access Card

- page_id: `304001607`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001607/2026x+Refresh1+Common+Access+Card
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:34.817+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Deployment Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Configuration > (2026x Refresh1) Authentication server
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Basic configuration

In order for Common Access Card (CAC) authentication to work, SSL must be enabled in Web Application Platform.

Next, you need to enable certificate authentication:

```text

```

The next step is to configure which part of the subject DN (distinguished name) will be used as the username (**authentication.certificate.username.template**), and which part will be displayed in the login screen (**authentication.certificate.displayname.template**).

Both of these values default to using (CN)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Configuring username

```text

```

The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in parenthesis that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or SAN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name).

For example, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB:

Template: *(CN)*, username: *JohnDoe*

Template: *(O)-(CN)*, username: *MyCompany*-*JohnDoe*

Template: *CERT_(CN)*, username: *CERT_JohnDoe*

##### Configuring the value displayed in the login button

To configure the value displayed in the login button, we must edit the **authentication.certificate.displayname.template** property:

```text

```

For example, as shown in the picture above, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is *CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB*, and the display template is *(CN) CERTIFICATE*, the button will display „JOHNDOE CERTIFICATE“.

For a list of all the advanced properties available for configuration, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Authentication by certificate' space=''].

##### Configuring truststore

CAC integration requires that a truststore containing the Certificate Authority (CA) certificates that issue the user's certificates would exist.

Certificate verification should be enabled in the *WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml* file. See the Tomcat configuration information for it on [this page](https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_SSLHostConfig). E.g., if the Tomcat SSL implementation is JSSE-based, you will need to add the following attributes to the SSLHostConfig property under Connector:

- certificateVerification with the value optional or required (if ONLY the certificate authentication is allowed).
- truststoreFile - path to the truststore file containing the CA certificates.
- truststorePassword - truststore password.
- truststoreType - e.g. "JKS" or "PKCS12".

##### Certificate revocation list

The authentication server supports 2 methods of handling certificate revocation lists - via a URL, or via a local list stored in the file system. To enable this feature, uncomment either **authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url** or **authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file**, and point it to the location of the revocation list.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Basic configuration</h3><p>In order for Common Access Card (CAC) authentication to work, SSL must be enabled in <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Web Application Platform.</span></p><p>Next, you need to enable certificate authentication:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bed921e8-f752-494e-ab99-43a4e73b0620"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.enabled=true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The next step is to configure which part of the subject DN (distinguished name) will be used as the username (<strong>authentication.certificate.username.template</strong>), and which part will be displayed in the login screen (<strong>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</strong>).</p><p>Both of these values default to using (CN)</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="login.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Common Access Card" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3><span>Configuring username</span></h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e20ae9f6-f5bc-4ee9-b38d-e2f895e5e10d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.username.template=(CN)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in parenthesis that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN (<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or SAN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name) </span>. </p><p>For example, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>(CN)</em>, username: <em>JohnDoe</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>(O)-(CN)</em>, username: <em>MyCompany</em>-<em>JohnDoe</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>CERT_(CN)</em>, username: <em>CERT_JohnDoe</em></p><h3><span>Configuring the value displayed in the login button</span></h3><p>To configure the value displayed in the login button, we must edit the <strong>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</strong> property:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a94afc71-b451-4bb8-bec3-e194fdeb8601"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.displayname.template=(CN)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example, as shown in the picture above, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is <em>CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB</em>, and the display template is <em>(CN) CERTIFICATE</em>, the button will display „JOHNDOE CERTIFICATE“.</p><p>For a list of all the advanced properties available for configuration, please refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Authentication by certificate" /></ac:link>.  </p><h3>Configuring truststore</h3><p>CAC integration requires that a truststore containing the Certificate Authority (CA) certificates that issue the user's certificates would exist.</p><p>Certificate verification should be enabled in the <em>WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml</em> file. See the Tomcat configuration information for it on <a href="https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_SSLHostConfig">this page</a>. E.g., if the Tomcat SSL implementation is JSSE-based, you will need to add the following attributes to the SSLHostConfig property under Connector:</p><ul><li><strong>certificateVerification</strong> with the value <em>optional</em> or <em> required </em>  (if ONLY the certificate authentication is allowed).</li><li><strong>truststoreFile</strong> - path to the truststore file containing the CA certificates.</li><li><strong>truststorePassword</strong> - truststore password.</li><li><strong>truststoreType</strong> - e.g. &quot;JKS&quot; or &quot;PKCS12&quot;.</li></ul><h3>Certificate revocation list</h3><p>The authentication server supports 2 methods of handling certificate revocation lists - via a URL, or via a local list stored in the file system. To enable this feature, uncomment either <strong>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url</strong> or <strong>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file</strong>, and point it to the location of the revocation list.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51f3c881-22bb-4a81-85d3-737159d3974d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url=http://someserver.somedomain.com/revocation.lst
authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file=/opt/local/revovcation.lst]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003272 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00051: (2026x Refresh1) Comparing elements in different versions

- page_id: `304003272`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003272/2026x+Refresh1+Comparing+elements+in+different+versions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:47.995+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Other features
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can track the evolution of your project and see how your elements have transformed over time by comparing diagrams and tables across different versions of a document.

##### Opening comparison screen

To compare elements in different document versions

1. Navigate to the document section you wish to compare in other versions.
2. At the top right, select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > **Compare versions.**The version comparison screen opens.

##### Structure of comparison screen

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

- Document version drop-down. In the app bar at the top of the screen, you can select the Cameo Collaborator document versions you wish to compare by choosing them in the Document version drop-down list.
- Comparison mode selection. In the centre of the app bar, you can select the mode for table or diagram comparison.
  - Fade: this mode allows you to transition between two overlapping diagrams. Use the sliding toolbar to adjust opacity and discern subtle differences. Fade mode is only available for diagrams. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Vertical: select this mode to view diagrams or tables side by side. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Horizontal: this mode shows diagrams or tables one below the other. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Compare texts: in tables, the Compare texts option is available, showing differences in texts. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Filtering: when comparing tables, you can click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the table name and enter text to filter out table elements. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Project version drop-down. When the project is changed from within the document, only the project version updates, not the document version. To compare different project versions, use the project version switcher to switch between project versions. [ATTACHMENT filename='cc_project_version_comparer.png'] You can only select the project version for documents that are published for editing.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

/><br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="f972444b-2403-469d-bab9-d35c88d9ae57"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Compare texts:</span></strong><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001583 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00052: (2026x Refresh1) Conditional user groups

- page_id: `304001583`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001583/2026x+Refresh1+Conditional+user+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:33.234+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Users application > (2026x Refresh1) Creating users and user groups
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In certain security-related authentication scenarios, user access rights must be determined dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account. Teamwork Cloud allows you to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values by using the conditional user groups feature.

When users are added to a conditional user group, they receive the group permissions only if the group condition is met, for example, if they log in from the right location.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- To allow users to create conditional user groups, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Enabling conditional user groups' space='']**Conditional user groups** option must be enabled in the Settings application.
- To define the conditions for conditional user groups, SAML attributes must already be configured on the SAML page of the Settings application.

To create a conditional user group

1. Go to the Users application
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating users and user groups' space=''] or select to edit an existing user group.
3. On the group details pane, turn on the **Conditional user group** switch.
4. In the **Condition** box, enter the condition users must meet to be granted the group permissions. Available attributes are displayed below the **Condition**box. . When defining the condition for a conditional user group, make sure to use SAML attributes in the same way they are configured on the **SAML** page of the Settings application.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the user group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a conditional user group.

##### Condition syntax

###### Configuring SAML attributes

Conditions for conditional user groups accept only the SAML attributes configured onthe Settings application's**SAML**pageused for SAML integration.

To configure SAML attributes for conditional user groups

1. Open the Web Application Platform's Settings > SAML page .
2. Fill in the**SAML user attributes for conditional user groups**field with SAML attributes you want to use for conditional groups.
3. Save the configuration.

###### Condition operators

You can define the conditions for conditional user groups using the configured SAML attributes and the following operators:

- Boolean operators: AND; OR
- Equality operators: ==; !=
- Grouping of statements: ()

#### TIP: Example. When defining conditions, use SAML attributes the same way the are configured in your SAML integration.

Example. When defining conditions, use SAML attributes the same way the are configured in your SAML integration.

(loginLocation == "Dallas" AND clearance == "Secret") OR department == "ITservices"

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In certain security-related authentication scenarios, user access rights must be determined dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account. Teamwork Cloud allows you to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values by using the conditional user groups feature.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When users are added to a conditional user group, they receive the group permissions only if the group condition is met, for example, if they log in from the right location.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3795cf74-c477-44a7-9174-3f5a134ec183"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="0fefb863-1bc9-40f4-b4f4-55d7f99526df">To allow users to create conditional user groups, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling conditional user groups" /><ac:link-body><strong>Conditional user groups</strong> option</ac:link-body></ac:link> must be enabled in the Settings application.</li><li data-uuid="a7f8eb96-b35e-4da2-ab6c-edba88023800">To define the conditions for conditional user groups, SAML attributes must already be configured on the <strong>SAML</strong> page of the Settings application.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To create a conditional user group</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Go to the Users application</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating user groups"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating users and user groups" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Start creating a new]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or select to edit an existing user group.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">On the group details pane, turn on the <strong>Conditional user group</strong> switch.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the <strong>Condition</strong> box, enter the condition users must meet to be granted the group permissions. Available attributes are displayed below the <strong>Condition </strong>box. <ac:link ac:anchor="Condition syntax"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about condition syntax]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f92836e6-bb1e-4a16-882f-b7d0add8f8c4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When defining the condition for a conditional user group, make sure to use SAML attributes in the same way they are configured on the <strong>SAML</strong> page of the Settings application.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="e4d96e26-af48-4fcc-babd-d1bc25259a77"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_user.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Conditional user groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to save the user group.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_conditional_user_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Conditional user groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a conditional user group.</h6><h3>Condition syntax</h3><h4>Configuring SAML attributes</h4><p>Conditions for conditional user groups accept only the SAML attributes configured on<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> the Settings application's </span><strong style="text-align: left;">SAML </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page</span><span> used for SAML integration.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To configure SAML attributes for conditional user groups</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="4cfe6740-d18f-4d6a-bf8e-1f74c8dccabd">Open the<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Web Application Platform's<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Settings<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&gt;<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">SAML<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page</span><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></em></li><li data-uuid="d68c7e8f-f206-4e15-8738-51f9088ec0bc"><p><span>Fill in the<span> </span><strong>SAML user attributes for conditional user groups</strong><span> </span>field with SAML attributes you want to use for conditional groups.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="850c9972-30a5-4dc7-b99f-dd1e70126787"><p><span>Save the configuration.</span></p></li></ol><h4><br />Condition operators</h4><p>You can define the conditions for conditional user groups using the configured SAML attributes and the following operators:</p><ul><li>Boolean operators: AND; OR</li><li>Equality operators: ==; !=</li><li>Grouping of statements: ()</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="79b1abde-1ceb-460e-a1e3-ff0aa9c1cacc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example. When defining conditions, use SAML attributes the same way the are configured in your SAML integration.</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>A conditional user group with condition <strong>(loginLocation == &quot;Dallas&quot; AND clearance == &quot;Secret&quot;) OR department == &quot;ITservices&quot;</strong> will grant its permissions to the members who either log in from Dallas and have the &quot;Secret&quot; clearance level or belong to the IT Services department.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001461 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00054: (2026x Refresh1) Configuring authentication server for SAML integration

- page_id: `304001461`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001461/2026x+Refresh1+Configuring+authentication+server+for+SAML+integration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:25.744+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Deployment Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Configuration > (2026x Refresh1) Authentication server > (2026x Refresh1) SAML integration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Configure the authentication server for SAML integration as described below.

To configure the authentication server parameters for SAML integration

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Settings application' space=''].
2. In the left-side menu of the Settings application page, select **SAML** to open the SAML configuration page.
3. In the **Configuration** tab of the **SAML configuration** page, turn on the **Enable SAML authentication** switch. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the values of the and click the **Save** button.
5. Wait for the message confirming that the SAML configuration was saved successfully.
6. Select the **Identity provider metadata** tab and upload the Identity Provider metadata by doing one of the following:
  - Click the **Upload** button and select the XML file with the Identity Provider metadata.
  - Copy the XML file's content directly into the **Identity provider metadata** tab.
7. Click the **Save** button to save the uploaded metadata. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. If the authentication server is deployed separately from Web Application Platform, restart the authentication server.

##### SAML parameters

| Parameter | Description | Default value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Entity ID | The entity ID of the Service Provider. It is generated automatically. | - |
| Name ID format | The name ID format that contains the username of the authenticated user. | urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:X509SubjectName |
| Name ID attribute | The SAML user attribute to map to the Teamwork Cloud username instead of the SAML user name ID, which is used by default. | - |
| Signature algorithm | SAML integration requests a signature algorithm. | RSA-SHA256 |
| AllowCreate Name ID policy | If enabled, adds the AllowCreate attribute to the NameIDPolicy parameter in the SAML request. | Switched on |
| Disable forced authentication | Sets ForceAuthn to true or false in the AuthnRequest in SAML-based authentication. Change it carefully, as you won't be able to log in with another user after logging out if this switch is turned on. This flag is only used in case the Identity Provider does not support single logout. | Switched off |
| AuthN contexts | (Optional) If AuthN contexts need to be used, fill in one AuthN context per line. | - |
| AuthN contexts comparison type | If AuthN contexts are used, select the AuthN Context comparison type. | exact |
| SAML button title | The button title displayed for the SAML user on the login page. | SAML |
| Show SAML authentication detailed error | If enabled, a detailed SAML authentication error text is displayed for users. | Switched off |
| Login RelayState format | The RelayState format for the authentication request. | uuid |
| Logout RelayState format | The RelayState format for the logout request. | uuid |
| SAML user attributes for conditional user groups | A list of SAML attributes (one attribute per line) that can be used while defining conditions for conditional user groups. . | - |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003169 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00055: (2026x Refresh1) Configuring data logging

- page_id: `304003169`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003169/2026x+Refresh1+Configuring+data+logging
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:38.986+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Administration Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the default data logging settings, including the logging level, for Web Application Platform and Teamwork Cloud as described below.

Web Application Platform uses the Apache Log4j logging framework. To learn more, see [https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/](https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/).

To configure the data logging

1. Go to the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open the log4j2.properties file.
2. Depending on your needs do one or both of the following:
  - To configure the logging of the Web Application Platform-related information, define the following properties:
    - logger.webappplatform.name
    - logger.webappplatform.level
    - logger.webappplatform.additivity
    - logger.webappplatform.appenderRef.file.ref
  - To change the number and size of logs to be stored, and the log storage time, define the following properties:
    - appender.file.type
    - appender.file.name
    - appender.file.fileName
    - appender.file.filePattern
    - appender.file.layout.type
    - appender.file.layout.pattern
    - appender.file.policies.type
    - appender.file.policies.time.type
    - appender.file.policies.size.type
    - appender.file.policies.size.size
    - appender.file.strategy.type
    - appender.file.strategy.max
3. Restart the Java application server.

#### NOTE: Standard logging levels

Standard logging levels

The Log4J framework has the following standard logging levels: OFF, FATAL, ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE, ALL.

##### How to turn off logging

You can disable data logging to a specific file by changing the logging level of the related logger.

To turn off logging

1. Locate the logger you want to turn off.
2. Change the logging level of that logger to "off".
3. Restart the Java application server.

#### NOTE: Turning logging back on

Turning logging back on

To turn on the data logging that has been disabled, change the logging level of the related logger from OFF to the logging level you want to use.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

To learn more, see <a href="https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/">https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/</a>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To configure the data the following:<ul><li>To configure the logging of the Web Application Platform-related information, define the following properties:<ul><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>logger.webappplatform.name</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>logger.webappplatform.level</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>logger.webappplatform.additivity </strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>logger.webappplatform.appenderRef.file.ref</strong></li></ul></li><li>To change the number and size of logs to be stored, and the log storage time, define the following properties:<ul><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.name</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.fileName</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.filePattern</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.layout.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.layout.pattern</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.policies.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.strategy.max</strong></li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Restart the Java application server.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90211fe4-01f9-4daf-bd9d-7d841f113e69"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Standard logging levels</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Log4J framework has the following standard logging levels: OFF, FATAL, ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE, ALL.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>How to turn off logging</h3><p>You can disable data logging to a specific file by changing the logging level of the related logger.</p><p><br /></p><p>To turn off logging</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4ae5340b-b346-494e-aae4-c61cb3d5c452">Locate the logger you want to turn off.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Change the logging level of that logger to &quot;off&quot;.</p></li><li>Restart the Java application server.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5eea956c-4dba-4676-ba50-11ebe8d5b40e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Turning logging back on</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To turn on the data logging that has been disabled, change the logging level of the related logger from OFF to the logging level you want to use.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
