<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003294 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00078: (2026x Refresh1) Creating project branches

- page_id: `304003294`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003294/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+project+branches
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:49:19.794+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Resources application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create project branches directly in the Resources application without using a modeling tool.

To create a project branch

1. Go to the Resources application.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the right side of the project line and select Resource history .
3. On the line of the project version from which you want to create a branch, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Create branch . [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_branch.png']
4. In the Create branch dialog, enter the branch name and comment (optional).
5. Click the Create button.

A new branch is created and displayed on the project history screen. You can also use the Resource app to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing project branches' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create project branches directly in the Resources application without using a modeling tool.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a project branch</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the Resources application.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating project branches" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the right side of the project line and select <strong>Resource history</strong>.</li><li>On the line of the project version from which you want to create a branch, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating project branches" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Create branch</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_branch.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating project branches" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Create branch</strong> dialog, enter the branch name and comment (optional).</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />A new branch is created and displayed on the project history screen. You can also use the Resource app to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing project branches" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[manage project branches]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002772 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00079: (2026x Refresh1) Creating remote resource configurations

- page_id: `304002772`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002772/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+remote+resource+configurations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:12.585+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Settings application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In Teamwork Cloud, you can create remote resource configurations allowing youto synchronize resources from one Teamwork Cloud server to another based on the selected synchronization trigger.This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. To learn how you can benefit from this functionality, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Cross-cluster resource synchronization' space=''].

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Be aware that **cross-cluster resource synchronization** is only supported between deployments running the **same major version**. While the **minor version** does not need to match, both deployments must be on the same major version to ensure compatibility and smooth operation.

- To create remote resource configurations, you must have the Remote Synchronization Manager role. The role scope needs to include the target Teamwork Cloud server categories where you want the synchronized resources to be saved.
- Cross-cluster resource synchronization works only in one direction - from the source to the target server. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.
- Remote resource configurations are created and managed on the target Teamwork Cloud server.

To create a remote resource configuration

1. Go to the **Settings**application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the wizard for creating a remote resource configuration.
3. In the first step of the wizard, log in to the source Teamwork Cloud server:
  1. In the **Source Teamwork Cloud address** box, enter the address of the source server from which you want to synchronize resources or categories.
  2. In the **Username** and **Password** boxes, enter the credentials of a source server user.
  3. If needed, toggle the **Use Secure Connection (TLS)** button to enable/disable the secure connection. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Required source server user permissionsFor successful synchronization, the user whose credentials are used to log in to the source Teamwork Cloud server needs to have the Read Resources permission in the source server.
4. Click **Next**.
5. Select the source server resources and/or categories you want to synchronize with the target Teamwork Could server (the current server) and click **Next**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Select the category in the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synchronized resources to be stored and click **Next**.
7. Select one of the following synchronization triggers: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - **Manual** - the resource or category is synchronized only when initiated by a user.
  - **On every new commit** - the resource or category is synchronized whenever a new resource version becomes available in the source server.
  - On specific commit tag - the resource or category is synchronized when a resource is committed to the source server with a specific commit tag.
8. Click **Create**.

A separate remote resource configuration is created for every resource and/or category selected in step 5. All existing remote resource configurations and their details including synchronization status are displayed in the remote repository management screen as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The remote repository management screen where you can create and manage remote resource configurations.

In the following table, you can find the descriptions of synchronizationstatus.

| Status | Description |
| --- | --- |
| In queue | The resource is staged to be synchronized and now is in queue. |
| Not synchronized | The resource is not staged for synchronization and you need to trigger it manually. |
| Synchronized | The resource was synchronized successfully from the source server. |
| Partially synchronized | This is an alert, indicating that there is a more recent version available in the source server. You may want to consider re-synchronizing the resource if your use case requires having the latest version on the target server. Reproducible with manual synchronization configuration. |
| Error | There can be a couple of reasons why there is an error. A reason will be displayed in the tooltip while hovering over the status. |

##### Synchronizing resources manually

After creating a remote resource configuration, the related resource or category is synchronized depending on the selected synchronization mode. However, you can also synchronize resources manually as described below.

Manual

To synchronize resources manually

1. Go to the Settings application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. Select the remote resource configurations of the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
3. In the top right corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='synchronize.png'] .

##### Deleting remote resource configurations

If remote resource configurations are no longer needed, you can delete them as described below.

To delete a remote resource configuration

1. Go to the Settings application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. Select the remote resource configurations you want to delete.
3. In the top right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
4. In the open dialog, choose one of the following:
  - **DELETE CONFIGURATION ONLY**- choose to delete only the configuration, leaving synchronized categories/resources in the target server.
  - **DELETE ALL**- choose to delete the configuration along with the synchronized resources and/or categories.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In Teamwork Cloud, you can create remote resource configurations allowing you </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">to synchronize resources from one Teamwork Cloud server to another based on the selected synchronization trigger. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. To learn how you can benefit from this functionality, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cross-cluster resource synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[cross-cluster resource synchronization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cde10e88-3be8-4dba-b27e-f63e0cadfdf3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be aware that <strong>cross-cluster resource synchronization</strong> is only supported between deployments running the <strong>same major version</strong>. /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1575fcd-054f-4e67-82f7-ea7d6bf9a1f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Required source style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the source server resources and/or categories you want to synchronize with the target Teamwork Could server (the current server) and click <strong>Next</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_resources.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating remote resource configurations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the category in the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synchronized resources to be stored and click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select one of the following synchronization triggers:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Manual</strong> - the resource or category is synchronized only when initiated by a user.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>On every new commit</strong> - the resource or category is synchronized whenever a new resource version becomes available in the source server.</span></li><li><strong>On specific commit tag</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> - the resource or category is synchronized when a resource is committed to the source server Reproducible with manual synchronization configuration.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Error</strong></td><td>There can be a couple of reasons why there is an error. A reason will be displayed in the tooltip while hovering over the status.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Synchronizing resources manually</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After creating a remote resource configuration, the related resource or category is synchronized depending on the selected synchronization mode. However, you can also synchronize resources manually as described below.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1b8b1e8-a5ac-433a-a480-c50585fe30ca"><ac:rich-text-body>If you select the <strong>Manual</strong> synchronization mode for a remote resource configuration, you need to synchronize resources by yourself as described in this section.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To synchronize resources manually</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Go to the Settings application and select <strong>Remote repository management</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the remote resource configurations of the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.</span></li><li>In the top right corner of the screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchronize.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating remote resource configurations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Deleting remote resource configurations</h3><p>If remote resource configurations are no longer needed, you can delete them as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To delete a remote resource configuration</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Go to the Settings application and select <strong>Remote repository management</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the remote resource configurations you want to delete.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating remote resource configurations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /></span></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the open dialog, choose one of the following:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">DELETE CONFIGURATION ONLY</span> </strong>- choose to delete only the configuration, leaving synchronized categories/resources in the target server. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">DELETE ALL</span> </strong>- choose to delete the configuration along with the synchronized resources and/or categories.</span></li></ul></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003309 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00080: (2026x Refresh1) Creating resource usage maps

- page_id: `304003309`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003309/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+resource+usage+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:49:21.115+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Resource Usage Map
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Resource Usage Map application allows you to create usage maps for one, several or all the projects in the Teamwork Cloud server. In addition, you can have have multiple usage maps open simultaneously to compare the usages of different projects as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a resource usage map.

To create a resource usage map

1. In the Resource Usage Map application, click the Select icon. If there are other usage maps open already, the **Select** icon is located at the top left corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource Usage Map application structure' space=''].
2. In the open dialog, select the resources whose usages you want to see in the resource usage map by doing one of the following:
  - To select one or several resources, start typing the resource name in the search box and select the desired resources from the search results.
  - To select all resources in one or several categories, select to search for categories as shown in the image below, start typing the category name in the search box, and select the desired categories form the search results.
  - To select all Teamwork Cloud projects, select the **Select all** check-box on the top of the dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the **Select** button.

After completing the above steps, the usage map of the selected resources is displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource Usage Map application structure' space=''] of the web application portal. You can use the filters on the left side of the portal to choose what types of usages should be displayed in the map. Tonavigate through all existing usage maps, select a respective tab on the top of the content pane. You can alsoselect a resource in the map to view its details.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001743 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00081: (2026x Refresh1) Creating roles

- page_id: `304001743`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001743/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+roles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:48.084+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Roles application > (2026x Refresh1) Managing roles
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Role**application allows you to create a role and select the permissions you want the role to have. For example, if you have users who will be responsible for viewing resources and making changes, you can assign the Author role to them. This role is a custom role therefore, you can edit or delete it if need be.

To create a user role

1. On the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_role.PNG'] to open the Create role pane.
2. Type the name of a user role and d esc r i pt i on (opt i o n a l ) .
3. Se l ect at l east o ne pe rmi ssion t o assign t o th e ro l e .
4. Click the Save button.

You can assign the newly created role to one or more users/groups by clicking the **Change** button in the **Role assignments**card. The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing custom roles' space='']** opens and you will be able to add users/groups.

To set a custom scope to the newly created role, you must add resource(s).

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001565 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00082: (2026x Refresh1) Creating users and user groups

- page_id: `304001565`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001565/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+users+and+user+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:32.560+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Users application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating users

To create a new user account

1. From the left side users/user groups filter select Users.
2. In the lower right side of the Users application, click the action button [ATTACHMENT filename='Action_button.png'] and select a Create User button.
3. In the **Create User** pane, fill in the required fields. A text box with a red asterisk (*) means that the information is mandatory. Thus, the **Create** button will be enabled only if you fill in the **Username**, **Password,** and **Confirm password** text boxes.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner to save the user account.

#### TIP: Enable or disable user access

Enable or disable user access

When creating a new user or editing user information, you may select to either enable or disable their access. If you want the user to access the Teamwork Cloud system and carry out the tasks assigned, select **Enable** (selected by default). If you clear the **Enable**, the user's status will be disabled and thus he or she cannot log into Teamwork Cloud Admin and Teamwork Cloud in the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the user account is saved, the **User details**pane will be opened, here you can:

- Edit user details,
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing custom roles' space=''] ,
- Assign a user to a group.

##### Creating user groups

To create new user groups

1. From the left users/user groups filter select **User groups**.
2. In the lower right side of the Users application, click the action button [ATTACHMENT filename='Action_button.png'] and Select a Create user group button, and in Create user group pane fill in the required fields.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner to save the user group.

After the user group is saved, the **Group details**pane will be opened, here you can:

- Edit group details,
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing custom roles' space=''] ,
- Assign users to a group.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Users application structure' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing users and groups' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing users' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Converting users to internal or external' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Copying roles and assignments' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing users' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing user roles and assignments' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003059 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00083: (2026x Refresh1) Cross-cluster resource synchronization

- page_id: `304003059`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003059/2026x+Refresh1+Cross-cluster+resource+synchronization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:27.022+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Administration Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Product administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Until 2026, synced resource revisions were renumbered on target servers. That is, if a revision gap existed in the source server's resource history (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 8, 9), the revisions would be renumbered consecutively on the target server upon sync (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 4, 5). Such gaps could result from deleted resource branches, historical versions, or failed commits. This behavior caused misalignment between source and target resources in sticky Used Project versions.

Starting with 2026x, revision gaps are preserved on the target server, eliminating this misalignment.

To update pre-2026x sync setups:

- Recreate existing pre-2026x sync configurations to continue using them.
- Resync already synced resources after their sync configurations have been recreated.
- If pre-2026x sync configurations are no longer needed and resources from these configurations are not expected to be synced in the future, no action is required.

Please note! You only need to recreate pre-2026x sync configurations if they fail with the following message: ”Some of the remote repository synchronization configurations created before 2026x might be affected by misaligned source-target resource revisions caused by revision gaps in the source models. <…>“

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Be aware that **cross-cluster resource synchronization** is only supported between deployments running the **same major version**. While the **minor version** does not need to match, both deployments must be on the same major version to ensure compatibility and smooth operation.

Teamwork Cloud offers a one-way resource synchronization functionality allowing you to move resources from one Teamwork Cloud server to another and periodically update them. This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. Therefore you can use resource synchronization as an alternative to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration between disconnected teams' space='MDTWRT'] and then updating server projects with local changes. In addition, you can perform a one-time synchronization operation to offload resources to another server for archiving.

- Cross-cluster resource synchronization works only in one direction - from the source to the target server. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.
- The synchronization feature checks project uniqueness by Project ID, Resource ID, and Project Name.

##### Using resource synchronization for collaborating

You can periodically synchronize Teamwork Cloud resources from the source server to the target server so that other users can use synchronized projects in other projects stored on the target server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting up a periodic synchronization operation to share resources that can be reused on the target server.

To use resource sync for collaboration between disconnected Teamwork Cloud sites

1. On the target Teamwork Cloud server, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating remote resource configurations' space=''] to synchronize selected resources from the source to the target server:
  1. Connect to the source Teamwork Cloud server and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
  2. Select the category on the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synced resources to be stored.
  3. Select one of the following synchronization triggers: on every commit, on commit with a specific tag, or manual synchronization
2. Use the synchronized resources in your projects stored on the target server.
3. Update synchronized resources when new project versions become available.

- The source server determines all of the resource's metadata. Therefore, if you change the name of a synchronized project on the source server, the name also changes on the target server on subsequent synchronization iteration. If you change the name of a synchronized project on the target server, the name changes back to the one on the source server during synchronization.
- If you move a synchronized project to a different category on the source server, the project continues synchronizing as before. If you do so on the target server, on subsequent synchronization iteration, the synchronized project returns to its original category specified in the synchronization configuration. However, if a whole category is being synchronized and you remove a resource from that category, this resource is not synchronized anymore. Only the remaining resources in the category are synchronized further.

###### Multi-hop synchronization

In the case of external collaboration between separate entities, direct network connectivity is usually not permitted between the two parties due to IT-level security constraints. As a remedy to this issue, the cross-cluster sync feature can be set up to support the so-called multi-hop deployment topology. That is, a separate Teamwork Cloud instance can be spun up in a neutral environment outside of the involved organizations' network, so the need to connect the two together is eliminated. Instead, resources can be shared and synced between the source and target in multiple hops through the common/neutral Teamwork Cloud repository in the middle.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing resources in multiple hops via a common or neutral environment.

##### Using resource synchronization for resource offloading

If there are dormant recourses in your Teamwork Cloud server, you can move them to another server for archiving to free up space on the main server (e.g., production server).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting up a one-time synchronization operation to move resources to another server.

To use resource sync for moving resources to another Teamwork Cloud server

1. On the target Teamwork Cloud server (e.g., archive server), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating remote resource configurations' space=''] for a single time synchronization operation:
  1. Connect to the source Teamwork Cloud server (e.g., production server) and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
  2. Select the category on the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synced resources to be stored.
  3. Select one the **On every new commit** synchronization trigger.
2. Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Data Manager' space=''] to remove the synced resources from the source server to free up server space.
3. Remove the remote resource configuration created in step 1.

###### Removing the read-only flag from offloaded resources

If you have used resource synchronization to offload resources to a remote server, these resources are read-only and you can no longer edit them. However, there is a way to remove the read-only flag from synchronized resources and make them active again.

Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.

The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again.

To remove the read-only flag from offloaded resources

- Run [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Data Manager' space=''] and execute the following command: bashDJangotrue,--categoryID=,>]]> Exampledatamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 --categoryID=a1d569ea-fe6a-402f-8a25-aef6b932de48

Before running Data Manager, stop Teamwork Cloud services to prevent users from accessing the database.

##### Configuring TLS encrypted connection for cross-cluster synchronization

###### Certificate Authority-signed certificate

If the source server has TLS set up with a Certificate Authority (CA)-signed certificate, no further actions are necessary. The target server will connect to source automatically using TLS.

###### Internal or self-signed certificate

In order to enable TLS encrypted connection with either a self-signed certificate or certificate signed by your organization internally, update the Java cacerts trustores on the target Teamwork Cloud server. Both the Java cacerts used by Teamwork Cloud and Web Application Platform on the target server will have to be updated with the source server's public certificate.

Copy the source Teamwork Cloud server's public certificate to the target server. Use the **keytool import**command to add the source server certificate to the target server's Java cacerts truststore. This command will need to be executed with the Teamwork Cloud Java keytool and Web Application Platform's Java keytool.

```bash
-alias ]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6306586d-8595-428c-9ae9-10e3dce1ee7a"><ac:rich-text-body><p> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Until 2026, synced resource revisions were renumbered on target servers. That is, if a revision gap existed in the source server's resource history (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 8, 9), the revisions would be renumbered consecutively on the target server upon sync (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 4, 5). Such gaps could result from deleted resource branches, historical versions, or failed commits. This behavior caused misalignment between source and target resources in sticky Used Project versions.</span></p><p>Starting with 2026x, revision gaps are preserved on the target server, eliminating this misalignment.</p><p>To update pre-2026x sync setups:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="154251eb-85de-4019-8266-59393fa40859"><p>Recreate existing pre-2026x sync configurations to continue using them.</p></li><li data-uuid="205bdb06-0026-4289-b400-77c174bc4c9c"><p>Resync already synced resources after their sync configurations have been recreated.</p></li><li data-uuid="996ad384-07ee-40b6-ae44-f2ca9d3a91b6"><p>If pre-2026x sync configurations are no longer <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="554ac41f-7eaf-4702-b2ea-b79adb65405c">needed <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">and resources from these configurations are not expected to be synced in the future</span></ac:inline-comment-marker>, no action is required.</p></li></ul><p>Please note! You only need to recreate pre-2026x sync configurations if they fail with the following message: ”Some of the remote repository synchronization configurations created before 2026x might be affected by misaligned source-target resource revisions caused by revision gaps in the source models. &lt;…&gt;“</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cde10e88-3be8-4dba-b27e-f63e0cadfdf3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be aware that <strong>cross-cluster resource synchronization</strong> is only supported between deployments running the <strong>same major version</strong>. While the <strong>minor version</strong> does not need to match, both deployments must be on the same major version to ensure compatibility and smooth operation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Teamwork Cloud offers a one-way resource synchronization functionality allowing you to move resources from one Teamwork Cloud server to another and periodically update them. This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. Therefore you can use resource synchronization as an alternative to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MDTWRT" ri:content-title="Collaboration between disconnected teams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exchanging .mdzip files]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and then updating server projects with local changes. In addition, you can perform a one-time synchronization operation to offload resources to another server for archiving.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b3f2fe0-c487-4409-9d94-1010b4ad64e3"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Cross-cluster resource synchronization works only in one direction - from the source to the target server. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.</li><li>The synchronization feature checks project uniqueness by Project ID, Resource ID, and Project Name.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Using resource synchronization for collaborating</h3><p><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can periodically synchronize Teamwork Cloud resources from the source server to the target server so that other users can use synchronized projects in other projects stored on the target server.</span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:height="179" ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_sync_for_collaboration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cross-cluster resource synchronization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Setting up a periodic synchronization operation to share resources that can be reused on the target server.</span></span></h6><p><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To use resource sync for collaboration between disconnected Teamwork Cloud sites</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">On the target Teamwork Cloud server, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating remote resource configurations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a remote resource configuration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to synchronize selected resources from the source to the target server:</span></span><ol><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Connect to the source Teamwork Cloud server and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.</span></span></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the category on the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synced resources to be stored.</span></span></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select one of the following synchronization triggers: on every commit, on commit with a specific tag, or manual synchronization</span></span></li></ol></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Use the synchronized resources in your projects stored on the target server.</span></span></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Update synchronized resources when new project versions become available.</span></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56febc20-94db-4a00-981e-3a7d34d8b48e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The source server determines all of the resource's metadata. Therefore, if you change the name of a synchronized project on the source server, the name also changes on the target server on subsequent synchronization iteration. If you change the name of a synchronized project on the target server, the name changes back to the one on the source server during synchronization.</li><li>If you move a synchronized project to a different category on the source server, the project continues synchronizing as before. If you do so on the target server, on subsequent synchronization iteration, the synchronized project returns to its original category specified in the synchronization configuration.<br />However, if a whole category is being synchronized and you remove a resource from that category, this resource is not synchronized anymore. Only the remaining resources in the category are synchronized further.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Multi-hop synchronization</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the case of external collaboration between separate entities, direct network connectivity is usually not permitted between the two parties due to IT-level security constraints. As a remedy to this issue, the cross-cluster sync feature can be set up to support the so-called multi-hop deployment topology. That is, a separate Teamwork Cloud instance can be spun up in a neutral environment outside of the involved organizations' network, so the need to connect the two together is eliminated. Instead, resources can be shared and synced between the source and target in multiple hops through the common/neutral Teamwork Cloud repository in the middle.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:width="900"><ri:attachment ri:filename="twc_sync.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cross-cluster resource synchronization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Synchronizing resources in multiple hops via a common or neutral environment.</h6><h3>Using resource synchronization for resource offloading</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If there are dormant recourses in your Teamwork Cloud server, you can move them to another server for archiving to free up space on the main server (e.g., production server).</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:height="175" ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_sync_for_project_archiving.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cross-cluster resource synchronization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Setting up a one-time synchronization operation to move resources to another server.</span></h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To use resource sync for moving resources to another Teamwork Cloud server</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">On the target Teamwork Cloud server (e.g., archive server), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating remote resource configurations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a remote resource configuration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for a single time synchronization operation:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Connect to the source Teamwork Cloud server (e.g., production server) and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the category on the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synced resources to be stored.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select one the <strong>On every new commit</strong> synchronization trigger.</span></li></ol></li><li>Use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Data Manager" /></ac:link> to remove the synced resources from the source server to free up server space.</li><li>Remove the remote resource configuration created in step 1.</li></ol><h4><br />Removing the read-only flag from offloaded resources</h4><p>If you have used resource synchronization to offload resources to a remote server, these resources are read-only and you can no longer edit them. However, there is a way to remove the read-only flag from synchronized resources and make them active again.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55e23054-2732-4c6e-85ef-3afcafee884e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.</p><p>The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To remove the read-only flag from offloaded resources</p><hr /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Run <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Data Manager" /></ac:link> and execute the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8cd2891-5915-4917-8e00-0f89f2567773"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-rrof, --remove-read-only-flag <-resourceID=<value1>,<value2> --categoryID=<value1>,<value2>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="867413c3-3f26-42e0-89de-ba871cd19a90"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>datamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 --categoryID=a1d569ea-fe6a-402f-8a25-aef6b932de48</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d89827fe-93d8-46ba-982a-f2122314d02f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Before running Data Manager, stop Teamwork Cloud services to prevent users from accessing the database.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Configuring TLS encrypted connection for cross-cluster synchronization</span></h3><h4 style="text-align: left;">Certificate Authority-signed certificate</h4><p style="text-align: left;">If the source server has TLS set up with a Certificate Authority (CA)-signed certificate, no further actions are necessary. The target server will connect to source automatically using TLS.</p><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Internal or self-signed certificate</span></h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In order to enable TLS encrypted connection with either a self-signed certificate or certificate signed by your organization internally, update the Java cacerts trustores on the target Teamwork Cloud server. Both the Java cacerts used by Teamwork Cloud and Web Application Platform on the target server will have to be updated with the source server's public certificate. </span></p><p style="text-align: left;">Copy the source Teamwork Cloud server's public certificate to the target server. Use the <strong>keytool import </strong>command to add the source server certificate to the target server's Java cacerts truststore. This command will need to be executed with the Teamwork Cloud Java keytool and Web Application Platform's Java keytool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f7a9bad-762e-4c27-beb8-666dfdb54804"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[keytool -import -cacerts -file <source server cert> -alias <unique alias>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001516 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00084: (2026x Refresh1) Customizing PDF/HTML templates

- page_id: `304001516`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001516/2026x+Refresh1+Customizing+PDF+HTML+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:29.524+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Sharing and exporting
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows customizing the appearance of the PDF or HTML files generated from Cameo Collaborator documents. You can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document, make any desired changes, then upload the modified template back to the document. Moreover, a single document has the capability to hold multiple PDF/HTML templates enabling you to generate different outputs from the same document.

In a custom document template, you can alter the generated document's cover page, header, footer, and font or add a custom logo to the document.

For more information, refer to the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows customizing the appearance of the PDF or HTML files generated from Cameo Collaborator documents. You can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document, make any desired changes, then upload the modified template back to the document. Moreover, a single document has the capability to hold multiple PDF/HTML templates enabling you to generate different outputs from the same document.</p><p>In a custom document template, you can alter the generated document's cover page, header, footer, and font or add a custom logo to the document.</p><p>For more information, refer to the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002688 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00085: (2026x Refresh1) Customizing the appearance of a project portal

- page_id: `304002688`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002688/2026x+Refresh1+Customizing+the+appearance+of+a+project+portal
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:01.354+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Publishing documents > (2026x Refresh1) Document templates > (2026x Refresh1) Working with custom document templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the appearance of a published project by customizing the template you plan to use for publishing. The best practice is to save an existing predefined template with a different name, and then make changes to the newly created template file. After creating a new template file, you need to use it in the project you intend to publish. There is the option to change the default color of the title bar, document header and text, to add or remove the search button, and other style options. The procedure bellow describes how to modify the *Entire Model* template (stored as *Cameo Collaborator Library.mdzip)*, but you can also modify other predefined templates the same way.

To customize the appearance a project portal

1. Go to the modelLibraries folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and open the Cameo Collaborator template you want to use for publishing as a separate project.
2. Save the project in the same directory using a different name (e.g., Custom template.mdzip ).
3. When you receive the warning not to edit standard/system profiles and libraries, click Continue .
4. Open a View Diagram of the template and double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype to open its properties Specification window.
5. Specify the values of the following properties:
  - 
    - Primary Color Palette - defines the color of the app bar in the project portal.
    - Secondary Color Palette - defines the color of the main portal icons, e.g., icons for creating and saving a comment.
6. Click Close and save the project.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The sample of a project portal showing the colors that can be customized.

##### Setting the default scope for publishing

In the custom template, you can set the default scope for publishing. When the default scope is set, you don't need to specify it while publishing the project. In this case, the default scope is published even if you specify another scope in the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog.

To set the default scope for publishing

1. If you haven't done so already, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MDTWRT'] you intend to publish.
2. Open the View Diagram of the custom template.
3. In the Containment tree, select the package you want to set as the default scope for publishing (e.g. Categorization ) and drag it onto the diagram pane.
4. Draw the «Expose» dependency from the element with the «Document» stereotype to the package. [ATTACHMENT filename='setting_scope.png'] The figure displays the View Diagram of the Entire Model template with a default scope that is specified through the «Expose» dependency.
5. If you want to add more packages to the default scope, repeat steps 3 and 4.
6. Save the project.
7. Select the custom template in the Cameo Collaborator Publisher dialog when you publish the project to Cameo Collaborator.

##### Setting the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document

The home page of a published project is the diagram that opens by default after the project portal is loaded. It can be set directly in the model that you intend to publish.

To more easily set the home page of your Cameo Collaborator document, see *Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip*file provided with Cameo Collaborator Publisher. The *2. Diagrams in a Package Template*sample of this fileshows you how to create a document displaying all diagrams from a specific package with one diagram set as the home page.

To set the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document

1. If you use a default template to publish Cameo Collaborator documents, proceed to step 2. If you use a custom template do the following:
  1. Open the custom template you intend to use for publishing.
  2. Open the Specification window of the View element which will be used to display the diagram you want to set as the home page.
  3. Click the specification cell of the Active Element property and in the drop-down list select the FindLandingPage value.
  4. Set the **Active Landing Page** property to *true*. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Using multiple ViewsIf your custom template has multiple Views, set the **Active Landing Page** for one View only.
  5. Close the Specification window and save the template.
2. Open the model you intend to publish.
3. Make sure that the Document View Collaborator Profile is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MDTWRT'] . You can find it in the <intall.root>\profiles directory.
4. In the model browser, right-click the diagram you want to display as the home page of the project portal and select Home Page . [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_home_page.png']

After completing the above steps, the «HomePage» stereotype is applied to the selected diagram. When you publish your model, a diagram with this stereotype is automatically displayed as the home page of a project portal.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>You can customize the appearance of a published project by customizing the template you plan to use for publishing. The best practice is to save an existing predefined template with a different name, and then make changes to the newly created template file.</span> After creating a new template file, you need to use it in the project you intend to publish. There is the option to change the default color of the title bar, document header and text, to add or remove the search button, and other style options. The procedure bellow describes how to modify the <em>Entire Model</em> template (stored as <em>Cameo Collaborator Library.mdzip)</em>, but you can also modify other predefined templates the same way.</p><p><br /></p><p>To customize the appearance a project portal</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>modelLibraries</em> folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and open the Cameo Collaborator template you want to use for publishing as a separate project.</li><li>Save the project in the same directory using a different name (e.g., <em>Custom template.mdzip</em>).</li><li>When you receive the warning not to edit standard/system profiles and libraries, click <strong>Continue</strong>.</li><li>Open a View Diagram of the template and double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype to open its properties Specification window.</li><li>Specify the values of the following properties:<br /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Primary Color Palette</strong> - defines the color of the app bar in the project portal.</li><li><strong>Secondary Color Palette</strong> - defines the color of the main portal icons, e.g., icons for creating and saving a comment.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong> and save the project.</li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="customizing_project_portal.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing the appearance of a project portal" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">The sample of a project portal showing the colors that can be customized.</span></h6><h3>Setting the default scope for publishing</h3><p>In the custom template, you can set the default scope for publishing. When the default scope is set, you don't need to specify it while publishing the project. In this case, the default scope is published even if you specify another scope in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog. </p><p><br /></p><p>To set the default scope for publishing</p><hr /><ol><li>If you haven't done so already, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MDTWRT" ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use your custom template in the project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you intend to publish.</li><li>Open the View Diagram of the custom template.</li><li>In the Containment tree, select the package you want to set as the default scope for publishing (e.g. <em>Categorization</em>) and drag it onto the diagram pane.</li><li>Draw the «Expose» dependency from the element with the «Document» stereotype to the package.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="setting_scope.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing the appearance of a project portal" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The figure displays the View Diagram of the <em>Entire Model</em> template with a default scope that is specified through the «Expose» dependency.<br /><br /></li><li>If you want to add more packages to the default scope, repeat steps 3 and 4.</li><li>Save the project.</li><li>Select the custom template in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog when you publish the project to Cameo Collaborator.</li></ol><h3><br />Setting the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document</h3><p>The home page of a published project is the diagram that opens by default after the project portal is loaded. It can be set directly in the model that you intend to publish.<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c37cdaf3-a80d-4ce3-bc38-1126c5430c38"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To more easily set the home page of your Cameo Collaborator document, see <em>Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip </em>file provided with Cameo Collaborator Publisher. The <em>2. Diagrams in a Package Template </em>sample of this file </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">shows you how to create a document displaying all diagrams from a specific package with one diagram set as the home page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To set the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document</p><hr /><ol><li>If you use a default template to publish Cameo Collaborator documents, proceed to step 2. If you use a custom template do the following:<ol><li>Open the custom template you intend to use for publishing.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the View element which will be used to display the diagram you want to set as the home page.</li><li>Click the specification cell of the <strong>Active Element</strong> property and in the drop-down list select the <strong>FindLandingPage</strong> value.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Set the <strong>Active Landing Page</strong> property to <em>true</em>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_window_of_a_view.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing the appearance of a project portal" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f92a329-8262-4937-9183-39f87d27ac55"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Using multiple Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your custom template has multiple Views, set the <strong>Active Landing Page</strong> for one View only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Close the Specification window and save the template.</li></ol></li><li>Open the model you intend to publish.</li><li>Make sure that the Document View Collaborator Profile is <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MDTWRT" ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[used in your model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can find it in the <em>&lt;intall.root&gt;\profiles</em> directory.</li><li>In the model browser, right-click the diagram you want to display as the home page of the project portal and select <strong>Home Page</strong>. <br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_home_page.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing the appearance of a project portal" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p>After completing the above steps, the «HomePage» stereotype is applied to the selected diagram. When you publish your model, a diagram with this stereotype is automatically displayed as the home page of a project portal.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003051 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00086: (2026x Refresh1) Data Manager

- page_id: `304003051`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003051/2026x+Refresh1+Data+Manager
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:26.267+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Administration Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Product administration
- labels: ['reset-admin-password', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Data Manager is a Java utility for interfacing directly with the Cassandra database. The Data Manager package can be found in the *Utilities*directory of your server installation, and as a portable zip package in the 3DS installer distribution.

Data Manager is used to make permanent and irrecoverable modifications to the Cassandra database. A full database backup should always be performed before making any modifications to the database.

Always use the Data Manager package included with your version of falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc distribution.

Running Data Manager

To run Data Manager

1. Stop falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc services to prevent users from accessing the database.
2. Ensure Cassandra is up and running: use the nodetool status command to verify the database status is Up and Normal .
3. Run a Data Manager command. If Data Manager is executed for the first time on the system, you will be prompted to provide the path to Java. Use the same version of Java as falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc. Execute with the *-help*option to ensure Data Manager is working: bashDJangotrue
4. After finishing all Data Manager steps, start falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc services to resume user access.

##### Data Manager configuration

Data Manager has its own configuration files, located in the Data Manager installation path, under *configuration*.

###### Database access

For best performance, run Data Manager on the Cassandra server. By default, Data Manager will access the database via Localhost. If you need to run Data Manager remotely from the Cassandra server, modify the**contact-points**field for **datastax-java-driver******in application.conf.

###### Cassandra Authentication

Data Manager will connect to Cassandra on localhost using anonymous authentication. If your Cassandra node is configured to bind to a specific IP address (by default, port 9042 binds to all interfaces), or if you have configured Cassandra to use an authenticator other than *AllowAllAuthenticator*, you will need to modify the**auth-provider******fields in**application.conf accordingly.

###### Logs

Data Manager will append to a detailed log file on each execution. The datamanager.log file is stored in the logs directory of the Data Manager installation path. You can change the location of the log file and the level of logging in logback.xml.

##### Data Manager commands

The following table outlines the commands you can use in Data Manager to manage your data.

| Server tool command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| -help | Displays Help information. |
| -clbl, --clear-liquibase-locks | Clears liquibase locks after an interrupted liquibase migration. |
| -rbs, --recalculate-blob-size | Recalculates blob size of the resources. This should be used when the blob size is too large due to an overly big resource.This process can take a while, depending on the size of the database. |
| -dk, --drop-keyspace <keyspace=value> --silent-drop | Drops persistent keyspace to free up the storage space.Add --silent-drop to run the command silently, i.e., without requesting user permission.You need to clear the removed keyspace snapshots using the nodetool utility command:]]> |
| -dr,--delete-resource <resource ID> | Deletes the given resource(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple resource IDs using space as a separator.If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server. |
| -drb,--delete-branch <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2> includeChildBranch=<true/false>> | Deletes the given branch(es) and all of its related information. Specify multiple branch ID using a comma as a separator.includeChildBranch has two values: true and false. When the includeChildBranch value is set to false, and the branch has child branches, this branch will not be deleted.When the includeChildBranch value is set to true, the branch including its child branches will be deleted.This command does not work for the branches of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its branches. |
| -drd,--delete-marked-deleted-resources | Deletes all resources that were marked as deleted.If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server. |
| -drr,--delete-revision <resourceID=value revision=<revision1,revision2> includeChildRevision=<true/false>> | Deletes the given revision(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple revisions using a comma as a separator.includeChildRevision has two values: true and false. When the includeChildRevision value is set to false, and the revision is not the latest, it will not be deleted.When the includeChildRevision value is set to true, the upward revisions and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.This command does not work for the revisions of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its revisions. |
| -du,--delete-user <username> | Deletes the given username(s) and all of its related information.To specify multiple usernames, use comma as a separator.If the username consists of two words (for example, dan smith) add double quotation marks (" ") before and after the username:text |
| -rrof, --remove-read-only-flag <-resourceID=<value1>,<value2> --categoryID=<value1>,<value2>> | Removes the read-only flag from a synchronized resource or category in the target server. Specify multiple resources or categories using a comma as a separator, for example:Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again. |
| -lb,--list-branch <resourceID=value> | Lists all branches of the given resource. |
| -lc,--list-cluster-ids | Lists previous cluster IDs and the current one. |
| -sgdb,--set-global-deny-edit-branch <resourceID=<value> branchID=<value> globalDenyEdit=<true/false>> | Sets whether the given branch should be denied to edit by global. |
| -sgdr,--set-global-deny-edit-resource <resourceID=<value1>,<value2> globalDenyEdit=<true/false>> | Sets whether the given resources should be denied to edit by global.Exampledatamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 |
| -lgdr,--list-global-deny-edit-resource <--resourceID=<value1>,<value2>> | Lists the status of deny edit by global of the given resource. |
| -lgdb,--list-global-deny-edit-branch <--resourceID=<value1>,<value2>> | Lists the status of deny edit by global for all branches of the given resource. |
| -lcat,--list-categories | Lists all the categories and their IDs. |
| -li,--list-index | Lists all available data that have indexes. |
| -lr,--list-resources | Lists all available resources. |
| -lrd,--list-marked-deleted-resources | Lists all resources that are marked as deleted. |
| -lrv,--list-revision <resourceID=value branchID=value> | Lists all revisions in the given branch. |
| -lu,--list-users <--no-info> | Lists all usernames and their information, sorted by name.You can add --no-info to show only the usernames. |
| -ram,--reset-administrator | Resets admin password, status, and permissions.If there is no Administrator account, it will be created.If the Administrator account exists in the database:If the account is for an external user, it will be reset to an internal user with a new password. This user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.If the account is an internal user, only a new password will be generated, and the user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.The default roles of the Teamwork Cloud Admin will be restored to make sure that this account can fully operate as the default administrator. |
| -rc,--recreate-cluster-id | Recreates a cluster ID. |
| -ri,--rebuild-index <data=value1,value2> | Rebuilds all indexes for the given data and uses the data=* option to rebuild all indexes of all available data. |
| -urd,--unmark-deleted-resources <resource ID> | Unmarks the given resource(s) previously marked as deleted. Specify multiple resource ID using space as a separator. |
| -vb,--validate-blob <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value --readAllWithCQL --createCompareOfThriftAndCQL> | Validates blob information. |
| -ve,--validate-eobject <resourceID=value1,value2 saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates the eObject information of a specific resource ID and uses the resourceID=* option to validate all resources. |
| -version | Displays the version of Data Manager. |
| -vi,--verify-index <data=value1,value2> | Verifies all indexes for the given data and uses the data=* option to verify all indexes of all available data. |
| -vr,--validate-rbac <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates a user, user group, role, and role assignment information. |
| -vrc,--validate-resource-commit <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates a metamodel, branch, and committed information. |
| -col,--cleanup-object-locks | Removes released object locks (that are no longer used) from the database.How to safely execute this command:Before performing object lock cleanup make sure that all Cassandra nodes are running and accessible.After Data Manager finishes cleaning locks, run the command in a separate terminal nodetool repair which you can find in the Cassandra/bin directory. Once nodetool repair is done, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.DataManager will ask to run command in a separate terminal nodetool compact. After running the command in nodetool compact terminal, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed. |
| -rcc,--rebuild-change-count <--resourceID=<resourceID1>,<resourceID2>> | Rebuilds the commit change count of all or the selected resources.The process may take a while depending on the commit and change count.You can specify multiple revisions using commas as separators.Example:datamanager -rccdatamanager -rcc resourceID=956ec67b-e79f-4b51-9197-2f041265b637datamanager -rcc resourceID=0c81b8e6-71ef-4dc6-b2f3-201626da1feb,5126b26c-275e-472d-adb5-8abd0cb9492c |
| -crb,--clean-resource-blobs <--resourceID=<resourceID1>,<resourceID2>> | Cleans resource metadata and obsolete local cache blobs. If the optional resourceID argument is not set, all the listed resource blobs (for archiving resources) will be cleaned. |
| -cu,--change-username <username=value newUsername=value> | Changes the given username and generates a new password. For usernames containing a space, put double quotes, ", around the username. Example:datamanager -cu username="John Old" newUsername="Andrew New" |
| -ll,--list-ldap | Lists all LDAP realm information. |
| -rlu, --remap-ldap-users | Remaps LDAP user realms to another existing LDAP realm.Exampledatamanager -rlu source-realm=b8ea2561-b952-4588-8fff-269f49f0764f target-realm=4d1bdd4b-8ea7-4b4c-bfdc-7ee48ab9be3b |
| -vlu,--validate-ldap-users <saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates users which have an LDAP realm ID pointing to a non-existing LDAP configuration. |
| -udb,--unmark-deleted-branches <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2>> | Unmarks the given branches that were previously marked as deleted. |

The **delete** command to delete resources and users works as follows.

###### Deleting selected resource

```bash
...]]>
```

These commands delete a selected resource and all of the following resource-related information:

- Tags information of the resource.
- Category information (the resource will be removed from all categories).
- Branch information.
- Commit information.
- Role assignment information (role assignment information related to the resource will be removed).
- Locking information.

###### Deleting selected user

```bash
, ...]]>
```

This command deletes a selected user and all of the following user-related information:

- Role assignment information (all role assignment information belonging to the user will be removed).
- Session (the existing user session will be removed).
- Lock information (model elements in projects locked by the user will be unlocked).
- User group information (the user will be removed from all user groups).

#### NOTE: Notes on deleting usernames

Notes on deleting usernames

- For usernames consisting of two words (such as *dan smith*), add double quotation marks (" ") before and after the username, as shown: text
- In the modeling tool's history panels, the word deleted will appear in brackets next to the deleted users.

###### Deleting selected branch

```bash
, includeChildBranch=>]]>
```

The branch-related information which will be removed together with the branch includes:

- Tags information of each revision in the branch
- Object information created/modified at each revision in the branch
- Commit information of each revision in the branch
- Locking information of each object locked in the branch
- Branch information of the branch
- If the branch has child branches, depending on the includeChildBranch value, the branch will be deleted or not:
  - When the includeChildBranch value is set to False and the branch has child branches, then this branch will not be deleted.
  - When the includeChildBranch value is set to True, then even if the branch has child branches it will be deleted.

###### Deleting revisions

```bash
includeChildRevision=>]]>
```

The revision-related information which will be removed together with the revision includes:

- Tags information of the revision
- Object information created/modified at the revision
- Commit information of the revision
- Locking information of objects whose id's do not exist anymore in the branch that the revision belongs to
- Branch information of the branch if the revision was the first revision of the branch
- If a selected revision is not the latest, depending on the includeChildRevision value, the revision will be deleted or not:
  - When the includeChildRevision value is set to False, and the revision is not the latest, then it will not be deleted.
  - When the includeChildRevision value is set to T rue, then revisions upward and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
