You can change the location of the log file and the level of logging in logback.xml.</span></p><h3>Data Manager commands</h3><p>The following table outlines the commands you can use in Data Manager to manage your data. </p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Server tool command</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-help</p></td><td>Displays Help information.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-clbl, --clear-liquibase-locks</p></td><td>Clears liquibase locks after an interrupted liquibase migration.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-rbs, --recalculate-blob-size</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Recalculates blob size of the resources. This should be used when the blob size is too large due to an overly big resource.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd1424db-54b5-492f-a9b5-f407e645da84"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This process can take a while, depending on the size of the database.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-dk, --drop-keyspace &lt;keyspace=value&gt; --silent-drop</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drops persistent keyspace to free up the storage space.</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="4a756e43-4e93-44e0-a349-b20d669aa66c">Add <strong>--silent-drop </strong>to run the command silently, i.e., without requesting user permission.</li><li data-uuid="551d5df3-f546-4284-a99f-8694d0b176e0"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>You need to clear the removed keyspace snapshots using the <strong>nodetool</strong> utility command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8c312e9-91b9-486b-8369-34c5278b1154"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ nodetool clearsnapshot -- <keyspace>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-dr,--delete-resource &lt;resource ID&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given resource(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple resource IDs using space as a separator.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a3b9b41-1672-4a1d-90f7-c850b049f90a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-drb,--delete-branch &lt;resourceID=value branchID=&lt;ID1&gt;,&lt;ID2&gt; includeChildBranch=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given branch(es) and all of its related information. Specify multiple branch ID using a comma as a separator.</p><p><strong>includeChildBranch</strong> has two values: <strong>true</strong> and <strong>false</strong><em>. </em></p><ul><li data-uuid="c65a3c67-e919-46bb-a2df-e95edf357bbb">When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <strong>false</strong>, and<em> </em>the branch has child branches, this branch will not be deleted.</li><li data-uuid="faf58974-2f78-45fe-945e-5bf8c7307123">When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <strong>true</strong><em>, </em>the branch including its child branches will be deleted.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8d1bcbc-2d3c-48ad-8f9f-00ddb873487f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command does not work for the branches of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its branches.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-drd,--delete-marked-deleted-resources</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes all resources that were marked as deleted.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22febd0d-99ad-4f01-af62-a1f821385f84"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-drr,--delete-revision &lt;resourceID=value revision=&lt;revision1,revision2&gt; includeChildRevision=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given revision(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple revisions using a comma as a separator.</p><p><strong>includeChildRevision</strong> has two values: <strong>true</strong> and <strong>false</strong><em>. </em></p><ul><li data-uuid="d71b539f-2b50-483f-be22-2cd9fa5345c8">When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <strong>false</strong><em>, </em>and the revision is not the latest, it will not be deleted.</li><li data-uuid="bc792988-9c54-4e25-af7c-58b809c677fc">When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <strong>true</strong><em>,</em> the upward revisions and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a8a1990-5cc3-4fae-bcea-8b43d2cdd8d4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command does not work for the revisions of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its revisions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-du,--delete-user &lt;username&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given username(s) and all of its related information.</p><ul><li data-uuid="f222f712-0958-438d-9d53-d44e3e5b8723">To specify multiple usernames, use comma as a separator.</li><li data-uuid="0bae4fee-fd86-4fe9-9eb7-fef02684d49f"><p class="auto-cursor-target">If the username consists of two words (for example, <em>dan smith</em>) add double quotation marks (&quot; &quot;) before and after the username:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0302fbce-a9ca-4057-9ea5-680c8447cf0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   --delete-user "dan smith" 
   -du "dan smith"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td>-rrof, --remove-read-only-flag &lt;-resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt; --categoryID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Removes the read-only flag from a synchronized resource or category in the target server. Specify multiple resources or categories using a comma as a separator, for example:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="502cc4e7-3c14-41c1-a0bf-cdaf58b591d0"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[datamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 --categoryID=a1d569ea-fe6a-402f-8a25-aef6b932de48]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55e23054-2732-4c6e-85ef-3afcafee884e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.</p><p>The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lb,--list-branch &lt;resourceID=value&gt;</p></td><td><p>Lists all branches of the given resource.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lc,--list-cluster-ids</p></td><td>Lists previous cluster IDs and the current one.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-sgdb,--set-global-deny-edit-branch &lt;resourceID=&lt;value&gt; branchID=&lt;value&gt; globalDenyEdit=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Sets whether the given branch should be denied to edit by global.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-sgdr,--set-global-deny-edit-resource &lt;resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt; globalDenyEdit=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Sets whether the given resources should be denied to edit by global.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f66c2956-ce83-46d5-8347-1cc052f3f753"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>datamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lgdr,--list-global-deny-edit-resource &lt;--resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Lists the status of deny edit by global of the given resource.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lgdb,--list-global-deny-edit-branch &lt;--resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Lists the status of deny edit by global for all branches of the given resource.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lcat,--list-categories</p></td><td>Lists all the categories and their IDs.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-li,--list-index</p></td><td>Lists all available data that have indexes.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lr,--list-resources</p></td><td>Lists all available resources.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lrd,--list-marked-deleted-resources</p></td><td>Lists all resources that are marked as deleted.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lrv,--list-revision &lt;resourceID=value branchID=value&gt;</p></td><td>Lists all revisions in the given branch.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lu,--list-users &lt;--no-info&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Lists all usernames and their information, sorted by name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7236b80d-5f13-4722-89a9-f1395659fa85"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can add <strong>--no-info</strong> to show only the usernames.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-ram,--reset-administrator</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Resets admin password, status, and permissions.</p><ul><li data-uuid="5aaaa701-b653-4eb9-b546-81287a49fca2">If there is no Administrator account, it will be created.</li><li data-uuid="a0fcc87f-2fd2-478c-93f2-ba3d619bb669">If the Administrator account exists in the database:</li></ul><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="e90bb653-8b8b-4efe-8b0d-0517faf9dc68"><ul><li>If the account is for an external user, it will be reset to an internal user with a new password. This user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.</li><li>If the account is an internal user, only a new password will be generated, and the user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.</li></ul></li></ul><ul class="alternate"><li data-uuid="68678fce-d15a-405c-89a7-7d074430e38c">The default roles of the Teamwork Cloud Admin will be restored to make sure that this account can fully operate as the default administrator.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-rc,--recreate-cluster-id</p></td><td><p>Recreates a cluster ID.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-ri,--rebuild-index &lt;data=value1,value2&gt;</p></td><td><p>Rebuilds all indexes for the given data and uses the <strong>data=*</strong> option to rebuild all indexes of all available data.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-urd,--unmark-deleted-resources &lt;resource ID&gt;</p></td><td><p>Unmarks the given resource(s) previously marked as deleted. Specify multiple resource ID using space as a separator.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-vb,--validate-blob &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value --readAllWithCQL --createCompareOfThriftAndCQL&gt;</p></td><td>Validates blob information.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-ve,--validate-eobject &lt;resourceID=value1,value2 saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates the eObject information of a specific resource ID and uses the <strong>resourceID=*</strong> option to validate all resources.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-version</p></td><td>Displays the version of Data Manager.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-vi,--verify-index &lt;data=value1,value2&gt;</p></td><td><p>Verifies all indexes for the given data and uses the <strong>data=*</strong> option to verify all indexes of all available data.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-vr,--validate-rbac &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates a user, user group, role, and role assignment information.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-vrc,--validate-resource-commit &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates a metamodel, branch, and committed information.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>-col,--cleanup-object-locks</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Removes released object locks (that are no longer used) from the database.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f91ddea-a561-4aad-b051-6f88b57e6abf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>How to safely execute this command:</p><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="53c31d38-bd57-402a-a6cf-030fecd30ff3">Before performing object lock cleanup make sure that all Cassandra nodes are running and accessible.</li><li data-uuid="032e4c3f-9d6d-4f4e-b1ac-d258765006f8">After Data Manager finishes cleaning locks, run the command in a separate terminal <strong>nodetool repair</strong> which you can find in the <em>Cassandra/bin</em> directory. Once <strong>nodetool repair </strong>is done, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.</li><li data-uuid="d099e136-1329-47fb-985f-619a7195fb61">DataManager will ask to run command in a separate terminal <strong>nodetool compact</strong>. After running the command in <strong>nodetool compact </strong>terminal, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-rcc,--rebuild-change-count &lt;--resourceID=&lt;resourceID1&gt;,&lt;resourceID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Rebuilds the commit change count of all or the selected resources.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e9397cb-2a34-4dc4-9979-ab9aa5e39b0d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The process may take a while depending on the commit and change count.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40487135-04e4-41b7-8e01-40229da20b94"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify multiple revisions using commas as separators.</p><p>Example:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b3fa7638-9929-4374-b8d1-a1da01ba12c1">datamanager -rcc</li><li data-uuid="c2765c9d-39d6-4615-beb7-029db3ffc4ed">datamanager -rcc resourceID=956ec67b-e79f-4b51-9197-2f041265b637</li><li data-uuid="e58b449a-1722-4d42-90c5-0d69e8faead6"><span>datamanager -rcc resourceID=0c81b8e6-71ef-4dc6-b2f3-201626da1feb,5126b26c-275e-472d-adb5-8abd0cb9492c</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-crb,--clean-resource-blobs &lt;--resourceID=&lt;resourceID1&gt;,&lt;resourceID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><p>Cleans resource metadata and obsolete local cache blobs. If the optional <strong>resourceID</strong> argument is not set, all the listed resource blobs (for archiving resources) will be cleaned.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-cu,--change-username &lt;username=value newUsername=value&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Changes the given username and generates a new password.<br />                                                                                                             </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e85862fb-7945-425b-a8d6-70450e7e40d8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For usernames containing a space, put double quotes, &quot;, around the username. Example:</p><p>datamanager -cu username=&quot;John Old&quot; newUsername=&quot;Andrew New&quot;</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-ll,--list-ldap</p></td><td><p>Lists all LDAP realm information.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-rlu, --remap-ldap-users</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Remaps LDAP user realms to another existing LDAP realm.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d17ead5-c338-4444-8707-2824840f8d12"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>datamanager -rlu source-realm=b8ea2561-b952-4588-8fff-269f49f0764f target-realm=4d1bdd4b-8ea7-4b4c-bfdc-7ee48ab9be3b</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-vlu,--validate-ldap-users &lt;saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td><p>Validates users which have an LDAP realm ID pointing to a non-existing LDAP configuration.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-udb,--unmark-deleted-branches &lt;resourceID=value branchID=&lt;ID1&gt;,&lt;ID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><p>Unmarks the given branches that were previously marked as deleted.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br />The <strong>delete</strong> command to delete resources and users works as follows.</p><h4>Deleting selected resource </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abe1732a-965d-4141-8318-eb14b852a461"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-dr,--delete-resource <resource ID> <resource ID> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>These commands delete a selected resource and all of the following resource-related information:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="ba3b58c4-fadb-4c74-9178-de6cbecb8cf8">Tags information of the resource. </li><li data-uuid="b3e78caa-1f32-4eb8-97e1-4c21cdc99b3d">Category information (the resource will be removed from all categories). </li><li data-uuid="1c361c5e-cad6-4c74-9596-2a8daffe9d30">Branch information. </li><li data-uuid="26a105b8-56da-4c9f-840a-d1e60133a63d">Commit information. </li><li data-uuid="2b085dc0-6b3c-4258-a21c-75ec2e240ca7">Role assignment information (role assignment information related to the resource will be removed). </li><li data-uuid="3d13708f-2ac3-46b8-8f8a-36ab431cb9f9">Locking information.</li></ul><h4>Deleting selected user </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d89fa469-6680-4099-8b73-5b9199426522"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-du,--delete-user <username>,<username> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>This command deletes a selected user and all of the following user-related information:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="58456664-1650-48df-9084-c063c3977859">Role assignment information (all role assignment information belonging to the user will be removed).</li><li data-uuid="7f753d3f-a80c-45d0-a3c4-eae6a816f757">Session (the existing user session will be removed).</li><li data-uuid="89955750-7fa2-41b7-a36e-5a316e176c10">Lock information (model elements in projects locked by the user will be unlocked).</li><li data-uuid="e2a96887-2831-4e09-8aa0-c76f260c37c2">User group information (the user will be removed from all user groups).<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdb4f757-4a39-4a2e-b0bc-bd228aefce15"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Notes on deleting usernames</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="9450b289-d66a-49ac-993d-10f0472f6ac3"><p class="auto-cursor-target">For usernames consisting of two words (such as <em>dan smith</em>), add double quotation marks (&quot; &quot;) before and after the username, as shown:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac17643d-503e-4234-bc3c-db295661ad2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   --delete-user "dan smith" 
   -du "dan smith"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="9d945638-3e97-4bd2-811a-56f518c436c1">In the modeling tool's history panels, the word <em>deleted</em> will appear in brackets next to the deleted users.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Deleting selected branch </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82a7bd5f-fc36-4868-bcb6-59a08022d27f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-drb,--delete-branch <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2> includeChildBranch=<true/false>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>The branch-related information which will be removed together with the branch includes:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="bc9e1106-6beb-4ce9-9ab1-63a814b848ad">Tags information of each revision in the branch</li><li data-uuid="53f9c91a-d297-4ed6-a5e9-593c3d34720b">Object information created/modified at each revision in the branch</li><li data-uuid="f68fd48d-b039-45e9-91a4-b96cd8e458f4">Commit information of each revision in the branch</li><li data-uuid="a01474ba-8d8d-46a4-9405-9cdab1cc30de">Locking information of each object locked in the branch</li><li data-uuid="5fd1ae22-c411-4c4a-a50c-a015b29d5cf4">Branch information of the branch</li><li data-uuid="a657bc41-b7b7-49c5-a54f-58596ca58441">If the branch has child branches, depending on the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong><strong> </strong>value, the branch will be deleted or not: <em> </em><br /><ul><li>When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <em>False </em>and<em> </em>the branch has child branches, then this branch will not be deleted.</li><li>When the <strong>includeChildBranch </strong>value is set to <em>True, </em>then even if the branch has child branches it will be deleted.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Deleting revisions </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7deaa30e-49cc-4711-9f3b-1d2227ceb92f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-drr,--delete-revision <resourceID=value revision=<revision1,revision2> includeChildRevision=<true/false>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>The revision-related information which will be removed together with the revision includes:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="a0b62886-0a41-4c33-9b2a-903989fbb7bc">Tags information of the revision</li><li data-uuid="3d2ba35b-f0c8-4e93-9a95-db8a402e7616">Object information created/modified at the revision</li><li data-uuid="fd6deb2c-7f40-4260-bfdc-a88989c0bff1">Commit information of the revision</li><li data-uuid="a7f04ab4-3309-48a1-9dd2-335e1f9093b0">Locking information of objects whose id's do not exist anymore in the branch that the revision belongs to</li><li data-uuid="ada3e51b-6213-4246-bfcc-d996bf60bfef">Branch information of the branch if the revision was the first revision of the branch</li><li data-uuid="b258902e-b8e2-46b2-89a5-a9e19955bf93">If a selected revision is not the latest, depending on the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong><strong> </strong>value, the revision will be deleted or not: <ul><li>When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <em>False, </em>and the revision is not the latest, then it will not be deleted.</li><li>When the <strong>includeChildRevision </strong>value is set to <em>T</em><em>rue,</em> then<em> </em>revisions upward and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.</li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003282 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00087: (2026x Refresh1) Data markings support

- page_id: `304003282`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003282/2026x+Refresh1+Data+markings+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:49:18.609+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Other features
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings created using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Markings and Classification Plugin' space='DMPTWRT'] to ensure consistency between documents and related Teamwork Cloud projects.The Data Markings and Classification Plugin allows you to mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classificationor sensitivity. All the data markings created in the modeling tool, including diagram and table banners, are also visible in a Cameo Collaborator document.

For information on how to use data markings to classify resources, categories, users, and user groups in Teamwork Cloud, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin' space=''].

The figure below demonstrates how data markings are displayed in parentheses next to element names in the navigation and content panes of a Cameo Collaborator document.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings next to element names in a Cameo Collaborator document.

The following figure shows data markings displayed on element shapes and in a diagram banner.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings on diagram shapes and in a diagram banner.

Data markings are supported in tables as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings displayed in a table banner.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings created using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMPTWRT" ri:content-title="Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /></ac:link> to ensure consistency between documents and related Teamwork Cloud projects. </span>The Data Markings and Classification Plugin allows you to mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classification<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">or sensitivity</span>. All the data markings created in the modeling tool, including diagram and table banners, are also visible in a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81888686-7ea9-4d43-8e16-47dacf2e4a5e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information on <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">how to use data markings to classify resources, categories, users, and user groups in Teamwork Cloud, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The figure below demonstrates how data markings are displayed in parentheses next to element names in the navigation and content panes of a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_element_names.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Data markings support" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings next to element names in a Cameo Collaborator document.</h6><p><br /></p><p>The following figure shows data markings displayed on element shapes and in a diagram banner.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_diagrams.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Data markings support" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings on diagram shapes and in a diagram banner.</h6><p>Data markings are supported in tables as well.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_tables.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Data markings support" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings displayed in a table banner.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002313 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00088: (2026x Refresh1) Deleting a comment

- page_id: `304002313`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002313/2026x+Refresh1+Deleting+a+comment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:27.100+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Working with comments > (2026x Refresh1) Working with comments in a modeling tool
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can delete the comments that are no longer needed, but keep in mind that this action is irreversible.

To delete a comment

1. In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to delete.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Opening the comments panel' space=''].
3. In the comments panel, find the comment you want to delete.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the bottom of the comment and select Delete .

#### TIP: Useful to know

Useful to know

- Deleting a comment also deletes all of its replies.
- You can delete individual comment replies the same way you delete comments.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can delete the comments that are no longer needed, but keep in mind that this action is irreversible.</p><p><br /></p><p>To delete a comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to delete.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Opening the comments panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Cameo Collaborator comments panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>In the comments panel, find the comment you want to delete.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Deleting a comment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Deleting a comment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="541e0626-073b-4cd7-8106-6c818482f294"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful to know</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Deleting a comment also deletes all of its replies.</li><li>You can delete individual comment replies the same way you delete comments.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001892 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00090: (2026x Refresh1) Deploying Cassandra Multi-Data Center

- page_id: `304001892`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001892/2026x+Refresh1+Deploying+Cassandra+Multi-Data+Center
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-06-25T10:38:30.880+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Deployment Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Installation > (2026x Refresh1) Advanced installation
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud2022x Refresh2 and later versions support Apache Cassandra multiple data centers (Multi-DC) capability. Multi-DC Cassandra ensures continuous availability of data to Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudusers. There are two main scenarios for using multiple data centers: live backup/disaster recovery and geographical location. The deployment configuration of a live backup data center is presented here as an example.

Please note that Multi-DC deployment requires in-depth knowledge of Cassandra architecture and administration. Incorrect deployment or operation can result in total data loss. Technical support is not available, as this is considered a custom deployment strategy.

##### Scenarios for deploying a Multi-DC Cassandra cluster

###### Live backup/disaster recovery

Multiple data centers can be deployed at different physical locations as a disaster recovery solution. In this situation, one Cassandra data center would be the primary access point, while an off-site secondary data center would continuously receive the latest database updates. If the primary data center goes down, data can be restored from the secondary data center by bringing new Cassandra nodes online at the primary data center. Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudcan also be reconfigured to connect to the secondary data center as a temporary solution.

In this scenario, there is no degradation in performance. Read and write operations are performed on the primary data center first, then eventually synced to the second data center as a background operation.

###### ****Geographical location

For organizations with users spread across multiple geographical locations, it may be desirable to optimize data access by location. Each region can have a dedicated data center. All of the data centers would form one Cassandra cluster, and all of them would have access to the latest data.

In this scenario, performance would mainly be affected by the database write operations. Write operations would be applied to all data centers. Read operations would remain local and thus not affect performance.

##### Deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for the product

This is the recommended configuration procedure for deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud.

To configure Multi-DC Cassandra cluster

1. 
2. Start Cassandra cluster and verify all data centers are online.
3. 
4. 
5. For an existing database, .
6. Start Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudservices and verify operation.

##### Configuring Cassandra cluster with multiple data centers

Our example deployment is a single Cassandra cluster with two data centers located in separate geographical locations. Each Cassandra data center has three nodes. When performing the initial installation and configuration of Cassandra nodes, make sure the version of Cassandra is consistent across all nodes. Also, make sure to synchronize clocks across all nodes.

Start by updating the *cassandra.yaml* configuration file.

Set a unique cluster name for all nodes. This deployment is a single cluster spanning multiple data centers. The cluster name should be the same across all nodes.

```text

```

Set the seed provider for all data centers. Assign at least two seed nodes for each data center. Specify the address for all seeds. Alternatively, use a seed provider service to keep track of seed node addresses.

```text

```

Then, change the endpoint snitch implementation on each node. The default snitch is *SimpleSnitch*, which does not support Multi-DC. Cassandra uses snitch to gather network topology information. In this case, we are using *GossipingPropertyFileSnitch* for Multi-DC setup. Cassandra will obtain data center information from the *cassandra-rackdc.properties* file when *GossipingPropertyFileSnitch* is set.

```text

```

Refer to Cassandra documentation if you are updating snitch for an exiting database: [https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html](https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html)

Edit the *cassandra-rackdc.properties* file for each node. All nodes in a data center should have the same “dc” name.

For the primary data center:

```text

```

For the backup data center:

```text

```

Start each Cassandra node. Port 7000 is the default inter-node communication (encrypted and unencrypted) port for a Cassandra cluster. Make sure port 7000 is open for communication between data centers. Please note that the initial connection between data centers can take some time. Run *nodetool status* to confirm that all data centers have connected to the cluster.

For an existing Cassandra cluster, run *nodetool repair* and *nodetool cleanup* after the above configurations have been made.

###### Configuring the product for Multi-DC

Once all Cassandra nodes are online and data center connections are confirmed, configure the *application.conf* file for Multi-DC.

In the *esi.persistence.cassandra.keyspace* section, we have added the following parameters for our example. With 3 nodes in each data center, the replication factor is set to 3. The consistency level is set to “FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL" for a disaster recovery scenario. Please refer to the table below for consistency setting options. If deploying an entirely new database, the “twc” keyspace will be created with the replication strategy and factors specified here. For an existing database, you will have to manually edit the keyspace replication parameters (see Cassandra Keyspace Configuration below).

```text

```

- When *is-multi-dc* is set to *true*, the *replication-factor* parameter is ignored.
- The *dc-replication-factors* configuration will only be applied when creating a new “twc” keyspace. If there is an existing “twc” keyspace in Cassandra, Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudwill use the existing replication factor set in the database. Use *ALTER KEYSPACE* to change the replication factor.

| Keyspace Consistency Setting | Write Consistency Level | Read Consistency Level |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_GLOBAL | ALL | ONE |
| BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_LOCAL | ALL | LOCAL_ONE |
| BEST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL | EACH_QUORUM | EACH_QUORUM |
| FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL | EACH_QUORUM | LOCAL_QUORUM |
| BEST_RELIABILITY_LOCAL | LOCAL_QUORUM | LOCAL_QUORUM |

In the *esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic* section, set the data center name and contact points to local values. The local data center will be used as the primary database for client requests.

```text

```

Use the seed nodes from the local data center specified in *cassandra.yaml*

```text

```

###### Configuring Cassandra keyspace

If you are deploying Multi-DC with an existing Cassandra database, you will need to manually reconfigure the replication parameters for the “twc” keyspace.

Use *cqlsh*, the Cassandra command-line interface, to modify “twc” keyspace replication parameters.

```bash

```

Run *nodetool repair* after keyspace modification to update all nodes.

To verify replication in *cqlsh*:

```bash

```

These steps can also be used to create the “twc” keyspace. Note that manual keyspace creation is not needed, as Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudwill create the keyspace during initial startup.

```bash

```

##### Multi-DC Cassandra Best Practices

- The Cassandra version must be the same across all nodes and data centers
- At least two seed nodes should be assigned for each data center
- Clocks should be synchronized for all node

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Multi-DC Cassandra ensures continuous availability of data to <span>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud </span>users. There are two main scenarios for using multiple data centers: live backup/disaster recovery and geographical location. The deployment configuration of a live backup data center is presented here as an example. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Please note that Multi-DC deployment requires in-depth knowledge of Cassandra architecture and administration. Write operations would be applied to all data centers. Read operations would remain local and thus not affect performance.</span></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for the product</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">This is the recommended configuration procedure for deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for <span>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud</span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">To configure Multi-DC Cassandra cluster</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Configuring Cassandra cluster with multiple data centers"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Configure Cassandra cluster with more than one data center.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li>Start Cassandra cluster and verify all data centers are online.</li><li><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Configuring the product for Multi-DC"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Configure the product for Multi-DC.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Configuring authentication in Web Application Platform for Multi-DC"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Configure authentication in Web Application Platform for Multi-DC.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">For an existing database</span>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Configuring Cassandra keyspace"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update the “twc” keyspace for Multi-DC setup]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Start <span>Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud</span>services and verify operation.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Configuring Cassandra cluster with multiple data centers</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Our example deployment is a single Cassandra cluster with two data centers located in separate geographical locations. This deployment is a single cluster spanning multiple data centers. The cluster name should be the same across all nodes.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74e767c7-e04a-4920-9421-eb5ba65a2644"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cluster_name: 'MDC Cluster']]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Set the seed provider for all data centers. rack=rack1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">For the backup data center:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec0d8678-6728-4ff6-99b5-3e3aa70a6088"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[dc=datacenter2
