work in the user's home folder. If needed, adapt the commands to meet your needs.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To deploy the product for testing purposes</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="47672edf-128f-4bf2-a702-e11f38ee4a29">Install kubectl as described in <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/</a><span>.</span></li><li data-uuid="426b660c-f40e-4c3b-a3fb-37cd054c7910">Install the helm to the K8S cluster admin host as described in <a href="https://helm.sh/docs/intro/install">https://helm.sh/docs/intro/install</a>.</li><li data-uuid="29428206-5a9b-4850-b02e-abde44bb6cbf"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Use the following command (or another way) to copy cluster config files to the K8S cluster admin host:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b334167-5031-4340-b10a-966f47f62f21"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scp -r <user@control_plane_address>:.kube ~
````html
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Then open to edit <em>~/.kube/config</em> and r<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">eplace the localhost IP address with your control plane IP address or hostname</span>, e.g., <em><span class="nolink"><span class="nolink">https://127.0.0.1:6443</span></span></em>.</p></li><li data-uuid="b0f6b9d5-0f9c-4e16-ba9d-08c035e4dd4d"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Prepare the required files and directories</span>:</p><ol><li><span data-ref="05ccbf6e-4a3e-40e8-982e-03ce766ef31f">Download</span> and extract the <em><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="twc-services-charts-2026xRefresh1.zip" /></ac:link></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> file.</span></li><li>Download the installation package for your product version. For more information, refer to <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Downloading installation files</a>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Extract the packaged files, and inside the ~/<em>twc-services-charts</em> directory (where you extracted the twc-services-charts<em>.zip</em> file) do the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Extract the <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">twcloud</span>_&lt;version&gt;_<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">no_install_linux64.zip</span> </em>file. After extracting the files, you should have the following folder structure: ~/twc-services-charts<em>/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/</em>.</p></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">E</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">xtract the<span> </span><em>Web_Application_Platform_&lt;version&gt;_war_files.zip</em><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">file.</span><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e80c54b1-810f-4f07-82b9-76a5067f5743"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You will find all files mentioned in this chapter in <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">twcloud</span>_&lt;version&gt;_<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">no_install_linux64.zip</span></em> and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>Web_Application_Platform_&lt;version&gt;_war_files.zip, </em></span>or on the software download website (except for Teamwork Cloud keystore and SSL certificate):</p><ul><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf directory contains server.xml.</li><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory contains other configuration files.</li><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/data directory contains files for Cameo Collaborator and document-exporter.</li><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration directory contains Teamwork Cloud configuration files.</li><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/Utilities/Cassandra contains the .jar file for Cassandra's communication with Zookeeper.</li><li>The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps directory contains application .war files.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="42542e38-f80d-4e6b-8ff5-823e26199511">Go to the <em>~/twc-services-charts</em> directory and execute the following command to generate a private key and self-signed certificate. Change the command by entering your DN identifiers.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3775817c-b921-47a3-a3a9-0e4c1f23f769"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[openssl req -new -newkey rsa:2048 -days 365 -nodes -x509 -keyout tls.key -out tls.crt -subj "/C=LT/ST=State/L=City/O=Organization/OU=Department/CN=example.com"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="37d79759-aec2-4e7c-961e-985a89263fa3">Execute the following command to create a PKCS#12 keystore. If you change the name or password, do not forget to change them in the configuration files. For more information about the CA certificate and P12 or PFX files, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing SSL certificate" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing SSL certificate]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="402beacd-44ef-4ad2-85c7-8f16b5787703"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[openssl pkcs12 -export -name teamworkcloud -in tls.crt -inkey tls.key -out keystore.p12 -password pass:nomagic]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5a3d33e-6b1d-4ec9-ba61-c6ad8cdb22eb"><ac:rich-text-body>The <em>tls.crt</em> and <em>tls.key</em> files will be used for HAProxy Ingress Controller, so file names in HAProxy Ingress Controller <u>must be the same</u>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="fae5fc06-8552-4643-a201-1e5927bc42fe"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following commands to prepare the <em>helm-charts root</em> directory:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdcc84d9-c996-4072-baed-f0c2b9e63d55"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts
mkdir -p configs/{auth,cassandra,ssl,tomcat,twcloud,wap} configs/twcloud/ssl]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="a8513f28-4da6-4c80-8391-4909a8dedce5">Copy the generated certificate files to the appropriate locations by executing the following commands:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d7aa0b5-984e-4445-9699-a621f48cbe6e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts
cp tls.crt tls.key ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/ssl
cp keystore.p12 ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/ssl]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="e961d815-99d3-4f37-99d3-d3a05137444a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://cassandra.apache.org/_/download.html">Download the Apache Cassandra files</a></span> to the <em>twc-services-charts</em> directory. You can find the compatible Cassandra version for this release on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Third-Party Component Compatibility" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Third-Party Component Compatibility]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li><li data-uuid="011dc2e3-6fba-47e7-8820-c6b9e40c8974"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following commands to extract the Cassandra <em>.tar</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7796de9-c1cd-4999-ae3b-bbb65ac6673c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[tar xzf apache-cassandra-<version>-bin.tar.gz
cd ~/twc-services-charts/apache-cassandra-<version>/conf]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="9d825bfc-1d76-4eb3-9f1c-cdd676334e95"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the <em>cassandra.yaml</em> file edit Cassandra settings as shown below.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a43330b-ca86-49dc-82ba-36ed595013ff"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sed -i "s/# commitlog_total_space:.*/commitlog_total_space: 8192MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/commitlog_total_space:.*/commitlog_total_space: 8192MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/^rpc_address:.*/rpc_address: 0.0.0.0/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/commitlog_segment_size:.*/commitlog_segment_size: 192MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/read_request_timeout:.*/read_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/range_request_timeout:.*/range_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/^write_request_timeout:.*/write_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/cas_contention_timeout:.*/cas_contention_timeout: 1000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/truncate_request_timeout:.*/truncate_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/request_timeout:.*/request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/batch_size_warn_threshold:.*/batch_size_warn_threshold: 3000KiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/batch_size_fail_threshold:.*/batch_size_fail_threshold: 5000KiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s|compaction_throughput:.*|compaction_throughput: 512MiB/s|g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/memtable_heap_space:.*/memtable_heap_space: 4092MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/key_cache_size:.*/key_cache_size: 200MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/# file_cache_size: 512MiB/file_cache_size: 3072MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/internode_compression: dc/internode_compression: all/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/concurrent_reads: 32/concurrent_reads: 64/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/concurrent_writes: 32/concurrent_writes: 64/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/concurrent_counter_writes: 32/concurrent_counter_writes: 64/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s|# stream_throughput_outbound: 24MiB/s|stream_throughput_outbound: 4096MiB/s|g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/#concurrent_compactors: 1/concurrent_compactors: 1/g" ./cassandra.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="f3e800de-c24a-4609-a7e1-7494e235f63e"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to copy files to the <em>twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/cassandra</em> directory:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5994d30-2dc0-4610-8d7d-818e011e3b0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp cassandra.yaml logback.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/cassandra]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="e51762ad-d791-4589-a1d0-33aca62c524a">Execute the following command to copy the Authserver file to the appropriate location:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0245011-1932-4d09-98bf-787e8d4d4700"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/auth]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="452e0b7f-6397-486b-9cd3-d994dea75206">Execute the following commands to copy the Tomcat's file to the appropriate location:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="98ae8211-47f6-49dc-958e-16b3ccb2cbc7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf/catalina.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat
cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat
sed -i "s|../Certs/keystore.p12|./shared/conf/keystore.p12|g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat/server.xml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="d6559fb9-d99a-402e-a6e9-ec114e7ccc02">Edit the <em>application.conf</em> file as shown below.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76aeb30b-9279-4d45-bef2-96f2f1aab230"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/logback.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
sed -i 's/contact-points.*/contact-points = [${?CASSANDRA_SEED0}][${?CASSANDRA_SEED1}][${?CASSANDRA_SEED2}]/g' ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/local.size.*/local.size = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/remote.size.*/remote.size = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/replication-factor.*/replication-factor = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="08d34d37-3229-4e8a-93d2-2225b7577387">Execute the following commands to edit the <em>jvm.options</em> file:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="256c7c0a-e45c-48fc-821b-71682f373d00"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/jvm.options ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
sed -i 's/-Xmx.*/-Xmx${TWC_XMX}/g' ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/jvm.options]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="87ace3e1-5cda-4ff2-a05e-175375846d45">Execute the following commands to copy Web Application Platform files to the appropriate location:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f028a28-4081-40e7-8d19-4d51cb04560e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf
cp revision.txt log4j2*.properties webappplatform.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/wap/]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="e5cad756-a986-4aa7-971e-2e6fa1776809"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Ensure all files listed below exist in their directories:</span><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 61.2451%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 100.0%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>auth</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em style="text-align: left;">authserver.properties</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>cassandra</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>cassandra.yaml</em></li><li><em>logback.xml</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>ssl</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>tls.crt</em></li><li><em>tls.key</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>tomcat</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>catalina.properties</em></li><li><em>server.xml</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>twcloud</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>application.conf</em></li><li><em>logback.xml</em></li><li><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">jvm.options</span></em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>twcloud/ssl</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>keystore.p12</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>web</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>revision.txt</em></li><li><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">log4j2.properties</span></em></li><li><em><span>log4j2simulation.properties</span></em></li><li><em>webappplatform.properties</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table></li><li data-uuid="be2bd0a6-2c18-43c0-8867-8ba9310777d7"><p class="auto-cursor-target"> Execute the following command to create a directory for each application you are going to use:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0966ee6-a2d5-4092-a627-a525d225d026"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp -r  ~/twc-services-charts/dockerfiles ~/twc-services-charts/imagebuild
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="9d75067c-0294-4761-b1fd-2a00ae9f3c16">Copy all files listed below to their directories (except the <em>artemis</em> directory):<table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 61.6938%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 99.9787%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>admin-console</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>admin.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>authentication</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>authentication.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>collaborator</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>collaborator.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>data</em><span> </span>(The directory is located in<span> ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices</span><em>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/</em>. Remove the<span> </span><em>document-exporter</em><span> </span>directory from the<span> </span><em>data</em><span> </span>directory after copying.)</li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>document-exporter</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>document-exporter.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>data</em> (This directory is located in <em>&lt;webapp-charts_root&gt;/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/.</em><span> R</span>emove the <em>collaborator</em> directory from the <em>data</em> directory after copying.)</li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>magiclab</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em><span>magiclab.war</span></em></li><li><em><span>Dockerfile</span></em></li><li><span><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in <em>~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</em>.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>magiclabcollab</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em><span>magiclab-collaborator.war</span></em></li><li><em><span>Dockerfile</span></em></li><li><span><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in <em>~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</em>.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>oslc</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>oslc.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>reports</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>reports.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>resource-usage-map</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>resource-usage-map.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>resources</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>resources.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>sgi-crawler</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>sgi-crawler.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>simulation</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>simulation.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>sysmlv2-api</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>sysmlv2-api.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>webapp</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>webapp.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>twcloud</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>TeamworkCloud</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em>CATIANoMagicServices</em>.)</li></ul></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table></li><li data-uuid="935e90c7-2c9e-47ee-a759-933b045d057c"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to start the local Docker registry:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f266a15a-0b1d-41f3-80af-f740267beedd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --restart=always --name registry registry:2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5fb7ad51-fe04-4e3b-b785-f54effb9b75e"><ac:rich-text-body>In this case, a local Docker registry is run on the K8S cluster admin host. It uses port 5000, so make sure this port is open on your host firewall and accessible to your Kubernetes cluster.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e77d086f-90e9-4982-a467-c09761c2f549"><ac:rich-text-body>This local Docker registry should be used for testing purposes only and is not considered production-ready. In the production environment, run the image registry with TLS and authentication.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="6715d7e1-8c03-4eab-a1db-d7749e7b3682"><span>Add your repo URL (FQDN of the K8S cluster admin host) to the Docker </span><em>/etc/docker/daemon.json</em> file.</li><li data-uuid="7cd31f53-d46a-4a01-9824-0e0a792f7731"><p class="auto-cursor-target">If the <em>daemon.json</em><span> file does not exist, create it. Assuming there are no other settings in the file, it should have the following content:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7dc2674a-6227-49ca-bc4d-6fa51a349241"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
"insecure-registries": ["admin.example.com:5000"]
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="30443364-db40-4222-a470-66ebdbc2b6cf"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to restart the Docker service:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52848786-752a-4d84-9862-cfa28007cc0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo systemctl restart docker]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="7921af65-56e5-4671-8e91-2fc175c165b1"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the commands below for each service that you want to build images for inside their own directories, as shown in the examples in substeps a, b, and c.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b587e64-bffd-4103-8a02-5f6cc94e9d01"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker build -f Dockerfile -t {APP_NAME} .
docker tag {APP_NAME}:{VERSION} {IMAGE_REPO_URL}:5000/{APP_NAME}:{VERSION}
docker push {IMAGE_REPO_URL}/{APP_NAME}:{VERSION}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f0d92e3-39cb-42e8-8eda-3fdc849c7245"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>{IMAGE_REPO_URL} - your image repository URL. It can differ depending on the image registry provider.</li><li>{APP_NAME} - application name.</li><li>{VERSION} - version to be used for the tag.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following command example builds images for Admin Console:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c34d30a-7506-46c6-9f90-f9000947b158"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd imagebuild/admin-console
docker build -f Dockerfile -t admin .]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following command example tags the built image:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1fe9603-6d3f-4e34-b2a9-d1281cb1be35"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker tag admin:latest admin.example.com:5000/admin:latest]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following command example pushes the image to the registry:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66dec9b0-a53c-4c0b-9f7d-1a6980426eed"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker push admin.example.com:5000/admin:latest]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93a3a193-6652-4358-865c-b0a4155bfe2e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Check the names of the local repositories after Docker images are pushed.<br />curl -s <a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/_catalog</a><br />curl -s <a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">http://</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">admin.example.com</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">:5000/v2/&lt;repository_name&gt;/tags/list</a><br />Example:<br />curl -s <a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">http://</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">admin.example.com</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list</a><br />REGISTRY_URL=&quot;<a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2">http://</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">admin.example.com</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">:5000</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2">:5000/v2</a>&quot;; curl -s $REGISTRY_URL/_catalog | jq -r '.repositories[]' | xargs -I {} sh -c 'echo &quot;Repository: {}&quot;; curl -s '&quot;$REGISTRY_URL&quot;'/{}/tags/list | jq -r &quot;.tags[]&quot;'</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="c9baebfe-47bb-4d31-bacf-0dbd244a3ea5">To be able to pull images to your Kubernetes cluster nodes, add your image repository to the Containerd configuration on <strong>all cluster nodes</strong><span>:</span><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Edit the <em>/etc/containerd/config.toml</em> file by adding the following lines to the &quot;mirrors&quot; part:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce222a88-8173-4138-a552-f7b758ba4225"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.mirrors."admin.exmple.com:5000"]
              endpoint = ["http://admin.example.com:5000"]

      [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.configs." admin.example.com"]
        [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.configs." admin.example.com".tls]
         insecure_skip_verify = true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c63a4c74-d89c-4c9d-aac0-6498bdbdfcf9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Keep in mind that alignment is very important, as shown in the example below.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="containerd.PNG" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to restart Containerd services on all cluster nodes:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30f7224c-5052-4ee9-896d-213ce765affe"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo systemctl restart containerd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="da1892e6-d95e-48a4-b969-4f5baa00ddd9"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following commands to add dependency repos:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7b39c59-1b71-49fe-8e78-0655d76946c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm repo add kedacore https://kedacore.github.io/charts
helm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb
helm repo add haproxy-ingress https://haproxytech.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

cd ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="68cb6c82-0603-40a2-9e72-4fe70a309601"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to update chart dependencies:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ed3f41d-dabc-4c86-bdca-efac821fbe0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm dependency update
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="d0596296-1b84-466f-b32e-386e240d8462"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to install Custom Resource Definitions (CRD). This will create all services and deployments.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab86223e-8aa4-4a3e-a192-1172d374a5fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm install keda kedacore/keda --namespace keda --create-namespace --wait --version 2.19.0]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1248d479-52cf-44e6-b483-b46e996e0a85"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can check the status of the services and pods with this command: kubectl get all -n keda</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8087bbb5-468f-4888-870e-19da350c9cc1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Keda and MetalLB should be added as separate resources.</p><p>Since CRDs (being a globally shared resource) are fragile, you have to assume that once a CRD is installed, it is shared across multiple namespaces and groups of users. For that reason, installing, modifying, and deleting CRDs is a process that has ramifications for all users and systems of that cluster.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="c0ee728e-d545-4509-b718-6fe2450e83f8"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to deploy MetalLB (skip this step if MetalLB is already deployed and configured):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd0038ee-6ffe-435d-a203-f7fe63214a71"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --create-namespace]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="188851d1-0f79-4a47-817b-b149c3d0aff6"><p class="auto-cursor-target">To check the MetalLB deployment status, execute the following command on the control plane node or the K8S cluster admin host:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0a94001-25fe-4080-8187-33a0a6a32644"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl get -n metallb-system all]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">You should see an output similar to this:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="output_example.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="038c7120-7519-4732-b67a-6b9c18d2126f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you see that the MetalLB status is 'Running,' but READY is 0/1, give it some time to start and repeat the command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="84b2a6b8-e4df-4d3e-9e60-38cec187e745"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Create configuration files for MetalLB as instructed below. Make sure to complete all the substeps of this step to create and apply both configurations. Otherwise, the product will not work properly.</p><ol><li><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37369021-132b-4d31-9583-ed17e9327989"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Your IT department should give you a reserved IP address or a range of IP addresses (depending on your needs) with a DNS A record that points to this IP address.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">At least one IP address with a domain name should be reserved for Ingress.</span></li><li>Configuration files should be formatted in &quot;<em>yaml&quot;.</em></li><li>The first IP address configured in <em>metallb_ipaddresspool.yaml</em> will be assigned for Ingress.<p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Edit the <em>~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/</em></span><em>metalLB/metallb_ipaddresspool.yaml</em> file as shown below:</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1e65202-5d7c-40f3-b00a-53180ac65e38"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: IPAddressPool
metadata:
  name: first-pool
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  addresses:
  - 192.168.1.1/32
  #- 192.168.10.0/24
  #- 192.168.9.1-192.168.9.5
  #- fc00:f853:0ccd:e799::/124]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Edit (if necessary) the <span><em>~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/</em></span><em>metalLB/l2advertisement.yaml</em> file as shown below. You can change the metadata name as needed. Keep in mind that the ipAddressPool name should match the specifications.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71524bf8-eed4-4074-a4e0-8f79fd8c3ede"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: L2Advertisement
metadata:
  name: example
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  ipAddressPools:
  - first-pool]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to apply the MetalLB configuration:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abf06588-d304-4971-90b4-cf688c8d7bc2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl apply -f ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/metalLB/metallb_ipaddresspool.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to check the applied configuration:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8aa8955-bd2a-4fbd-81b4-0475ee7e6e89"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl describe ipaddresspools.metallb.io first-pool -n metallb-system]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to create the MetalLB advertisement:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83c1efea-26d2-49cc-8676-00f5981124ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl apply -f ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/metalLB/l2advertisement.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to check the advertisement configuration:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97bdb3e7-5488-4a6d-ad63-49d215cdfc32"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl describe l2advertisements.metallb.io example -n metallb-system]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="ddb90365-a7e8-413b-9dfe-55a340e03eb6">Install HAProxy CRD<ul><li><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1772cf7f-895a-4ef3-8634-d719ed1a5481"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/haproxytech/kubernetes-ingress/master/crs/definition/ingress.v3.haproxy.org_tcps.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="e452b524-ec9a-4857-8eff-44dd7f0edb81">Find the <em>values.yaml</em> file in the parent helm chart ( <em>~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/values.yaml </em>) and provide the values of the parameters in the file to enable or disable specific applications or parts of the configuration.<br />All parameters marked with the MANDATORY comment in the <em>values.yaml</em> file <strong>must be configured before deployment</strong>. Parameters without this marker are optional and may be modified as needed to customize the deployment.<br />When configuring parameters, follow the guidelines below:<br /><strong>Image repositories</strong> — Set the repository name for each application image to match exactly the name registered in your image registry. To verify the available repository names, query your registry catalog:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24421efe-390b-4000-b3b5-b5af6d4d8e6a"><ac:rich-text-body>curl -s <a href="http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/_catalog">http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/_catalog</a><br />Use the names returned by this command as the repository values in <em>values.yaml</em>. Names are case-sensitive and must match exactly.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><strong>Image tags</strong> — Tag names are set to <code>latest</code> by default. If you used a different tag when pushing images to the registry (see Step 25), set the tag to match. To verify which tags are available for a given repository, run:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6587fdd5-cafa-4137-a553-58175358e1b7"><ac:rich-text-body>curl -s <a>http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/&lt;repository_name&gt;/tags/list</a></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc1a74ca-8629-41f0-91d0-84fab988cab7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example:</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[curl -s http://ingress.example.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><strong>Ingress host</strong> — If ingress is enabled in <em>values.yaml</em>, the <code>host</code> field must be set to the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) that resolves to your Ingress external IP address (the DNS A record configured earlier, e.g., <em>ingress.example.com</em>). Leaving this field empty or incorrectly set will prevent routing to the web applications.</li><li data-uuid="7a05ea67-6902-4685-a5b2-b4401f658409"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To deploy services to the default namespace, execute this command in the helm parent chart directory (<em>you can change &quot;twc&quot; to any release name</em>):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09ac308a-be18-47b1-a3cd-e22440970304"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd helm-charts
helm install twc .]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create a namespace and deploy services in this namespace, execute this command in the helm parent chart directory :<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e8390c7-bc7c-4a31-97f7-759f36729c32"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm install twc . --namespace=twc --create-namespace --wait]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de256884-47cb-4ca6-9294-ef6b56ea5e45"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This helm chart includes Zookeeper and HAProxy ingress as dependencies. They will be deployed automatically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="e76a2974-5539-43f0-82cf-a16596f9fa16"><p class="auto-cursor-target">After all web applications are deployed, execute the following command to check their status:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="443b763f-e98f-4938-8800-9ede02d74821"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl get all]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c6792b1-83f5-4892-bb7e-c37c75f84abf"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>All pods should be READY:1/1, STATUS:Running.</li><li>The HAProxy Ingress Controller service should be Type:LoadBalancer.</li><li>EXTERNAL-IP should match the address that you provided in the MetalLB configuration.</li><li>If pods do not run, check for problems by executing this command:</li></ul><pre>		kubectl describe pod pod_name</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="9df44465-39d2-4adb-b941-c265fabd880b"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to check Ingress rules:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="993736c2-6794-4221-b041-237deb3159d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl describe ingress]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>You should get an output similar to this:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07c5cbd4-0fa8-4b89-b05a-446de8bc859e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Name:             webapp-tcas-ingress
Labels:           app=tcas
                  app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm
                  app.kubernetes.io/version=2026xRefresh1
                  helm.sh/chart=tcas-0.1.5
                  project=tcas
Namespace:        wap
Address:          ***.***.***.***
Ingress Class:    haproxy
Default backend:  <default>
TLS:
  tls-secret terminates insgress.example.com
Rules:
  Host                       Path  Backends
  ----                       ----  --------
  nm-wap10639.dsone.3ds.com
                             /admin                   webapp-adminconsole:8443 (10.233.88.83:8443)
                             /authentication          webapp-authentication:8443 (10.233.88.84:8443)
                             /collaborator            webapp-collaborator:8443 (10.233.73.213:8443)
                             /document-exporter       webapp-docexporter:8443 (10.233.88.86:8443)
                             /reports                 webapp-reports:8443 (10.233.88.88:8443)
                             /resources               webapp-resources:8443 (10.233.73.217:8443)
                             /magiclab                webapp-magiclab:8443 (10.233.73.215:8443)
                             /magiclab-collaborator   webapp-magiclabcollab:8443 (10.233.105.15:8443,10.233.73.216:8443,10.233.88.89:8443)
                             /webapp                  webapp-webapp:8443 (10.233.73.218:8443)
Annotations:                 haproxy.org/cookie-persistence: rtns-cookie
                             haproxy.org/server-ssl: true
                             haproxy.org/timeout-tunnel: 3600s
                             meta.helm.sh/release-name: webapp
                             meta.helm.sh/release-namespace: wap
Events:                      <none>

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="d222cd6b-710e-456d-bff5-67d8c4026468"><span>Test the product deployment:</span><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In an internet browser, go to <em><a href="http://ingress.example.com">https://ingress.example.com/webapp</a></em> (the DNS A record that points to the Ingress external IP reserved earlier).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e86a164e-e4a0-4dd2-aee3-dde0200f624a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you added a DNS record to bind the domain name to the IP address, you can use the domain name instead of the IP. The browser should show a warning because of the self-signed certificate. Accept it to proceed, and you will be redirected to the Teamwork Cloud Authentication web page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="web_app_platform_welcome_screen.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>Log in with your credentials, and you will be redirected back to Web Application Platform.</li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002317 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00094: (2026x Refresh1) Disabling authentication with username and password

- page_id: `304002317`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002317/2026x+Refresh1+Disabling+authentication+with+username+and+password
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:27.558+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Deployment Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Configuration > (2026x Refresh1) Authentication server
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can disable authentication with a username and password as described below.

This feature affects only the logins using the Authentication server, such as logging into Teamwork Cloud from web applications and from a modeling tool using SSO.

To disable authentication with a username and password

1. Go to the <install_root> \WebAppPlatform\shared\conf directory and open the authserver.properties file.
2. In the file, find the authentication.disable.credentials property and change its value to true .
3. Restart the webapp service.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can disable authentication with a username and password as described below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b37c49da-7404-4f6c-94e9-cdf9b95a6809"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature affects only the logins using the Authentication server, such as logging into Teamwork Cloud from web applications and from a modeling tool using SSO.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To disable authentication with a username and password</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;</em><em>\<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">WebAppPlatform\shared\conf</span></em> directory and open the <strong>authserver.properties</strong> file.</li><li>In the file, find the <strong>authentication.disable.credentials</strong> property and change its value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Restart the webapp service.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002074 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00095: (2026x Refresh1) Disabling or removing a LDAP server

- page_id: `304002074`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002074/2026x+Refresh1+Disabling+or+removing+a+LDAP+server
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:17.400+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Settings application > (2026x Refresh1) Managing LDAP servers
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can disable a connection to LDAP server temporarily or remove it from Teamwork Cloud system. External users from the disabled LDAP server can request for authentication from other connected LDAP servers. If the result is valid, they will be able to log into Teamwork Cloud Admin. Those who are in the system when you disable the connection to the LDAP server can still access and perform all operations as normal, however as soon as they log out from the Teamwork Cloud Admin, their status will change to **Disable**. You still can import users from the LDAP server even though it has been disabled.

To disable an LDAP directory

1. On the **LDAP management** page, select a LDAP server you want to disable, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
2. From the list choose to Disable . A confirmation dialog will appear.
3. Click Disable .

An LDAP directory icon that is disabled turn grey in the **LDAP management** page. You can enable this server by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and selecting the **Enable.**

To remove an LDAP directory

1. On the LDAP management page, select an LDAP directory you want to remove, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] .
2. Click Remove . A confirmation dialog will appear.
3. Click Remove .

If user removes LDAP configuration, he will not be able to synchronize imported users and groups associated with removed LDAP.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can disable a connection to LDAP server temporarily or remove it from Teamwork Cloud system. External users from the disabled LDAP server can request for authentication from other connected LDAP servers. If the result is valid, they will be able to log into Teamwork Cloud Admin. Those who are in the system when you disable the connection to the LDAP server can still access and perform all operations as normal, however as soon as they log out from the Teamwork Cloud Admin, their status will change to <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Disable</strong>. You still can import users from the LDAP server even though it has been disabled. </p><p><br /></p><p>To disable an LDAP directory</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the <strong>LDAP management</strong> page, select a LDAP server you want to disable, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Disabling or removing a LDAP server" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li>From the list choose to <strong>Disable</strong>. A confirmation dialog will appear.</li><li>Click <strong>Disable</strong>. </li></ol><p>An LDAP directory icon that is disabled turn grey in the  <strong>LDAP management</strong> page. You can enable this server by clicking <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using the app bar" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and selecting the <strong>Enable.</strong> </p><p><br /></p><p>To remove an LDAP directory </p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>LDAP management</strong> page, select an LDAP directory you want to remove, click<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Disabling or removing a LDAP server" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <strong>Remove</strong>. A confirmation dialog will appear.</li><li>Click <strong>Remove</strong>. </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e930de2f-871d-4195-b5c4-d79db982faf4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If user removes LDAP configuration, he will not be able to synchronize imported users and groups associated with removed LDAP.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002042 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00097: (2026x Refresh1) Document templates

- page_id: `304002042`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002042/2026x+Refresh1+Document+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:16.488+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Publishing documents
- labels: ['using-predefined-templates', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When configuring settings for publishing a model to Cameo Collaborator, you must choose a template to determine the appearance and content of a published model. There are two types of templates you can use:

- **Predefined templates**, which are stored the*modelLibraries*folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog. Be aware that the list of available templates depends on your modeling tool, e. g., MagicDraw (with or without plugins), Cameo Systems Modeler, etc.
- **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with custom document templates' space='']**, which are .mdzip files created or modified by a user and stored in the user defined location. These templates appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog, if they are stored or used in the project you are going to publish. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating a custom template' space='']

The following table briefly describes all predefined templates and lists No Magic's modeling tools where each of them is available.

| Template name | Short description | Available in... |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BPMN | Apply this template to publish a model as the process portal, which provides the following views of the model:ProcessesData ObjectsOrganization StructuresInformation Systems | MagicDraw + Cameo Business Modeler PluginCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Entire Model | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal, which provides a single view of the entire model. | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Diagrams | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal providing the diagrams view of the model. | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Requirements Document | Apply this template to publish a project as a single-view Requirements Report portal. | MagicDraw + Cameo Requirements Modeler PluginMagicDraw + SysML v1 PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Requirements | Apply this template to publish a project as the Requirements portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureTest CasesTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams | MagicDraw + Cameo Requirements Modeler PluginMagicDraw + SysML v1 PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Software Engineering | Apply this template to publish a project as the Software Engineering portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureImplementationTest CasesArchitecture TraceabilityTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| SysML | Apply this template to publish a project as the SysML v1 portal, which provides the following views of the model:RequirementsStructureInterfacesConstraintsBehaviorDiagrams | MagicDraw + SysML v1 PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| UAF | Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:50%Security ViewResources View - ResourcesSystems View - ResourcesPhysical Resources View - ResourcesResources View - Resource ExchangesSystems View - Resource ExchangesPhysical Resource View - Resource ExchangesResources View - FunctionsSystems View - FunctionsPhysical Resources View - FunctionsService Oriented View - Service InterfacesServices View - Service InterfacesService Oriented View - Service SpecificationsServices View - Service SpecificationsService Oriented View - Service FunctionsServices View - Service FunctionsProgramme View - ProjectsProject View - ProjectsAcquisition View - Projects50%Personnel View - Organisational RelationshipsOperational View - Organizational RelationshipsLogical View - Organisational RelationshipsLogical View - Operational ExchangesOperational View - Operational ExchangesLogical View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational PerformersLogical View - Operational PerformersStrategic View - Enterprise PhasesCapability View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - CapabilitiesCapability View - CapabilitiesStrategic View - CapabilitiesDiagramsData and InformationDictionaryTechnical Standards | MagicDraw + UAF Plugin |
| UPDM | Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:50%Capability View - Enterprise PhasesCapability View - CapabilitiesOperational View - NodesOperational View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational ExchangesOperational View - Organizational RelationshipsSystems View - ResourcesSystems View - FunctionsSystems View - Resource InteractionsServices View - Service Accesses50%Services View - Resource InteractionsServices View - Service InterfacesServices View - InterfacesServices View - Service FunctionsProject View - ProjectsStandardsDictionaryDataDiagrams | MagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Enterprise Architecture |

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Publishing documents' space='']

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>When configuring settings for publishing a model to Cameo Collaborator, you must choose a template to determine the appearance and content of a published model. There are two types of templates you can use:</p><ul><li><p><strong>Predefined templates</strong>, which are stored <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">the </span><em>modelLibraries</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and</span> appear on the list of the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog. Be aware that the list of available templates depends on your modeling tool, e. g., MagicDraw (with or without plugins), Cameo Systems Modeler, etc.</p></li><li><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with custom document templates" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Custom templates]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>, which are .mdzip files created or modified by a user and stored in the user defined location. These templates appear on the list of the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog, if they are stored or used in the project you are going to publish. <ac:link><ri:page Requirements Modeler Plugin</li><li>MagicDraw + SysML v1 Plugin</li><li>MagicDraw + UPDM 2 Plugin</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture</li></ul></td></tr><tr <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003118 space=MCS version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00098: (2026x Refresh1) Document view modes

- page_id: `304003118`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003118/2026x+Refresh1+Document+view+modes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:33.537+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can view a Cameo Collaborator document in several different view modes:

- Model view : In this view mode, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Content pane' space=''] of a document portal shows only the content of the item selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Navigation pane' space=''] . In addition, t he Model view shows model information and is the default mode of a Cameo Collaborator document.
- Document view : This mode hides model details and allows you to browse a Cameo Collaborator document continuously. You can scroll through the entire content of the selected model view, or select an item in the navigation pane to load the related content.
- **Wiki view**: Similar to the Document view, The Wiki viewhides model details while displaying the content of just one section, like the Model view.

To change the view mode of a Cameo Collaborator document

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the model view whose view mode you want to change.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. In the open menu, click **Active view** and select the desired view mode. 
 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

After changing the document view mode, the selected model view is reloaded and displayed in a new mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In addition, t<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">he </span>Model view<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> shows model information and is the default mode of a Cameo Collaborator document.</span></li><li><strong>Document view</strong>: This mode hides model details and allows you to browse a Cameo Collaborator document continuously. You <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">can scroll through the entire content of the selected model view, or select an item in the navigation pane to load the related content.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Wiki view</strong>: <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">Similar to the Document view, The Wiki view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>hides model details while displaying the content of just one section, like the Model view.</span></span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the view mode of a Cameo Collaborator document</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_view_list.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document view modes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>  and select the model view whose view mode you want to change.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">On the right side of the app bar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document view modes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the open menu, click </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Active view</strong> and select the desired view mode.<br /><br /> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_document_view_mode.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document view modes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br />After changing the document view mode, the selected model view is reloaded and displayed in a new mode.</span></p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page lists the scripts needed to install and configure the products.

Before using any of the scripts below, make sure to review the linked documentation.

| File | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and components installation script package. |
|  | Scripts to back up and restore the Cassandra database. |
|  | The script package with utilities for gathering and managing log files on a Teamwork Cloud server. |
|  | Scripts to upgrade Apache Tomcat and JDK when hardening Web Application Platform. |
|  | Scripts to install Monitoring Stack. |
|  | The Admin Tools package which is a set of Linux utility scripts to facilitate server-side tasks. |
|  | Script to configure port access for the firewall when hardening Cassandra. |
|  | Sample scripts to deploy nGinx and HAProxy proxies for Teamwork Cloud. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002538 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00100: (2026x Refresh1) Downloading and uploading PDF/HTML templates

- page_id: `304002538`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002538/2026x+Refresh1+Downloading+and+uploading+PDF+HTML+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:45.694+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Sharing and exporting > (2026x Refresh1) Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

PDF/HTML templates for exported documents are document-specific. You can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document, make any desired changes, and then upload the modified template back to the document. In addition, a single document can hold multiple PDF/HTML templates, enabling you to generate different outputs from the same document.

To download the default PDF/HTML template

1. Open the document whose PDF/HTML template you want to download.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select About .
3. On the left side of the open screen, select PDF/HTML templates .
4. In the Default template card, click the Download button. [ATTACHMENT filename='downloading_default_pdf_html_template.png']

The default PDF/HTML template for the open document is downloaded to your machine and you can customize it to meet your specific needs.

To upload a custom PDF/HTML template

1. Open the document to which you want to upload a custom PDF/HTML template.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select About .
3. On the left side of the open screen, select PDF/HTML templates .
4. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_icon.png'] .
5. In the Template title box of the open dialog, type the template name.
6. Drag the template file to the dialog or click the file icon to find the file on your machine. [ATTACHMENT filename='uploading_pdf_html_template.png']
7. When the template file is uploaded, click the Save button.

The uploaded template is displayed in the **PDF/HTML templates** screen.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">PDF/HTML templates for exported documents are document-specific. You can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document, make any desired changes, and then upload the modified template back to the document. In addition, a single document can hold multiple PDF/HTML templates, enabling you to generate different outputs from the same document.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To select <strong>About</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the open screen, select <strong>PDF/HTML templates</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Default template</strong> card, click the <strong>Download</strong> button.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="downloading_default_pdf_html_template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Downloading and uploading PDF/HTML templates" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br />The default PDF/HTML template for the open document is downloaded to your machine and you can customize it to meet your specific needs.</p><p><br /></p><p>To upload a custom PDF/HTML template</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the document to which you want to upload a custom PDF/HTML template.</li><li>On the right side of the app bar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Downloading and uploading PDF/HTML templates" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>About</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the open screen, select <strong>PDF/HTML templates</strong>.</li><li>In the bottom right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Downloading and uploading PDF/HTML templates" templates" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>When the template file is uploaded, click the <strong>Save</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />The uploaded template is displayed in the <strong>PDF/HTML templates</strong> screen.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001424 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00101: (2026x Refresh1) Downloading permissions report

- page_id: `304001424`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001424/2026x+Refresh1+Downloading+permissions+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:22.826+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Users application
- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can download a user's permission report and view the permissions they were assigned in Excel format.

To download the permissions report, you must have the following permissions: **List All Resources**,******Manage Security Roles**, and**Manage User Permissions**.

To download the permissions report

Do one of the following:

- In the Users application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the user and select Generate permissions report. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- In the My account application, c lick [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom left corner to download the report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can download a user's permission report and view the permissions they were assigned in Excel format.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89f16d31-5ebf-41e2-8c09-1227705c5513"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To download the permissions report, you must have the following permissions: <strong><span>List All Resources</span></strong><span>,</span><strong><span> </span></strong><span><strong>Manage Security Roles</strong>, and </span><span><strong>Manage User Permissions</strong>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To download the permissions report</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2a8b23f9-20f1-4185-a36e-e59c72590ead">In the <strong>Users </strong>application, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Downloading permissions report" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the user and select <strong>Generate permissions report.<br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generate_permissions_report.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Downloading permissions report" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></li><li data-uuid="7e5f1551-b783-43e2-a7cc-e092087b6cbb">In the <strong>My account </strong>application, c<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Download icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Downloading permissions report" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">at the bottom left corner to download the report.<br /></span></li></ul><p><br /><ac:image ac:width="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="my_account_app_download.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Downloading permissions report" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````
