<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002168 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00102: (2026x Refresh1) Editing a comment

- page_id: `304002168`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002168/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+a+comment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:21.229+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Working with comments > (2026x Refresh1) Working with comments in a modeling tool
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can edit comments in the Cameo Collaborator comments panel as described below.

To edit a comment

1. In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to edit.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Opening the comments panel' space=''].
3. In the comments panel, find the comment you want to edit.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom of the comment and select **Edit**. The **Edit comment** pane opens.
6. In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.
7. If needed, click the High priority switch on the bottom of the Edit comment pane to change the comment priority.
8. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.

##### Discarding comments

If you do not want to save comment changes after starting to edit the comment, you can cancel the editing process.

To discard a comment

- After starting to edit a comment, click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_comment.png'] on the top left corner of the Edit comment pane to cancel the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can edit comments in the Cameo Collaborator comments panel as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To edit a comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to edit.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Opening the comments panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Cameo Collaborator comments panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>In the comments panel, find the comment you want to edit.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Editing a comment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Editing a comment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Edit</strong>. The <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane opens.</p></li><li>In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.</li><li>If needed, click the <strong>High priority</strong> switch on the bottom of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to change the comment priority.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Editing a comment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.</li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Discarding comments</h3><p>If you do not want to save comment changes after starting to edit the comment, you can cancel the editing process.</p><p><br /></p><p>To discard a comment</p><hr /><ul><li>After starting to edit a comment, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_comment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Editing a comment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the top left corner of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to cancel the changes.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002055 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00103: (2026x Refresh1) Editing an LDAP server configuration properties

- page_id: `304002055`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002055/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+an+LDAP+server+configuration+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:16.785+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Settings application > (2026x Refresh1) Managing LDAP servers
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the configuration properties of an LDAP server connected to the Teamwork Cloud system. The system will update the configuration properties with the new values automatically.

To edit the configuration properties of an LDAP server do one of the following

- On the **LDAP management** page, select card with LDAP server which you want to edit****and click **Edit**button.
  - Change wanted configuration parameters.
  - Click Save to save changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can change the configuration properties of an LDAP server connected to the Teamwork Cloud system. The system will update the configuration properties with the new values automatically.</p><p><br /></p><p>To edit the configuration properties of an LDAP server do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>On the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">LDAP management</strong> page, select card with LDAP server which you want to edit<strong> </strong>and click <strong>Edit </strong>button. </p><ul><li>Change wanted configuration parameters.</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong> to save changes. </li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001377 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00104: (2026x Refresh1) Editing and contextualizing

- page_id: `304001377`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001377/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+and+contextualizing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:19.667+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can edit certain model elements right in a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document. This is very beneficial for the whole product team, because engineers and even stakeholders can make changes to a model without having to use a modeling tool. In addition, the model editing capability speeds up the collaboration on a model. It allows you to instantly correct property values in a Cameo Collaborator document and skip the commenting process.

The sections below explain several main workflows where the model editing feature can be used.

#### NOTE: Documents published from synchronized projects

Documents published from synchronized projects

If a Cameo Collaborator document is published from a synchronized project on the target Teamwork Cloud server, the commenting and model editing capabilities in the document will be disabled so that the synchronized project on the target server cannot be modified. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Cross-cluster resource synchronization' space=''].

##### Model editing to provide feedback

If you need to review a Cameo Collaborator document and provide feedback on the model, follow the workflow below.

To provide feedback on a model

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document you want to review.
2. Review the document, including its structure, element characteristics, and diagrams.
3. Do one or several of the following actions:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with comments in Cameo Collaborator' space=''] for items that need to be addressed.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing element values' space=''] if you know what they should be.
4. Inform the author that you have finished reviewing the document so that he/she could react to your feedback.

##### Model editing to react to feedback

When a reviewer provides feedback on a Cameo Collaborator document, address the comments right in the document without having to use a modeling tool.

To react to feedback

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document that has comments you need to address.
2. In the app bar click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Comments pane' space=''] and read the comments.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing element values' space=''] in the document to address the reported issues.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Replying to comments' space=''] to inform the reviewer that required changes have been made or ask for additional information.
5. Inform the reviewer that you have finished updating the model and ask for another round of review.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This is very beneficial for the whole product team, because engineers and even stakeholders can make changes to a model without having to use a modeling tool. In addition, the model editing capability speeds up the collaboration on a model. It allows you to instantly correct property values in a Cameo Collaborator document and skip the commenting process.</p><p>The sections below explain several main workflows where the model editing <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001775 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00105: (2026x Refresh1) Editing custom roles

- page_id: `304001775`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001775/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+custom+roles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:51.322+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Roles application > (2026x Refresh1) Managing roles
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating roles' space=''], you can modify it by changing its name, description, and permissions as described in the following sections.

#### NOTE: Editing roles

Editing roles

- You can edit only custom (user-created) roles.
- You must have the Security Manager role to edit custom roles.

##### Editing role details

In the Roles application, you can edit the name and description of a custom role as described below.

To edit role details

1. Do one of the following actions:
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Edit button in the role details card. [ATTACHMENT filename='editing_role_details.png']
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='actions.png'] next to a role name and select Edit role details .
2. When the Edit role details pane opens, change role name and or description and click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_role.png'] .

##### Changing role permissions

You can use the Roles application to add or remove [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Permissions' space='']of a custom role. Note that a role must have at least one permission.

To add or remove permissions of a role

1. Do one of the following actions:
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Change button in the role details card.
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='actions.png'] next to a role name and select Change permissions .
2. When the Change permissions pane opens, select the permissions you want to assign to the role (as shown below) and/or clear the check-boxes next to the permissions you want to remove. [ATTACHMENT filename='change_permissions_pane.png']
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_role.png'] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

its name, description, and permissions as described in the following sections.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003188 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00106: (2026x Refresh1) Editing element values

- page_id: `304003188`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003188/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+element+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:41.909+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Editing and contextualizing
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you need to provide feedback on a model or address reviewer's comments, edit certain element values right in the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

- In order to edit element values in a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Publishing documents' space=''] .
- Before starting editing element values, read about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model editing behavior and constraints' space=''] to understand how this feature works.

To edit element values in a Cameo Collaborator document

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Content pane' space=''].
3. On the content pane, select the item you want to edit. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
4. In the **Edit** pane (displayed below), change the item value and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example shows how you can edit requirement text.

Once you finish the steps described above, the modified item value is saved both in the Cameo Collaborator document and Teamwork Cloud project. See the figures below for more examples of editing different types of values.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Editing a Boolean type of value.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Editing an Enumeration type of value.

#### TIP: Deleting property values

Deleting property values

To delete the value of an editable property:

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Content pane' space=''].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to delete. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. When the **Edit** pane opens, click next to the value you want to delete.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

##### Reordering the values of multi-value properties

You can use the model editing feature to reorder the values of multi-value properties as described below.

To reorder the values of multi-value properties

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Content pane' space=''].
3. On the content pane, select the item you want to edit. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
4. When the **Edit** pane opens, drag a value to the desired position as displayed below. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

ri:filename="edit_value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Editing element values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>On the content pane, select the item you want to edit. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.</p></li><li class="confluence-link"><p>When the <strong>Edit</strong> pane opens, drag a value to the desired position as displayed below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reoredering_values.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Editing element values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li class="confluence-link"><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Editing element values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to save the changes.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002544 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00107: (2026x Refresh1) Element chooser service

- page_id: `304002544`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002544/2026x+Refresh1+Element+chooser+service

- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Element chooser service allows you to select model elements in a modeling tool using the web UI. You need to run this service to be able to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using server projects' space='CM']functionality.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the Element chooser in modeling tool.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002852 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00108: (2026x Refresh1) Enabling and disabling users

- page_id: `304002852`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002852/2026x+Refresh1+Enabling+and+disabling+users
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:18.928+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Users application > (2026x Refresh1) Managing users > (2026x Refresh1) Managing users details
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to disable login for user. To enable or disable user login you must have **User manager** role.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Easily distinguish enabled user from disabled.

To enable/disable user login, do one of the following

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the user and from menu select to Disable user/Enable user .
- Go to User pane and select to edit user details,
  - Change sliders position next to Enabled (slide left to disable, slide right to enable user`s login).
- Go to User pane and click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on Account card, Actions menu will open.
  - From menu select to Disable user/Enable user.

If you disable a user's access while he or she is online or logged in, Teamwork Cloud Admin will not log them out immediately. Changes to access permission will take effect immediately once the user logs out.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is possible to disable login for user. To enable or disable user login you must have <strong>User manager</strong> role. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="enabled_disabled_users.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling and disabling users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Easily distinguish enabled user from disabled.</h6><p>To enable/disable user login, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling and disabling users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </span> next to the user and from menu select to <strong>Disable user/Enable user</strong>.</li><li>Go to <strong>User</strong> pane and select to edit user details,<ul><li> Change sliders position next to <strong>Enabled </strong>(slide left to disable, slide right to enable user`s login). </li></ul></li><li>Go to <strong>User</strong> pane and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling and disabling users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on <strong>Account </strong>card, <strong>Actions</strong> menu will open. <br /><ul><li>From menu select to <strong>Disable user/Enable user. </strong></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f37d2c2-3674-43dd-9dd0-560cee5f9f94"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you disable a user's access while he or she is online or logged in, Teamwork Cloud Admin will not log them out immediately. Changes to access permission will take effect immediately once the user logs out.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001820 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00109: (2026x Refresh1) Enabling conditional user groups

- page_id: `304001820`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001820/2026x+Refresh1+Enabling+conditional+user+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:56.400+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Settings application > (2026x Refresh1) Managing server settings
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using conditional user groups allows you to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values. When users are added to a conditional user group, they receive the group permissions only if the group condition is met, for example, if they log in from the right location. For instructions on how to create conditional user groups, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Conditional user groups' space=''].

To allow users to create conditional user groups, first, you have to enable this feature in the Settings application as described below.

To enable conditional user groups

1. Go to the Settings application and select **Server settings** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the User management card, turn on the **Conditional user groups** option.

Once conditional user groups are enabled, you can set a user group as conditional and determine user access rights dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabling conditional user groups.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Using conditional user groups allows you to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values. When users are added to a conditional user group, they receive the group permissions only if the group condition is met, for example, if they log in from the right location. For instructions on how to create conditional user groups, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Conditional user groups" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To allow users to create conditional user groups, first, you have to enable this feature in the Settings application as described below.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To enable conditional user groups</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Go to the Settings application and select <strong>Server settings</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the User management card, turn on the <strong>Conditional user groups</strong> option.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Once conditional user groups are enabled, you can set a user group as conditional and determine <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">user access rights dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account.</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="enabling_conditional_user_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling conditional user groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Enabling conditional user groups.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001826 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00110: (2026x Refresh1) Enabling external user creation

- page_id: `304001826`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001826/2026x+Refresh1+Enabling+external+user+creation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:57.705+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Settings application > (2026x Refresh1) Managing server settings
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **User management**section of **Server settings**has the options to manage the external user creation process.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Managing external user creation options.

- If you enable the Create external user on successful authentication option, an external user will be created upon successful authentication. This means that external users will be able to log in to Teamwork Cloud Admin even if no user existed before. An external user will be created upon such login. If you disable this option, no external users will be created if the user tries to log in.

After an external user is created, it takes some time for it to show up inthe **User** application list. This delay is caused by the properties caching.

- Once you enable external user creation, you can also enable the Default user permissions template . With this option enabled, external users created upon successful authentication are added to the Default Template user group and given its permissions and assignments.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>User management </strong>section of <strong>Server settings </strong>has the options to manage the external user creation process.</p><p style="text-align: center;"> <ac:image ac:height="220"><ri:attachment ri:filename="user_management.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling external user creation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Managing external user creation options.</h6><ul><li>If you enable the<strong> Create external user on successful authentication </strong>option, an external user will be created upon successful authentication. This means that external users will be able to log in to Teamwork Cloud Admin even if no user existed before. An external user will be created upon such login.<br />If you disable this option, no external users will be created if the user tries to log in.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78a82261-7405-43f9-9dfb-b128f1cf1369"><ac:rich-text-body><p>After an external user is created, <span>it takes some time for it to show up in </span>the <strong>User</strong> application list. This delay is caused by the <span>properties</span> caching.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>Once you enable external user creation, you can also enable the <strong>Default user permissions template</strong>. With this option enabled, external users created upon successful authentication are added to the Default Template user group and given its permissions and assignments.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001866 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00111: (2026x Refresh1) Enabling secure connection between client and server

- page_id: `304001866`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001866/2026x+Refresh1+Enabling+secure+connection+between+client+and+server
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:53:53.090+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Admin web applications > (2026x Refresh1) Settings application > (2026x Refresh1) Managing server settings
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

By default, communication between the Teamwork Cloud server and client is unencrypted. This page provides information on how to encrypt communication using TLS over TCP.

##### Enabling TLS encryption on server side

To enable a secure connection to Teamwork Cloud, you must enable TLS (Transport Layer Security) on the Teamwork Cloud Admin's **Server Settings** page. This page also allows you to disable the option if you do not need to use a secure connection (see the following figure). You can always enable it whenever necessary. The most straightforward way to set up encryption is to use the keystore generated for Teamwork Cloud and/or Web Application Platform. Locate and download the server Java keystore (default: *<install_root>/Certs**/keystore.p12*). Make a note of the password used for the keystore. If there is a corresponding public certificate (e.g. *teamworkcloud.crt*) on the server, download it as well. If there is no public certificate, use the following command to extract the public certificate from the Java keystore:

```bash
-keystore  -file ]]>
```

If you need to generate a new keystore, see [Regenerating self-signed keystore](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/(2026x Refresh1) Managing+SSL+certificate#ManagingSSLcertificate-Regeneratingself-signedkeystore).

The unencrypted port (default: 3579) remains active even after encryption is enabled. To prevent unencrypted connections, block the port at the firewall level.

1. Go to the Settings app.
2. Move the slider to the right to enable the TLS protocol.
3. Enter a custom port for encrypted communication, or keep default value of 10002.
4. Upload the Java keystore file, and type the password.
5. Click Save .

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabling TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud.

##### Setting up client-side TLS

Server Certificates

The default port for a secure connection is 10002. If you are using another port for a secure connection instead of the default, append the port number to the server name in the **Login** dialog:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The unecrypted port (3579) remains active even after TLS is enabled. Teamwork Cloud will continue to accept communication through both encrypted and unencrypted ports. Use firewall to disable port 3579 and block unencrypted communication.

#### EXPAND: Generating a new key pair manually

Generating a new key pair manually

To enable a secure connection between the client and the server, you need to generate two keystore files, each in their own keystores:

- keystore.jks (contains key and certificate) – server-side – upload it using Teamwork Cloud Admin console.
- cert.jks (contains public certificate) – client-side – add it using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Server Certificates' space='MDTWRT'] option group in Environment Options.

1. Go to your Java directory and open the folder named bin .
2. Enter keytool -genkey -alias MyDomain -keyalg RSA -keystore keystore.jks -keysize 2048 and press Enter . You can give any name to*MyDomain* and **keystore.jks**.
3. Enter a password for your keystore and answer the questions asked. A certificate with a key is generated and placed into the keystore.
4. To extract a certificate without a key, type keytool -export -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore keystore.jks and press Enter .
5. To place a certificate into another keystore, e.g. cert.jks , type keytool -import -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore cert.jks and press Enter . You can give any name to **cert.jks**. However, by default, the tool searches for this exact name. Two keystore files are generated: one for the server ( keystore.jks ) and one for the client ( cert.jks ). You can now proceed to enable a secure connection between the client (modeling tool) and the server (Teamwork Cloud).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>By default, communication between the Teamwork Cloud server and client is unencrypted. This page provides information on how to encrypt communication using TLS over TCP.</p><h3>Enabling TLS encryption on server side</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="883be2ec-c8f0-48e4-a645-7e01e9463656">To enable a secure connection</ac:inline-comment-marker> to Teamwork Cloud, you must enable TLS (Transport Layer Security) on the Teamwork Cloud Admin's <strong>Server Settings</strong> page. This page also allows you to disable the option if you do not need to use a secure connection (see the following figure). You can always enable it whenever necessary. The most straightforward way to set up encryption is to use the keystore generated for Teamwork Cloud and/or Web Application Platform. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Locate and download the server Java keystore (default: <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/Certs</em></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>/keystore.p12</em>). Make a note of the password used for the keystore. If there is a corresponding public certificate (e.g. <em>teamworkcloud.crt</em>) on the server, download it as well. If there is no public certificate, use the following command to extract the public certificate from the Java <span>keystore</span>:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec4149b3-edfc-4638-9615-c21e2bb76fa4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[keytool -export -alias <teamworkcloud> -keystore <keystore.p12> -file <teamworkcloud.crt>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81d2bf01-d7ee-414c-b837-b008ca6ae249"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to generate a new keystore, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/(2026x Refresh1) Managing+SSL+certificate#ManagingSSLcertificate-Regeneratingself-signedkeystore">Regenerating self-signed keystore</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16cd75e5-08bb-4b6c-895d-c0558e82a18d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The unencrypted port (default: 3579) remains active even after encryption is enabled. To prevent unencrypted connections, block the port at the firewall level.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span>To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud Admin<hr /><ol><li>Go to the <strong>Settings</strong> app.</li><li>Move the slider to the right to enable the TLS protocol. </li><li>Enter a custom port for encrypted communication, or keep default value of 10002.</li><li>Upload the <strong>Java <span>keystore </span></strong>file, and type the password.</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><h3 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="WAP_Secure_Connection.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling secure connection between client and server" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h3><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Enabling TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">.</span></h6><h3>Setting up client-side TLS</h3>To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol on the client-side, use the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MDTWRT/Server+Certificates">Server Certificates</a> tool and provide the public certificate.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc7b6091-11cb-4b03-926c-04d2152afeeb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default port for a secure connection is 10002. If you are using another port for a secure connection instead of the default, append the port number to the server name in the <strong>Login</strong> dialog:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="login_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling secure connection between client and server" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed6bf311-e684-4c82-a57f-7b7151f64604"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The unecrypted port (3579) remains active even after TLS is enabled. Teamwork Cloud will continue to accept communication through both encrypted and unencrypted ports. Use firewall to disable port 3579 and block unencrypted communication.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f799e476-60ca-42f0-92a9-e767a686d4cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Generating a new key pair manually</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span>To enable a secure connection between the client and the server, you need to generate two keystore files, each in their own keystores:</span><ul><li><strong><span>keystore</span>.jks </strong>(contains key and certificate) – server-side – upload it using Teamwork Cloud Admin console.</li><li><strong>cert.jks</strong> (contains public certificate) – client-side – add it using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MDTWRT" ri:content-title="Server Certificates" /></ac:link> option group in Environment Options.</li></ul><br />To generate a key pair<hr /><ol><li>Go to your <strong>Java </strong>directory and open the folder named <strong>bin</strong>.</li><li>Enter<em> keytool -genkey -alias MyDomain -keyalg RSA -keystore <span>keystore</span>.jks -keysize 2048 </em>and press <strong>Enter</strong>. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cd4558d-c979-473d-bf41-edd5de405c45"><ac:rich-text-body>You can give any name to<em> MyDomain</em> and <strong>keystore.jks</strong>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Enter a password for your keystore and answer the questions asked. A certificate with a key is generated and placed into the keystore. </li><li>To extract a certificate without a key, type <em>keytool -export -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore <span>keystore</span>.jks</em> and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>To place a certificate into another keystore, e.g. <strong>cert.jks</strong>, type <em>keytool -import -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore cert.jks</em> and press <strong>Enter</strong>. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4db5da94-0333-4f2f-b369-8d4ec95b4c0d"><ac:rich-text-body>You can give any name to <strong>cert.jks</strong>. However, by default, the tool searches for this exact name.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>Two keystore files are generated: one for the server (<strong>keystore.jks</strong>) and one for the client (<strong>cert.jks</strong>). You can now proceed to enable a secure connection between the client (modeling tool) and the server (Teamwork Cloud). </li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003015 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00112: (2026x Refresh1) Enabling the Document View

- page_id: `304003015`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003015/2026x+Refresh1+Enabling+the+Document+View
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:24.121+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Publishing documents > (2026x Refresh1) Document templates > (2026x Refresh1) Working with custom document templates
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

TheDocument Viewis the mode of a published project that allows you to view it as a continuous document. Essentially, by using theDocument View,you can scroll through the entire content of a project view instead of jumping from one navigation tree element to another as in the Model View.TheDocument Viewcan be enabled for individual views of a project. After selecting a view,you are presented with the document containing information from that view. The sections of the document are numbered hierarchically both in the Navigation pane and the content pane.

The Document View can be enabled by customizing the template that will be used for publishing or from the title bar of a published project.

To enable the Document View in the template used for publishing

1. Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.
2. Double-click the element with the «view» stereotype. In the View Diagram of the template, each element with the «view» stereotype represents a separate navigation tree of a published project. If your template has more than one such element, you can enable the Document View for each of them separately.
3. In the open Specification window, set the **Document View** property as *true.* [ATTACHMENT filename='view_properties_specification_window.png'] The figure shows the Specification window of the element with the «view» stereotype with the Document View enabled.
4. Click the **Close** button.
5. Save the template file.

The Document View is enabled for the selected view of a published project.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

The **Document View** property can also be enabled in the Specification window of the element with the «Document» stereotype. Doing this overrides the property values set for individual elements with the «view» stereotype.

To enable the Document View from the title bar of a published project

1. In the app bar of a published model, click [ATTACHMENT filename='model_view_list.png'] and select the model view for which you want to enable the Document View mode.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Document View .

The Document View is enabled for the selected navigation tree of a published project.

When the mode is set from the title bar, it's only valid until the refresh of a web portal.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: black;">The </span><span>Document View</span><span style="color: black;"> </span><span style="color: black;">is the mode of a published project that allows you to view it as a continuous document.</span> <span style="color: black;">Essentially, by using the </span><span>Document View,</span><span style="color: black;"> you can scroll through the entire content of a project view instead of jumping from one navigation tree element to another as in the Model View.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The </span>Document View<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> can be enabled for individual views of a project. After selecting a view,</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> you are presented with the document containing information from that view. The sections of the document are numbered hierarchically both in the Navigation pane and the content pane.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Document View can be enabled by customizing the template that will be used for publishing or from the title bar of a published project.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the Document View in the template used for publishing</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.</li><li><p>Double-click the element with the «view» stereotype.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26f4b52b-1f5e-45e4-b3a0-c01e43945ffb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the View Diagram of the template, each element with the «view» stereotype represents a separate navigation tree of a published project. If your template has more than one such element, you can enable the Document View for each of them separately.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>In the open Specification window, set the <strong>Document View</strong> property as <em>true.<br /> </em></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="view_properties_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling the Document View" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The figure shows the Specification window of the element with the «view» stereotype with the Document View enabled.<br /><br /></li><li><p>Click the <strong>Close</strong> button.</p></li><li>Save the template file.</li></ol><p>The Document View is enabled for the selected view of a published project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1858f022-fb12-4b14-a1da-6c364d39ca0f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Document View</strong> property can also be enabled in the Specification window of the element with the «Document» stereotype. Doing this overrides the property values set for individual elements with the «view» stereotype.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To enable the Document View from the title bar of a published project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the app bar of a published model, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_view_list.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling the Document View" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the model view for which you want to enable the Document View mode.</li><li>On the right side of the app bar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling the Document View" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Document View</strong>.</li></ol><p><br />The Document View is enabled for the selected navigation tree of a published project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9403120-b3ac-4031-8451-d2d1e4e2b109"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When the mode is set from the title bar, it's only valid until the refresh of a web portal.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002992 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00113: (2026x Refresh1) Enabling the Single Tree View

- page_id: `304002992`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002992/2026x+Refresh1+Enabling+the+Single+Tree+View
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:23.068+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Publishing documents > (2026x Refresh1) Document templates > (2026x Refresh1) Working with custom document templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Single Tree View is the mode of a published model that allows you to render different model views as the nodes of a single navigation tree. This mode enables you to navigate a document without having to switch between different views.

To enable the Single Tree View

1. Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.
2. Double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype.
3. In the open Specification window, set the Single Tree View property as true .
4. Click the Close button.
5. Save the template file.

When you publish a model using this template, all the views of a published model are rendered in a single navigation tree. To access the content of the desired view, simply expand the node of that view in the Navigation pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Single Tree View is the mode of a published model that allows you to render different model views as the nodes of a single navigation tree. This mode enables you to navigate a document without having to switch between different views. </p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the Single Tree View</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.</li><li>Double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype.</li><li>In the open Specification window, set the <strong>Single Tree View</strong> property as <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Close</strong> button.</li><li>Save the template file.</li></ol><p><br />When you publish a model using this template, all the views of a published model are rendered in a single navigation tree. To access the content of the desired view, simply expand the node of that view in the Navigation pane.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002103 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00114: (2026x Refresh1) Encrypting property values

- page_id: `304002103`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002103/2026x+Refresh1+Encrypting+property+values
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-06-26T09:25:21.053+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Deployment Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Configuration > (2026x Refresh1) Hardening the deployment and its services
- labels: ['encrypt-properties', 'property-encryption', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Generating keys for encryption and decryption

By default, properties are stored as plain text. If required, any of these properties can be encrypted. For property encryption and decryption, you need to generate a pair of keys by using our property encryption tool. The tool is provided as the *encryptor.jar* file and the steps below explain how to use it.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

The encryption tool uses Java 21. Therefore, the system **PATH** variable needs to point to the location of the *bin*folder of the Java 21 home directory, for example, *C:\Java\Java11\bin* or */etc/alternatives/jre_21*.

#### NOTE: Note on special characters

Note on special characters

Encrypted properties (like passwords) can include special characters, such as exclamation marks, ampersands, or quotes. These characters must be escaped because the shell that runs the command interprets them within the property and does not invoke the encryptor.The characters that need to be escaped and the escape method depend on the command line shell used to run the command, socheck your shell documentation on how to escape the property value if it contains special characters.

The following examples use the property value **!&**:

- Invoking the command in the Linux bash shell: texttrue

- Invoking the command in the Windows Command Shell: texttrue

To encrypt properties

1. Click the link to download the [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor-2026x.jar'] [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor.jar'] file.
2. Open the command-line interface and navigate to the directory where the encryptor.jar file is located.
3. Run the java -jar encryptor.jar command. You should see the information on how to use the tool as displayed below. [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor_open.png']
4. Run the **java -jar encryptor.jar generate-keys <path_to_folder_for_keys>** command to generate a pair of keys for property encryption and decryption. Make sure to replace the**<path_to_folder_for_keys>**placeholder with the actual path to the directory where you want to store the keys.You should see a confirmation that the keys have been successfully generated. Keys for encryption and decryptionAfter executing the command, the following keys are created in the specified directory:**propertiesEncryptionKey.pub** - a public key used to encrypt property values.**propertiesEncryptionKey** - a private key used to decrypt property values.
5. To encrypt a property, run the java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt <unencrypted_property_value> <path_to_the public_key> command. Make sure to replace the <unencrypted_property_value> and <path_to_the public_key> placeholders with the actual property value and the path to the propertiesEncryptionKey.pub file. The tool will output the encrypted property value. [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor.png']

##### Configuring Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudencrypted property values

To configure Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudencrypted property values

1. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/ directory and edit the application.conf file.
2. In the *application.conf*file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: **ENC(encrypted_property_value)**. Example of an encrypted propertyFor example, an encrypted Cassandra password, should look similar to this one:**password=ENC("lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/ 
+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN 
TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==")**.
3. In the same *application.conf* file, add the **esi.config.decrypt_key_file******property and specify the path to the private key (the*propertiesEncryptionKey* file) as its value, e.g., **esi.config.decrypt_key_file=*<install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/**TeamworkCloud/keys/**propertiesEncryptionKey***. Private key locationThe private key (the *propertiesEncryptionKey* file) should be in a location that Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudcan access.
4. Restart Teamwork Cloud service.

##### Configuring Web Application Platform encrypted property values

To configure Web Application Platform encrypted values

1. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open the webappplatform.properties file.
2. In the *webappplatform.properties* file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: **ENC(encrypted_property_value)**. Example of an encrypted propertyFor example, an encrypted Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloudadministrator password should look similar to this one:**twc.admin.password=ENC(lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/ 
+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN 
TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==)**.
3. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf directory and open the catalina.properties file.
4. In the *catalina.properties* file, add the **properties.encryption.private.key.location** property and specify the path to the private key (the*propertiesEncryptionKey* file) as its value, e.g., **properties.encryption.private.key.location=*<install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebApp**Platform/conf/keys/propertiesEncryptionKey****.* Private key locationThe private key (the *propertiesEncryptionKey* file) should be in the location which Web Application Platform can access.
5. Restart Web Application Platform service.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304001435 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00115: (2026x Refresh1) Enterprise deployment

- page_id: `304001435`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304001435/2026x+Refresh1+Enterprise+deployment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:46:23.607+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Deployment Guide
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Web Application Platform as well as all of its plugins are provided as separate web applications. Therefore, it is possible to containerize applications and this way achieve better performance and scalability.

To learn how to containerize Web Application Platform applications using Docker, see the following topics:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The enterprise deployment option for Teamwork Cloud and its services.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Web Application Platform as well as all of its plugins are provided as separate web applications. Therefore, it is possible to containerize applications and this way achieve better performance and scalability.</p><p>To learn how to containerize Web Application Platform applications using Docker, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="WAP &amp; TWC enterprise deployment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enterprise deployment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The enterprise deployment option for Teamwork Cloud and its services.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003162 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00116: (2026x Refresh1) Exporting comments to PDF and HTML

- page_id: `304003162`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304003162/2026x+Refresh1+Exporting+comments+to+PDF+and+HTML
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:48:38.091+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Sharing and exporting
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows you to export comments to the PDF/HTML format. This way you can archive the discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes made in the model and thus trace the reasoning behind these changes. The exported files include not only all comments and replies created in the document but also the document and project information and comments statistics as shown below.

To export Cameo Collaborator comments to PDF or HTML

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator document containing the comments you want to export.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Export document to PDF or HTML .
3. In the open wizard, click the Type drop-down box and select Comments .
4. Click the Document scope drop-down box and select one of the following scope options:
  - Active section - exports the comments in the document's open section, which is selected in the Navigation pane.
  - Active section's presentation elements - selecting this option allows you to choose from which elements of the active section comments are exported. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_comment_scope.png']
  - Active view - exports the comments in the open view of the document which is selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) App bar' space=''] .
  - Whole document - exports all the comments in the document.
5. Click the Next button.
6. In the second step of the wizard, specify the following export properties:
  - Choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
  - To add a cover page, turn on the**Cover page**switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.
  - To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the**Table of contents**switch . Adding the table of contents works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.
  - To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the **Password protected** switch and enter the desired password in the **Password** box. Enabling password protection works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.
  - If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the **Template** drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document has no custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click the Finish button.
8. When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the Download button.

Note that exporting a large number of comments may take a while, so be patient while the file is being generated. You can check the file status in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing long-running tasks' space=''].

You can check the file status in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing long-running tasks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Task Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002490 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00117: (2026x Refresh1) Exporting documents to PDF and HTML

- page_id: `304002490`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002490/2026x+Refresh1+Exporting+documents+to+PDF+and+HTML
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-07-01T13:37:22.361+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Sharing and exporting
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator allows you to export an open document or a part of it to PDF and HTML formats. You can generate PDF documents of 100,000 pages or more.

#### TIP: Customizing PDF templates

Customizing PDF templates

PDF templates are customizable and can be modified according to your needs. You can find them in the *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/**shared/conf/data/collaborator/pdf* directory.

#### NOTE: Limitations

Limitations

By default, you can export up to 400 sections to PDF and up to 3000 sections to HTML.

To change the export to PDF/HTML limits:

1. Open the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties file.
2. Do one or both of the following:
  - Add the export.limit.size.pdf property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to PDF as its value (the default value is 400).
  - Add the export.limit.size.html property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to HTML as its value (the default value is 3000).
3. Restart Web Application Platform.

To export a Cameo Collaborator document to PDF/HTML

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator document you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on right side of the app bar and select an Export document to PDF or HTML .
3. In the open wizard, click the Type drop-down box and select Document .
4. Click the Document scope drop-down box and select one of the following scope options:
  - Active section - exports the open section of the document.
  - Active section's presentation elements - selecting this option allows you to choose which elements of the active section will be exported as displayed below. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_scope.png']
  - Active view - exports the open view of the document selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using the app bar' space=''] .
  - Whole document - exports the whole document.
5. Click the Next button.
6. Specify the following export properties:
  - Choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
  - To add a cover page, turn on the**Cover page**switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.
  - To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the**Table of contents**switch . Adding the table of contents works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - To add a page with the table of figures, turn on the **Table of figures**switch.
  - To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the **Password protected** switch and enter the desired password in the **Password** box. Enabling password protection works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - To add a page with the table of diagrams, turn on the **Table of diagrams**switch.
  - To add a page with the table of tables, turn on the **Table of tables**switch.
  - If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the **Template** drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document does not have any custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used. To use the Table of figures, Table of diagrams, or Table of tables option with a custom template, you need to update the custom template first.
  - Click the Media numberin **g** drop-down box and select one of the following options:
    - Generate automatically - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.
    - Use current numbering - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.
  - Click the Section numbering drop-down box and select one of the following options:
    - Automatic numbering - all sections in the generated document are numbered automatically.
    - None - sections in the generated document are not numbered.
  - Click the Break page at drop-down box and select the heading after which page breaks are added in the generated document. You cannot add page breaks when exporting documents to the HTML format. [ATTACHMENT filename='cc_document_export.png']
7. Click the Finish button.
8. When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the Download button.

Note that exporting a large document may take a while, so be patient while the file is being generated. You can check the file status in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing long-running tasks' space=''].

#### NOTE: Microsoft TrueType fonts

Microsoft TrueType fonts

If your Cameo Collaborator document uses fonts that are different from the default font used in documents, the exported PDF/HTML files may not support these fonts.

To avoid this, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Installing Microsoft TrueType fonts for Document Exporter' space=''].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002495 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00118: (2026x Refresh1) Exporting tables

- page_id: `304002495`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002495/2026x+Refresh1+Exporting+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:39.903+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Sharing and exporting > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting documents to PDF and HTML
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export Generic, Instance, and custom tables in a Cameo Collaborator document to PDF, HTML, or Excel.

##### Exporting tables to PDF or HTML

To export a table to PDF or HTML

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, navigate to the table you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the top right corner of the table and select Export to PDF/HTML .
3. An export wizard opens. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
In the open wizard, specify the following export properties:
  - Choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
  - To add a cover page, turn on the**Cover page**switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.
  - To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the**Table of contents**switch . Adding the table of contents works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the **Password protected** switch and enter the desired password in the **Password** box. Enabling password protection works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the **Template** drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document does not have any custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used.
  - Click the Media numberin **g** drop-down box and select one of the following options:
    - Generate automatically - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.
    - Use current numbering - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.
4. Click the Finish button.
5. When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the Download button.

##### Exporting tables to Excel/CSV/JSON

To export a table to Excel/CSV/JSON

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, navigate to the table you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the top right corner of the table and select Export to Excel/CSV/JSON.
3. An export wizard opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='cc_export.png']
4. With each of the options, select whether you want to Include HTML tags.
5. If you select CSV , also choose the CSV delimiter in the list.
6. Click Export and save the file.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.</span></p></li><li><p><span>To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the </span><strong>Table of contents</strong><span> switch .<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5497a82e-8ce2-4bc9-97b4-e5bde1cc0c4f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Adding the table of contents works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the <strong>Password protected</strong> switch and enter the desired password in the <strong>Password</strong> box. <br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa164650-1553-4a2b-a826-8e0edd916429"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Enabling password protection works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the <strong>Template</strong> drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document does not have any custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used.</span></li><li><span>Click the </span><strong>Media numberin</strong><span><strong>g</strong> drop-down box and select one of the following options:</span><ul><li><strong>Generate automatically</strong><span> - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.</span></li><li><strong>Use current numbering</strong><span> - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.</span></li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Download</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>button.</span></li></ol><h3>Exporting tables to Excel/CSV/JSON</h3><p><br /></p><p>To export a table to Excel/CSV/JSON</p><hr /><ol><li>In a Cameo Collaborator document, navigate to the table you want to export.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exporting tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the top right corner of the table and select <strong>Export to Excel/CSV/JSON.</strong></li><li data-uuid="13793ffc-44d0-4b74-b302-81d28d0439a3">An export wizard opens.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_export.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exporting tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="fbb080bf-9d40-4734-90fe-2b462736898a">With each of the options, select whether you want to <strong>Include HTML tags.</strong></li><li data-uuid="21d837f6-d49c-46bf-8078-557887ddaee2">If you select <strong>CSV</strong>, also choose the <strong>CSV delimiter </strong>in the list.</li><li data-uuid="24dad3f3-b69b-4081-8bc4-c0ee9fdfa721">Click <strong>Export </strong>and save the file.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304002501 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00120: (2026x Refresh1) File locations

- page_id: `304002501`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/304002501/2026x+Refresh1+File+locations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T10:47:40.508+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > (2026x Refresh1) FAQ and Troubleshooting
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The default locations for log and configuration files are listed on this page from a standard server installation.

To download installation files, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

###### Installation paths

| PACKAGE | OS | VERSION | INSTALLATION DIRECTORY |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Teamwork Cloud | Linux | 22xR2 and later | <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices |
| 22xR1 and older | <install_root>/TeamworkCloud |  |  |
| Windows | 22xR2 and later | <install_root>\CATIANoMagicServices |  |
| 22xR1 and older | <install_root>\TeamworkCloud |  |  |
| Magic Collaboration Studio | Linux | All | <install_root>/MagicCollaborationStudio |
| Windows | All | <install_root>\MagicCollaborationStudio |  |

CATIANoMagicServices

TeamworkCloud

MagicCollaborationStudio

<install_root>

###### Web Application Platform

Log files (e.g., *webapp.log*, *collaborator.log*, etc.) 
Linux:*<install_root>**/WebAppPlatform/logs/webappplatform*Windows:*<install_root>**\WebAppPlatform\logs\webappplatform*

Configuration 
Linux: *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties* 
Windows: *<install_root>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\webappplatform.properties*

###### Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud

Log file for 2024x and newer 
Linux*: <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/logs/server.log***Windows:*<install_root>\TeamworkCloud\logs\**server.log*

Configuration 
Linux:*<install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf* 
Windows:*<install_root>\TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf*

Log file for earlier versions 
Linux:*<user.home>/.twcloud/<version>/server.log* 
Windows:*C:\Users\<user>\.twcloud\<version>\server.log*

###### Authentication server

Log file for 2022x and newer 
Linux: *<install_root>**/WebAppPlatform/logs/webappplatform/authentication.log* 
Windows: *<install_root>\**WebAppPlatform\logs\webappplatform\authentication.log*

Configuration for 2022x and newer 
Linux: *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties* 
Windows: *<install_root>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties*

Log file for earlier versions 
Linux: *<user.home>/.authserver/<version number>**/authserver.log* 
Windows: *C:\Users\<user>\.authserver\<version>\authserver.log*

Configuration for earlier versions 
Linux: *<install_root>**/AuthServer/config/authserver.properties* 
Windows: *<install_root>\**AuthServer\config\authserver.properties*

###### Cassandra

Log file 
Linux: */var/log/cassandra/system.log*

Configuration filesLinux:*/etc/cassandra/conf*

###### Default keystore location

For 2026x and newer 
Linux: *<install_root>/Certs* 
Windows: *<install_root>\Certs*

For earlier versions 
Linux: *<install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration* 
Windows: *<install_root>\TeamworkCloud\configuration*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
